questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Bronze is an alloy of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Cu and Sn.

Here's why:

Bronze is a metal alloy primarily composed of copper (Cu) and tin (Sn). It's known for its durability, strength, and resistance to corrosion.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Option A: Cu and Zn - This describes brass, not bronze.
  • Option C: Zn and Mg - This is not a common metal alloy.
  • Option D: Fe and Cu - This combination is not typically used to make bronze.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which one of the following salts does not possess water of crystallization?
questions.solution:
Water of crystallization refers to the water molecules that are part of the crystalline structure of a salt. Many salts incorporate these water molecules into their solid structure, indicating that they possess water of crystallization. Let's look at each option:

  • Option A: Potassium permanganate

    • Potassium permanganate ($KMnO_4$) is a salt that does not incorporate water molecules into its crystal structure. It is generally found in its anhydrous form.

  • Option B: Blue vitriol

    • Blue vitriol is the common name for copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate ($CuSO_4 \cdot 5H_2O$). It clearly possesses water of crystallization, as indicated by the "pentahydrate" suffix, which means it contains five molecules of water of crystallization.

  • Option C: Washing soda

    • Washing soda is sodium carbonate decahydrate ($Na_2CO_3 \cdot 10H_2O$). The "decahydrate" suffix indicates that it has ten water molecules of crystallization.

  • Option D: Mohr’s salt

    • Mohr's salt is ammonium iron(II) sulfate hexahydrate ($(NH_4)_2Fe(SO_4)_2 \cdot 6H_2O$). The "hexahydrate" suffix indicates it contains six molecules of water of crystallization.

Based on the descriptions above, the correct option is:

Option A: Potassium permanganate, as it does not possess water of crystallization.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Bee sting leaves an acid which causes pain and irritation. The acid released is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: methanoic acid.

Here's why:

  • Methanoic acid (HCOOH), also known as formic acid, is the simplest carboxylic acid. It's the primary irritant in bee stings (and ant stings too!).

  • Tartaric acid is found in grapes and is used in baking.

  • Ethanoic acid is found in vinegar.

  • Citric acid is found in citrus fruits like lemons and oranges.

The pain and irritation you feel after a bee sting are due to the methanoic acid interacting with your body's cells, causing inflammation and a pain response.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Liquid vegetable oils are converted to solid margarine by the use of
questions.solution:
The process of converting liquid vegetable oils into solid margarine is known as hydrogenation. During hydrogenation, hydrogen gas is used to saturate the carbon atoms in the fatty acids of the vegetable oils. This addition of hydrogen atoms transforms the unsaturated fats (which are typically liquid at room temperature) into saturated fats (which are solid at room temperature). Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: hydrogen gas

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: The number of structural isomers of pentane is
questions.solution:
The correct answer is 3.

Isomers:

  • Chemical compounds that have identical chemical formulae but differ in properties and the arrangement of atoms in the molecule are called isomers.
    • Therefore, the compounds that exhibit isomerism are known as isomers.
  • Such structural isomers, which differ in the chain of carbon atoms, are known as chain isomers.

Pentane:

  • Pentane is an organic compound that is a clear, colourless liquid.
  • The odour of pentane is similar to petroleum.
  • It is less dense than water and is insoluble in water. It is also known as quintane and n-pentane.
  • The rotatable bond count of pentane is 2.
  • Molecular Formula: C5H12.

Isomers of Pentane: There are three structural isomers of pentane:

  • n-pentane is the first isomer, which is a straight chain with a normal structure of pentane.

  • 2-methylbutane is the second isomer, which is a branched-chain structure in which one carbon atom is connected to three other carbon atoms.

  • 2,2-dimethylpropane is the third isomer, which is a branched-chain structure with the central carbon atom connected to four other carbon atoms.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Vapours of sulphur escaping from a volcano often form a crust on the rocks. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
When vapors of sulfur escape from a volcano and form a crust on the rocks, the process involved is known as deposition. Deposition is the phase transition in which a substance goes directly from a gas to a solid without passing through the liquid phase. This is observed when sulfur vapors cool rapidly and solidify on the surfaces of rocks.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: deposition

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Dry ice is used on a performing stage to produce mist in air. The process involved is an example of
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option A: sublimation. Here's why:

Sublimation is the process where a substance transitions directly from a solid phase to a gaseous phase, bypassing the liquid phase.

Here's how it applies to dry ice:

  • Dry ice is frozen carbon dioxide (CO2).

  • At room temperature and standard atmospheric pressure, CO2 exists as a gas.

  • When dry ice is exposed to air, it absorbs heat energy from the surroundings.

  • This heat energy causes the CO2 molecules in the dry ice to vibrate faster and transition directly from a solid to a gas.

This gaseous CO2 is what creates the mist or fog effect on stage. The mist is not actually water vapor, but tiny droplets of liquid water that condense around the cold CO2 gas.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Evaporation: Involves a transition from liquid to gas (like water boiling). Dry ice skips the liquid phase.

  • Condensation: Is the opposite of evaporation (gas to liquid), like dew forming on grass.

  • Precipitation: Involves water falling from the atmosphere as rain, snow, sleet, or hail.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: C<sub>4</sub>H<sub>8</sub> belongs to the homologous series of
questions.solution:
To determine the homologous series to which the compound C4H8 belongs, we should first recognize that the general formula for alkanes, alkenes, alkynes, and cycloalkenes vary and can be used to classify the compound.

The general formulas are:

Alkanes: $$C_nH_{2n+2}$$

Alkenes: $$C_nH_{2n}$$

Alkynes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Cycloalkenes: $$C_nH_{2n-2}$$

Here, the compound C4H8 follows the formula for alkenes:

$$C_4H_8 \implies C_nH_{2n} \text{ where } n = 4$$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B: Alkenes

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The locus of the mid-point of the chord if contact of tangents drawn from points lying on the straight line $$4x - 5y = 20$$ to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is
questions.solution:
Let $$P\left( {t,{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)$$ be a point on the line $$4x - 5y = 20$$. Then the chord of contact of tangents drawn from P to the circle $${x^2} + {y^2} = 9$$ is

$$tx + \left( {{{4t - 20} \over 5}} \right)y = 9$$ ...... (i)

Let $$\theta$$ (h, k) be the mid-point of this chord of contact, then its equation is also $$hx + ky = {h^2} + {k^2}$$ ..... (ii) [using $$T = S'$$]

Clearly Eqs. (i) and (ii) represent the same line

$$\therefore$$ $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {t \over h} = {9 \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $${t \over h} = {{4t - 20} \over {5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow t = {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}}$$ and $$t = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{9h} \over {{h^2} + {k^2}}} = {{20h} \over {4h - 5k}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow h\{ 20({h^2} + {k^2}) - 36h + 45k\} = 0$$

$$x = 0$$ or $$[20({x^2} + {y^2}) - 36x + 45y] = 0$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Let $$A B C$$ be the triangle with $$A B=1, A C=3$$ and $$\angle B A C=\frac{\pi}{2}$$. If a circle of radius $$r>0$$ touches the sides $$A B, A C$$ and also touches internally the circumcircle of the triangle $$A B C$$, then the value of $$r$$ is __________ .
questions.solution:
Here ABC is a right angle triangle. BC is the Hypotenuse of the triangle.

We know, diameter of circumcircle of a right angle triangle is equal to the Hypotenuse of the triangle also midpoint of Hypotenuse is the center of circle.

$$\therefore$$ $$BC$$ = Diameter of the circle

Here $$B = (0,1)$$ and $$C(3,0)$$

$$\therefore$$ $$BC = \sqrt {{3^2} + {1^2}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {9 + 1} $$

$$ = \sqrt {10} $$

$$\therefore$$ Radius of circumcircle $$(R) = {{\sqrt {10} } \over 2}$$

$$\therefore$$ Center of circle $$(M) = \left( {{{3 + 0} \over 2},\,{{0 + 1} \over 2}} \right) = \left( {{3 \over 2},\,{1 \over 2}} \right)$$

Center of circle which touches line AB and AC $$ = (r,r)$$

Now distance between center of two circles,

$$ME = R - r = {{\sqrt {10} } \over 2} - r$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\left( {r - {3 \over 2}} \right)^2} + {\left( {r - {1 \over 2}} \right)^2} = {\left( {{{\sqrt {10} } \over 2} - r} \right)^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {r^2} - 3r + {9 \over 4} + {r^2} - r + {1 \over 4} = {{10} \over 4} + {r^2} - \sqrt {10} r$$

$$ \Rightarrow {r^2} - 4r + \sqrt {10} r = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow r(r - 4 + \sqrt {10} ) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow r = 0$$ or $$r = r - \sqrt {10} $$

$$\therefore$$ $$r = 4 - \sqrt {10} $$ [as $$r \ne 0$$]

$$ = 0.837$$

$$ \simeq 0.84$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Let $G$ be a circle of radius $R>0$. Let $G_{1}, G_{2}, \ldots, G_{n}$ be $n$ circles of equal radius $r>0$. Suppose each of the $n$ circles $G_{1}, G_{2}, \ldots, G_{n}$ touches the circle $G$ externally. Also, for $i=1,2, \ldots, n-1$, the circle $G_{i}$ touches $G_{i+1}$ externally, and $G_{n}$ touches $G_{1}$ externally. Then, which of the following statements is/are TRUE?
questions.solution:
Here if we add center of circles G1, G2, G3 ....... Gn, then we get a polygon of n sides.

From figure you can see one side of polygon makes angle $$\theta$$ with the center.

$$\therefore$$ n sides make angle = n$$\theta$$

We know, $$n\theta = 2\pi $$

$$ \Rightarrow \theta = {{2\pi } \over n}$$

Here triangle OMN is an isosceles triangle. Line joining of point O and midpoint O of MN (point A) is perpendicular to line MN and perpendicular bisector of angle $$\theta$$.

$$\therefore$$ $$\angle MOA = {\theta \over 2} = {\pi \over n}$$

From right angle triangle OMA,

$$\sin {\pi \over n} = {r \over {R + r}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R + r = r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over n}$$

Option A:

When $$n = 4$$,

$$R + r = r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 4}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R + r = \sqrt 2 r$$

$$ \Rightarrow R = \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right)r$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (A) is wrong.

Option B :

When $$n = 5$$,

$$R + r = r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 5} = r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 36^\circ $$

We know, in 0 to $${\pi \over 2}$$, $${\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} \theta $$ is decreasing.

$$\therefore$$ $${\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 45^\circ < {\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 36^\circ < {\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 30^\circ $$

$$ \Rightarrow \sqrt 2 < {\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 36^\circ < 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow \sqrt 2 r < r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 36^\circ < 2r$$

$$ \Rightarrow \sqrt 2 r - r < r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} 36^\circ - r < 2r - r$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right)r < R < r$$

$$\therefore$$ $$R < r$$, when $$n = 5$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (B) is wrong.

Option C :

When $$n = 8$$,

$$R + r = r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 8}$$

$${\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} \theta$$ is decreasing function in 0 to $${\pi \over 2}$$.

$$\therefore$$ $${\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 8} > {\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 4}$$

$$ \Rightarrow r{\mathop{\rm cosec}\nolimits} {\pi \over 8} > \sqrt 2 r$$

$$ \Rightarrow R + r > \sqrt 2 r$$

$$ \Rightarrow R > \left( {\sqrt 2 - 1} \right)r$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (C) is correct.

Option D :

When $$n = 12$$, then

$$R + r = r\left( {\cos ec{\pi \over {12}}} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow R + r = r \times {{2\sqrt 2 } \over {\sqrt 3 - 1}}$$ [as $$\cos ec{\pi \over {12}} = {{2\sqrt 2 } \over {\sqrt 3 - 1}}$$]

$$ \Rightarrow R + r = r \times {{2\sqrt 2 \left( {\sqrt 3 + 1} \right)} \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R + r = \sqrt 2 r\left( {\sqrt 3 + 1} \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow R = \left[ {\sqrt 2 \left( {\sqrt 3 + 1} \right) - 1} \right]r$$

$$ \Rightarrow R < \sqrt 2 \left( {\sqrt 3 + 1} \right)r$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (D) is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the equation of the circle which touches both the axes in the first quadrant and the line y - 2 = 0?
questions.solution:
To determine the equation of the circle that touches both the coordinate axes in the first quadrant and the line $y = 2$, we need to understand some properties of such circles.

Firstly, when a circle touches both the x-axis and y-axis in the first quadrant, its center $(h, k)$ must be at $(r, r)$, where $r$ is the radius of the circle. This is because the distance from the center to each of the coordinate axes (which is the radius) must be equal.

Now, since the circle also touches the line $y = 2$, we can set up the following:

The distance from the center of the circle $(r, r)$ to the line $y = 2$ should be equal to the radius $r$. Hence, we have:

$$|r - 2| = r$$

Now, solving this equation, we have:

$$|r - 2| = r$$

This can be either:

  1. $r - 2 = r$ (which gives no solution since $2 \neq 0$), or

  1. $-(r - 2) = r$

Simplifying the second case:

$$-r + 2 = r$$

We get:

$$2r = 2$$

Therefore:

$$r = 1$$

Thus, the center of the circle is $(1, 1)$ and the radius is 1.

The standard form of the equation of a circle with center $(h, k)$ and radius $r$ is:

$$(x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2$$

Substituting $h = 1$, $k = 1$ and $r = 1$:

$$(x - 1)^2 + (y - 1)^2 = 1$$

Expanding this equation:

$$x^2 - 2x + 1 + y^2 - 2y + 1 = 1$$

Simplifying:

$$x^2 + y^2 - 2x - 2y + 2 = 1$$

We can rewrite it as:

$$x^2 + y^2 - 2x - 2y + 1 = 0$$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: x2 + y2 - 2x - 2y + 1 = 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the diameter of a circle inscribed in a regular polygon of 12 sides, each of length 1 cm ?
questions.solution:
1. In a regular 12-gon of side $AB=1$, draw radii $OA$ and $OB$.

    ∠AOB = central angle = $\dfrac{360^\circ}{12}=30^\circ$.

  2. Let $M$ be midpoint of $AB$. Then $OM$ is the inradius $r$, and $AM=\tfrac12$.

  3. In right ΔAOM,

    $\displaystyle \tan\angle AOM=\tan\bigl(\tfrac{∠AOB}2\bigr)=\tan15^\circ =\frac{AM}{OM}=\frac{\tfrac12}{r}$.

    So

    $ r=\frac{\tfrac12}{\tan15^\circ} =\frac12\;\frac1{\tan15^\circ}. $

  4. Compute $\tan15^\circ$ using $\tan(45^\circ-30^\circ)$:

    $ \tan15^\circ =\frac{\tan45^\circ-\tan30^\circ}{1+\tan45^\circ\tan30^\circ} =\frac{1-\tfrac1{\sqrt3}}{1+1\cdot\tfrac1{\sqrt3}} =2-\sqrt3. $

    Hence

    $ \frac1{\tan15^\circ}=\frac1{2-\sqrt3} =2+\sqrt3. $

  5. Diameter $D=2r$:

    $ D=2\cdot\frac12\,(2+\sqrt3)=2+\sqrt3. $

Answer: Option C.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: The centre of the circle passing through the origin and making positive intercepts 4 and 6 on the coordinate axes, lies on the line
questions.solution:
Let's solve for the center of the circle passing through the origin and making positive intercepts 4 and 6 on the coordinate axes.

Given the intercepts, let's denote:

  • The x-intercept as $a = 4$
  • The y-intercept as $b = 6$

We can use these intercepts to determine the coordinates of the points where the circle intersects the x and y axes. These points are $(4, 0)$ and $(0, 6)$, respectively.

The general form of the equation of a circle is given by:

$$ (x - h)^2 + (y - k)^2 = r^2 $$

where $(h, k)$ is the center and $r$ is the radius of the circle. Since the circle passes through the origin $(0, 0)$, we substitute this point into the equation to get:

$$ h^2 + k^2 = r^2 \quad (1) $$

Since the circle also passes through the points $(4, 0)$ and $(0, 6)$, we have:

For $(4, 0)$:

$$ (4 - h)^2 + (0 - k)^2 = r^2 $$

For $(0, 6)$:

$$ (0 - h)^2 + (6 - k)^2 = r^2 $$

We now have three equations:

1) $ h^2 + k^2 = r^2 $

2) $ (4 - h)^2 + k^2 = r^2 $

3) $ h^2 + (6 - k)^2 = r^2 $

From 2), we expand and simplify:

$$ 16 - 8h + h^2 + k^2 = r^2 \quad (4 - h)^2 \Rightarrow 16 - 8h + h^2 $$

From 3), we expand and simplify:

$$ h^2 + 36 - 12k + k^2 = r^2 \quad (6 - k)^2 \Rightarrow 36 - 12k + k^2 $$

Subtracting equation 1) from the other two equations:

From 2):

$$ 16 - 8h = 0 \Rightarrow 8h = 16 \Rightarrow h = 2 $$

From 3):

$$ 36 - 12k = 0 \Rightarrow 12k = 36 \Rightarrow k = 3 $$

The center of the circle is $(h, k) = (2, 3)$.

Now, we need to determine which line passes through the point $(2, 3)$. We plug $(2, 3)$ into each line equation:

  • Option A: $2x - y + 1 = 0$
  • Option B: $3x - 2y - 1 = 0$
  • Option C: $3x - 4y + 6 = 0$
  • Option D: $2x + 3y - 26 = 0$

For Option A:

$$ 2(2) - 3 + 1 = 4 - 3 + 1 \Rightarrow 2 \ne 0 $$

For Option B:

$$ 3(2) - 2(3) - 1 = 6 - 6 - 1 \Rightarrow -1 \ne 0 $$

For Option C:

$$ 3(2) - 4(3) + 6 = 6 - 12 + 6 \Rightarrow 0 = 0 $$

For Option D:

$$ 2(2) + 3(3) - 26 = 4 + 9 - 26 \Rightarrow -13 \ne 0 $$

The correct option is Option C.

Therefore, the center of the circle lies on the line:

3x - 4y + 6 = 0

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: If $$|w| = 2$$, then the set of points $$z = w - {1 \over w}$$ is contained in or equal to the set of points z satisfying
questions.solution:
To solve this, we need to first express $$w$$ in the polar form since we know the modulus (or absolute value) of $$w$$.

$$w = |w|e^{iθ} = 2e^{iθ}$$

where $$θ$$ is the argument of $$w$$.

Substitute this into the expression for $$z$$:

$$z = w - {1 \over w} = 2e^{iθ} - {1 \over 2e^{iθ}}$$

By applying Euler's formula, which states that $$e^{iθ} = cos(θ) + isin(θ)$$, the expression becomes :

$$z = 2[cos(θ) + isin(θ)] - {1 \over 2}[cos(θ) - isin(θ)]$$

Simplify the expression :

$$z = [2cos(θ) - {1 \over 2}cos(θ)] + i[2sin(θ) + {1 \over 2}sin(θ)]$$

$$z = {3 \over 2}cos(θ) + {5 \over 2}isin(θ)$$

The modulus of $z$ can be found using the formula

$|z| = \sqrt{(Re(z))^2 + (Im(z))^2}$, where $Re(z)$ and $Im(z)$ are the real and imaginary parts of $z$, respectively.

So,

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{3}{2} \cos(θ))^2 + (\frac{5}{2} \sin(θ))^2} = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} \cos^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

We know that $\sin^2(θ) + \cos^2(θ) = 1$ (based on the Pythagorean trigonometric identity), so let's replace $\cos^2(θ)$ with $1 - \sin^2(θ)$ in the equation above.

$|z| = \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} (1 - \sin^2(θ))) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{(\frac{9}{4} - \frac{9}{4} \sin^2(θ)) + (\frac{25}{4} \sin^2(θ))}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9}{4} + \frac{16}{4} \sin^2(θ)}$

$= \sqrt{\frac{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}{4}}$

$ = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16 \sin^2(θ)}$

Now, $\sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 1, so $16 \sin^2(θ)$ is always less than or equal to 16.

So, the maximum value of $|z|$ is when $\sin^2(θ) = 1$, which makes $|z|$ equal to $\frac{1}{2} \sqrt{9 + 16} = \frac{1}{2} \sqrt{25} = \frac{5}{2}$.

So the set of points $z$ satisfying $|z| ≤ 3$ (Option D) includes all points $z$, and thus it is the correct choice.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The smallest positive integral value of n such that $${\left[ {{{1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} + i\cos {\pi \over 8}} \over {1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} - i\cos {\pi \over 8}}}} \right]^n}$$ is purely imaginary, is equal to
questions.solution:
$${\left[ {{{1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} + i\cos {\pi \over 8}} \over {1 + \sin {\pi \over 8} - i\cos {\pi \over 8}}}} \right]^n} = {\left[ {{{1 + \cos \alpha + i\sin \alpha } \over {1 + \cos \alpha - i\sin \alpha }}} \right]^n}$$ [$$\because$$ $$\alpha = {\pi \over 2} - {\pi \over 8}$$]

$$ = {\left[ {{{2{{\cos }^2}{\alpha \over 2} + 2i\sin {\alpha \over 2}\cos {\alpha \over 2}} \over {2{{\cos }^2}{\alpha \over 2} - 2i\sin {\alpha \over 2}\cos {\alpha \over 2}}}} \right]^n}$$

$$ = \left[ {{{\cos {\alpha \over 2} + i\sin {\alpha \over 2}} \over {\cos {\alpha \over 2} - i\sin {\alpha \over 2}}}} \right]_ - ^n = {\left( {{e^{2t{\alpha \over 2}}}} \right)^n} = {e^{in\,\alpha }}$$

$$ = {e^{in\left( {{{3\pi } \over 8}} \right)}} = \cos {{3n\pi } \over 8} + i\sin {{3n\pi } \over 8}$$

For n = 4 we get imaginary part.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $$z$$ be a complex number with a non-zero imaginary part. If

$$ \frac{2+3 z+4 z^{2}}{2-3 z+4 z^{2}} $$

is a real number, then the value of $$|z|^{2}$$ is _________.
questions.solution:
For a complex number $$z = x + iy$$, it's conjugate $$\overline z = x - iy$$. Now z is purely real when $$y = 0$$.

When $$y = 0$$ then $$z = x + i \times (0) = x$$ and $$\overline z = x - i \times (0) = x$$

$$\therefore$$ $$z = \overline z $$ when z is purely real.

Now given, $$w = {{2 + 3z + 4{z^2}} \over {2 - 3z + 4{z^2}}}$$ is real

$$\therefore$$ $$w = \overline w $$

$$ \Rightarrow {{2 + 3z + 4{z^2}} \over {2 - 3z + 4{z^2}}} = \left( {\overline {{{2 + 3z + 4{z^2}} \over {2 - 3z + 4{z^2}}}} } \right)$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{2 + 3z + 4{z^2}} \over {2 - 3z + 4{z^2}}} = {{2 + 3\overline z + 4{{(\overline z )}^2}} \over {2 - 3\overline z + 4{{(\overline z )}^2}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 4 - 6\overline z + 8{(\overline z )^2} + 6z - 9z\overline z + 12z{(\overline z )^2} + 8{(\overline z )^2} - 12{z^2}\overline z + 16{z^2}{(\overline z )^2} = 4 + 6\overline z + 8{(\overline z )^2} - 6z - 9z\overline z - 12z{(\overline z )^2} + 8{z^2} + 12{z^2}\overline z + 16{z^2}{(\overline z )^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 6\overline z + 6z + 12z{(\overline z )^2} + 12{z^2}\overline z = 6\overline z - 6z - 12z{(\overline z )^2} + 12{z^2}\overline z $$

$$ \Rightarrow 6(z - \overline z ) + 12z\overline z (\overline z - z) = 6(\overline z - z) + 12z\overline z (z - \overline z )$$

$$ \Rightarrow 12(z - \overline z ) + 24(\overline z - z)(z\overline z ) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 12(z - \overline z )[1 - 2z\overline z ] = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 12(x + iy - (x - iy))[1 - 2|z{|^2}] = 0$$ [as $$|z{|^2} = z\overline z $$]

$$ \Rightarrow 12 \times 2iy[1 - 2|z{|^2}] = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 24iy[1 - 2|z{|^2}] = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ $$y = 0$$ or $$1 - 2|z{|^2} = 0$$

$$y = 0$$ not possible as given z is a complex number with non-zero imaginary part.

$$\therefore$$ $$1 - 2|z{|^2} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow |z{|^2} = {1 \over 2} = 0.5$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $$\bar{z}$$ denote the complex conjugate of a complex number $$z$$ and let $$i=\sqrt{-1}$$. In the set of complex numbers, the number of distinct roots of the equation

$$ \bar{z}-z^{2}=i\left(\bar{z}+z^{2}\right) $$

is _________.
questions.solution:
Let, $$z = x + iy$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\overline z = x - iy$$

Given, $$\overline z - {z^2} = i(\overline z + {z^2})$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - iy) - {(x + iy)^2} = i\left[ {(x - iy) + {{(x + iy)}^2}} \right]$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - iy) - ({x^2} - {y^2} + 2ixy) = i[x - iy + {x^2} - {y^2} + 2ixy]$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - {x^2} + {y^2}) - iy(1 + 2x) = xi - {i^2}y + {x^2}i - i{y^2} + 2{i^2}xy$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - {x^2} + {y^2}) - iy(1 + 2x) = xi + y + i{x^2} - i{y^2} - 2xy$$

$$ \Rightarrow (x - {x^2} + {y^2}) - iy(1 + 2x) = y(1 - 2x) + i(x + {x^2} - {y^2})$$

Comparing both sides real part we get,

$$x - {x^2} + {y^2} = y - 2xy$$

$$ \Rightarrow x - {x^2} + {y^2} - y + 2xy = 0$$ ..... (1)

And comparing both sides imaginary part we get,

$$ - y(1 + 2x) = x + {x^2} - {y^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow - y - 2xy = x + {x^2} - {y^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x + {x^2} - {y^2} + y + 2xy = 0$$ ...... (2)

Adding equation (1) and (2) we get,

$$x - {x^2} + {y^2} - y + 2xy + x + {x^2} - {y^2} + y + 2xy = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2x + 4xy = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2x(1 + 2y) = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ $$x = 0$$ or $$1 + 2y = 0 \Rightarrow y = - {1 \over 2}$$

Case 1 : When $$x = 0$$ :

Put $$x = 0$$ at equation (1), we get

$${y^2} - y = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow y(y - 1) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow y = 0,1$$

$$\therefore$$ $$z = 0 + 0i$$ or $$0 + i$$

Case 2 : When $$y = - {1 \over 2}$$ :

Put $$y = - {1 \over 2}$$ in equation (1), we get

$$x - {x^2} + {1 \over 4} + {1 \over 2} - x = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} = {1 \over 4} + {1 \over 2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {x^2} = {3 \over 4}$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = \, \pm \,{{\sqrt 3 } \over 2}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$z = {{\sqrt 3 } \over 2} - {i \over 2}$$ or $$z = - {{\sqrt 3 } \over 2} - {i \over 2}$$

$$\therefore$$ Number of distinct $$z = 4$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let $\bar{z}$ denote the complex conjugate of a complex number $z$. If $z$ is a non-zero complex number for which both real and imaginary parts of

$$
(\bar{z})^{2}+\frac{1}{z^{2}}
$$

are integers, then which of the following is/are possible value(s) of $|z|$ ?
questions.solution:
Let, complex number $${\left( {\overline z } \right)^2} + {1 \over {{z^2}}}$$ is a new complex number $$w$$.

$$\therefore$$ $$w = {\left( {\overline z } \right)^2} + {1 \over {{z^2}}}$$

Now, let $$z = r{e^{i\theta }}$$ where $$r = |z|$$ and $$\theta$$ = argument

$$\therefore$$ $$w = {\left( {r{e^{ - i\theta }}} \right)^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}({e^{ - 2i\theta }})$$

$$ = \left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right){e^{ - 2i\theta }}$$

$$ = \left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right)(\cos 2\theta - i\sin 2\theta )$$

$$\therefore$$ Real part of $$w = {\mathop{\rm Re}\nolimits} (w) = \left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right)\cos 2\theta $$

Imaginary part of $$w = {\mathop{\rm Im}\nolimits} (w) = \left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right)( - \sin 2\theta )$$

Given both $${\mathop{\rm Re}\nolimits} (w)$$ and $${\mathop{\rm Im}\nolimits} (w)$$ are integer.

$$\therefore$$ Let $$Re(w) = {I_1}$$

and $${\mathop{\rm Im}\nolimits} (w) = {I_2}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$I_1^2 + I_2^2 = {\left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right)^2} = \alpha $$ (where $$\alpha$$ is an integer)

[Note : As I1 and I2 are integer so $$I_1^2 + I_2^2$$ is also an integer.]

Now, $${\left( {{r^2} + {1 \over {{r^2}}}} \right)^2} = \alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow {r^4} + 2 + {1 \over {{r^4}}} = \alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow {r^8} + 2{r^4} + 1 = {r^4}\alpha $$

$$ \Rightarrow {r^8} - (\alpha - 2){r^4} + 1 = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ $${r^4} = {{(\alpha - 2)\, \pm \,\sqrt {{{(\alpha - 2)}^2} - 4\,.\,1\,.\,1} } \over {2\,.\,1}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow r = {\left( {{{(\alpha - 2)\, \pm \,\sqrt {{\alpha ^2} - 4\alpha } } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow |z| = r = {\left( {{{(\alpha - 2)\, \pm \,\sqrt {{\alpha ^2} - 4\alpha } } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

In option only positive sign is given so ignoring negative sign we get,

$$|z| = r = {\left( {{{(\alpha - 2) + \sqrt {{\alpha ^2} - 4\alpha } } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$ ....... (1)

From option (A),

$$|z| = {\left( {{{43 + 3\sqrt {205} } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

Comparing with (1), we get

$$\alpha - 2 = 43$$

$$ \Rightarrow \alpha = 45$$

Putting $$\alpha = 45$$ in (1), we get

$$|z| = {\left( {{{43 + \sqrt {45(41)} } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

$$ = {\left( {{{43 + \sqrt {9 \times 5 \times 41} } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

$$ = {\left( {{{43 + 3\sqrt {205} } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (A) is correct.

We can re-write

$$|z| = {\left( {{{(\alpha - 2) + \sqrt {{\alpha ^2} - 4\alpha } } \over 2}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

as $$|z| = {\left( {{{2(\alpha - 2) + 2\sqrt {\alpha (\alpha - 4)} } \over 4}} \right)^{{1 \over 4}}}$$

Comparing with option (B) we get,

$$2(\alpha - 2) = 7$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2\alpha = 11$$

$$ \Rightarrow \alpha = {{11} \over 2}$$ $$\ne$$ Integer

$$\therefore$$ Option (B) is incorrect.

Similarly option (C) and (D) also incorrect.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the principal argument of $\frac{1}{1 + i}$ where $i = \sqrt{-1}?$
questions.solution:
To determine the principal argument of the complex number $$\frac{1}{1 + i}$$ where $$i = \sqrt{-1}$$, we will perform the following steps:

  1. Find the complex conjugate of the denominator.
  2. Multiply the numerator and the denominator by the complex conjugate of the denominator.
  3. Simplify the expression to obtain the complex number in standard form $a + bi$.
  4. Calculate the argument (angle) of the resulting complex number.

Step 1: The complex conjugate of $1 + i$ is $1 - i$.

Step 2: Multiply the numerator and the denominator by the complex conjugate $1 - i$.

$$\frac{1}{1 + i} \cdot \frac{1 - i}{1 - i} = \frac{1 - i}{(1 + i)(1 - i)}$$

Step 3: Simplify the denominator:

$$(1 + i)(1 - i) = 1^2 - i^2 = 1 + 1 = 2$$

Thus,

$$\frac{1 - i}{2} = \frac{1}{2} - \frac{i}{2}$$

Which means the complex number in standard form is $\frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{2}i$.

Step 4: Calculate the argument:

The argument $\theta$ of a complex number $a + bi$ is given by:

$$\theta = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{b}{a}\right)$$

For $\frac{1}{2} - \frac{1}{2}i$, $a = \frac{1}{2}$ and $b = -\frac{1}{2}$.

$$\theta = \tan^{-1}\left(\frac{-\frac{1}{2}}{\frac{1}{2}}\right) = \tan^{-1}(-1)$$

The value of $\tan^{-1}(-1)$ is $-\frac{\pi}{4}$, which is in the range $-\pi < \theta \leq \pi$ for the principal argument.

Therefore, the principal argument of $$\frac{1}{1 + i}$$ is:

Option B: $$-\frac{\pi}{4}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the modulus of $\left(\frac{\sqrt{-3}}{2}-\frac{1}{2}\right)^{200}?$
questions.solution:
To determine the modulus of the given complex number expression, we first need to identify the modulus of the base complex number before raising it to the $200$th power.

The given complex number is:

$$\left(\frac{\sqrt{-3}}{2} - \frac{1}{2}\right)$$

We can rewrite this as:

$$\frac{\sqrt{-3}}{2} - \frac{1}{2} = \frac{\sqrt{3}i - 1}{2}$$

Let's denote the complex number as $z$:

$$z = \frac{\sqrt{3}i - 1}{2}$$

We need to find the modulus of $z$. The modulus of a complex number $a + bi$ is given by:

$$|a + bi| = \sqrt{a^2 + b^2}$$

For our complex number $z = \frac{\sqrt{3}i - 1}{2}$, $a = -\frac{1}{2}$ and $b = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$.

Therefore, the modulus of $z$ is:

$$|z| = \sqrt{\left(-\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 + \left(\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)^2}$$

Simplifying the expression inside the square root:

$$|z| = \sqrt{\frac{1}{4} + \frac{3}{4}} = \sqrt{1} = 1$$

This shows that the modulus of the complex number $z = \frac{\sqrt{3}i - 1}{2}$ is $1$.

Now, we need to find the modulus of $z^{200}$. Since the modulus of a power of a complex number is the power of the modulus of that number, we get:

$$|z^{200}| = |z|^{200} = 1^{200} = 1$$

Therefore, the modulus of $\left(\frac{\sqrt{-3}}{2} - \frac{1}{2}\right)^{200}$ is $1$.

The correct answer is:

Option C: 1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the modulus of z?
options: 1 + sin2 θ
questions.solution:
1. $z=\frac{1+i\sin\theta}{1−i\sin\theta}$

  2. $|z|=\frac{|1+i\sin\theta|}{|1−i\sin\theta|}$

  3. $|1+i\sin\theta|=\sqrt{1^2+(\sin\theta)^2}=\sqrt{1+\sin^2\theta}$

  4. $|1−i\sin\theta|=\sqrt{1^2+(-\sin\theta)^2}=\sqrt{1+\sin^2\theta}$

  5. $\displaystyle |z|=\frac{\sqrt{1+\sin^2\theta}}{\sqrt{1+\sin^2\theta}}=1$

Answer: Option A (1).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question:
What is angle θ such that z is purely real ?

where n is an integer
questions.solution:
1. Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator:

    $$z=\frac{1+\mathrm{i}\sin\theta}{1-\mathrm{i}\sin\theta} \times\frac{1+\mathrm{i}\sin\theta}{1+\mathrm{i}\sin\theta} =\frac{(1+\mathrm{i}\sin\theta)^2}{1+(\sin\theta)^2}.$$

  2. Expand the numerator:

    $$(1+\mathrm{i}\sin\theta)^2 =1+2\mathrm{i}\sin\theta+(\mathrm{i}\sin\theta)^2 =1+2\mathrm{i}\sin\theta-\sin^2\theta =\cos^2\theta+2\mathrm{i}\sin\theta.$$

  3. Thus

    $$z=\frac{\cos^2\theta+2\mathrm{i}\sin\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta} =\frac{\cos^2\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta} \;+\;\mathrm{i}\,\frac{2\sin\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta}.$$

  4. For $z$ to be purely real, its imaginary part must be zero:

    $$\frac{2\sin\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta}=0 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \sin\theta=0 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \theta=n\pi,\; n\in\mathbb{Z}.$$

Answer: θ = nπ. (Option C)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question:
What is angle θ such that z is purely imaginary ?

where n is an integer
questions.solution:
1. Write

    $$ z=\frac{1+i\sin\theta}{1-i\sin\theta}. $$

  2. Multiply numerator and denominator by the conjugate of the denominator:

    $$ z =\frac{(1+i\sin\theta)(1+i\sin\theta)}{(1-i\sin\theta)(1+i\sin\theta)} =\frac{(1+i\sin\theta)^2}{1+\sin^2\theta}. $$

  3. Expand the numerator:

    $$ (1+i\sin\theta)^2 =1+2i\sin\theta+(i\sin\theta)^2 =1+2i\sin\theta-\sin^2\theta =\cos^2\theta+2i\sin\theta. $$

  4. So

    $$ z =\frac{\cos^2\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta} +i\,\frac{2\sin\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta}. $$

  5. For $z$ to be purely imaginary, the real part must be zero:

    $$ \frac{\cos^2\theta}{1+\sin^2\theta}=0 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \cos^2\theta=0 \quad\Longrightarrow\quad \cos\theta=0. $$

  6. $\cos\theta=0$ when

    $$ \theta=\frac{(2n+1)\pi}{2},\quad n\in\mathbb{Z}. $$

Answer: $\displaystyle \theta=\frac{(2n+1)\pi}{2}$ (Option B).

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: Ninhydrin gives yellow colour in paper chromatography with which amino acid?
questions.solution:
Option B : Proline

Explanation :

Ninhydrin is a chemical reagent commonly used to detect amino acids, peptides, and proteins. While most amino acids react with ninhydrin to give a purple-blue color, Proline and hydroxyproline are exceptions. These two amino acids react with ninhydrin to give a yellow color instead. This is because proline is a secondary amino acid, meaning its amino group is a part of a ring structure, which alters its reactivity compared to primary amino acids.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Match the compounds in LIST-I with the observations in LIST-II, and choose the correct option.

List-I List-II
(I) Aniline
 (P) Sodium fusion extract of the compound on boiling with $\mathrm{FeSO}_{4}$, followed by acidification with conc. $\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{SO}_{4}$, gives Prussian blue color.
(II) $o$-Cresol (Q) Sodium fusion extract of the compound on treatment with sodium nitroprusside gives blood red color.
(III) Cysteine
 (R) Addition of the compound to a saturated solution of $\mathrm{NaHCO}_{3}$ results in effervescence.
(IV) Caprolactam
 (S) The compound reacts with bromine water to give a white precipitate.
(T) Treating the compound with neutral $\mathrm{FeCl}_{3}$ solution produces violet color.
questions.solution:
(I) Aniline has $\mathrm{N}$ atoms hence gives prussian blue colour in Sodium fusion extract test. Aniline also gives white precipitate with Bromine water.

(II) $\mathbf{O}$-Cresol has phenolic group hence gives violet colour complex with netural $\mathrm{FeCl}_3$.

(III) Cysteine has 'S' atom, hence gives blood red color when odium S fusion extract of this compound is treated with sodium nitroprusside.

(IV) Caprolactam has $\mathrm{N}$ atom, hence sodium extract of this compound gives prussian blue color.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: Consider the inequations 5x - 4y + 12 < 0, x + y < 2, x < 0 and y > 0. Which one of the following points lies in the common region?
questions.solution:
To determine which of the given points lies within the common region defined by the inequations, we need to check each point against all the given conditions:

The inequations are:

  • $$5x - 4y + 12 < 0$$
  • $$x + y < 2$$
  • $$x < 0$$
  • $$y > 0$$

Let's evaluate each of the given points:

Option A: (0, 0)

  • $$5(0) - 4(0) + 12 = 12 \not< 0$$
  • $$0 + 0 = 0 < 2$$
  • $$0 \not< 0$$
  • $$0 \not> 0$$

This point does not satisfy all the inequations.

Option B: (-2, 4)

  • $$5(-2) - 4(4) + 12 = -10 - 16 + 12 = -14 < 0$$
  • $$-2 + 4 = 2 \not< 2$$
  • $$-2 < 0$$
  • $$4 > 0$$

This point does not satisfy all the inequations.

Option C: (-1, 4)

  • $$5(-1) - 4(4) + 12 = -5 - 16 + 12 = -9 < 0$$
  • $$-1 + 4 = 3 \not< 2$$
  • $$-1 < 0$$
  • $$4 > 0$$

This point does not satisfy all the inequations.

Option D: (-1, 2)

  • $$5(-1) - 4(2) + 12 = -5 - 8 + 12 = -1 < 0$$
  • $$-1 + 2 = 1 < 2$$
  • $$-1 < 0$$
  • $$2 > 0$$

This point satisfies all the inequations.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: (-1, 2)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If the points with coordinates (-5, 0), (5p<sup>2</sup>, 10p) and (5q<sup>2</sup>, 10q) are collinear, then what is the value of pq where p ≠ q ?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of $ pq $ where $ p \neq q $, given that the points $(-5, 0)$, $(5p^2, 10p)$, and $(5q^2, 10q)$ are collinear, we must use the concept that three points are collinear if the area of the triangle formed by these points is zero. We can use the determinant formula for the area of a triangle.

Given points $ A (-5, 0) $, $ B (5p^2, 10p) $, and $ C (5q^2, 10q) $, the area of the triangle formed by these points is given by the determinant:

$$ \text{Area} = \frac{1}{2} \begin{vmatrix} -5 & 0 & 1 \\ 5p^2 & 10p & 1 \\ 5q^2 & 10q & 1 \\ \end{vmatrix} $$

For the points to be collinear, the area must be zero:

$$ \begin{vmatrix} -5 & 0 & 1 \\ 5p^2 & 10p & 1 \\ 5q^2 & 10q & 1 \\ \end{vmatrix} = 0 $$

Expanding this determinant:

$$ -5 \begin{vmatrix} 10p & 1 \\ 10q & 1 \\ \end{vmatrix} - 0 \begin{vmatrix} 5p^2 & 1 \\ 5q^2 & 1 \\ \end{vmatrix} + 1 \begin{vmatrix} 5p^2 & 10p \\ 5q^2 & 10q \\ \end{vmatrix} = 0 $$

Evaluating the 2x2 determinants:

$$ = -5 \left( 10p \cdot 1 - 10q \cdot 1 \right) + 1 \left( 5p^2 \cdot 10q - 5q^2 \cdot 10p \right) $$

$$ = -5 (10p - 10q) + (50p^2 q - 50pq^2) $$

$$ = -50 (p - q) + 50 (p^2 q - pq^2) $$

Factor out the common terms:

$$ = 50 \left[ (p^2 q - pq^2) - (p - q) \right] $$

$$ = 50 \left[ pq(p - q) - (p - q) \right] $$

$$ = 50 (p - q) [pq - 1] = 0 $$

Since $ p \neq q $, $ (p - q) \neq 0 $. Therefore:

$$ pq - 1 = 0 $$

$$ pq = 1 $$

Thus, the correct value of $ pq $ is 1. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option C: 1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the equation of the straight line which passes through the point (1, -2) and cuts off equal intercepts from the axes ?
questions.solution:
To determine the equation of the straight line that passes through the point (1, -2) and cuts off equal intercepts from the axes, we can proceed as follows:

Let the intercepts on the x-axis and y-axis be $ a $. Hence, the equation of the line with equal intercepts is given by

$$\frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{a} = 1$$

or simply,

$$x + y = a$$

We need to find the value of $ a $. Since the line passes through the point (1, -2), we can substitute $ x = 1 $ and $ y = -2 $ into the equation:

$$1 + (-2) = a$$

$$a = -1$$

Therefore, the equation of the line is

$$x + y = -1$$

This equation can also be written as

$$x + y + 1 = 0$$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option C: $ x + y + 1 = 0 $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: A straight line passes through the point of intersection of x + 2y + 2 = 0 and 2x - 3y - 3 = 0. It cuts equal intercepts in the fourth quadrant. What is the sum of the absolute values of the intercepts?
questions.solution:
Let’s solve the problem step by step.

We start by finding the point of intersection of the given lines:

$$x + 2y + 2 = 0 \quad \text{(1)}$$

$$2x - 3y - 3 = 0 \quad \text{(2)}$$

To find the intersection point, we can solve these simultaneous equations.

Multiply equation (1) by 2:

$$2(x + 2y + 2) = 0$$

$$2x + 4y + 4 = 0 \quad \text{(3)}$$

Now we have:

$$2x + 4y + 4 = 0 \quad \text{(3)}$$

$$2x - 3y - 3 = 0 \quad \text{(2)}$$

Subtract equation (2) from (3):

$$ (2x + 4y + 4) - (2x - 3y - 3) = 0$$

$$ 2x + 4y + 4 - 2x + 3y + 3 = 0$$

$$ 7y + 7 = 0$$

$$ y = -1 $$

Substitute $ y = -1 $ back into equation (1):

$$ x + 2(-1) + 2 = 0$$

$$ x - 2 + 2 = 0$$

$$ x = 0$$

So, the point of intersection is $ (0, -1) $.

Next, we need to determine the equation of the line that passes through this point and cuts equal intercepts in the fourth quadrant. The general form of a line cutting intercepts on the coordinate axes is:

$$ \frac{x}{a} + \frac{y}{b} = 1 $$

If the intercepts are equal (let's call them $ c $), then:

$$ a = -c \ \text{and} \ b = -c $$

Thus, the equation becomes:

$$ \frac{x}{c} + \frac{y}{c} = 1 $$

$$ \frac{x + y}{c} = 1 $$

$$ x + y = c $$

We know that the line passes through point $ (0, -1) $, so substitute these coordinates into the equation:

$$0 + (-1) = c$$

$$ c = -1 $$

The equation of the line is:

$$ x + y = -1 $$

The x-intercept is found by setting $ y = 0 $:

$$ x + 0 = -1 $$

$$ x = -1 $$

The y-intercept is found by setting $ x = 0 $:

$$ 0 + y = -1 $$

$$ y = -1 $$

The absolute values of the intercepts are:

$$ \left| -1 \right| = 1 $$

Since the intercepts are equal, the sum of the absolute values is:

$$ 1 + 1 = 2 $$

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: 2

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: Under which one of the following conditions are the lines ax + by + c = 0 and bx + ay + c = 0 parallel (a ≠ 0, b ≠ 0)?
questions.solution:
To determine under which one of the conditions the lines $ ax + by + c = 0 $ and $ bx + ay + c = 0 $ are parallel, we need to consider the slopes of these lines. Two lines are parallel if their slopes are equal.

First, we rewrite the given lines in slope-intercept form $ y = mx + b $.

For the line $ ax + by + c = 0 $:

$$ ax + by + c = 0 \implies by = -ax - c \implies y = -\frac{a}{b}x - \frac{c}{b} $$

So, the slope $ m_1 $ of the first line is $ -\frac{a}{b} $.

For the line $ bx + ay + c = 0 $:

$$ bx + ay + c = 0 \implies ay = -bx - c \implies y = -\frac{b}{a}x - \frac{c}{a} $$

So, the slope $ m_2 $ of the second line is $ -\frac{b}{a} $.

For the lines to be parallel, their slopes must be equal:

$$ -\frac{a}{b} = -\frac{b}{a} $$

We can simplify this equation to find the condition for parallelism:

$$ \frac{a}{b} = \frac{b}{a} \implies a^2 = b^2 \implies a^2 - b^2 = 0 $$

Hence, the correct condition under which the lines are parallel is $ a^2 - b^2 = 0 $.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

a2 - b2 = 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the equation of the locus of the mid-point of the line segment obtained by cutting the line x + y = p, (where p is a real number) by the coordinate axes ?
questions.solution:
To determine the equation of the locus of the mid-point of the line segment cut by the line $ x + y = p $ on the coordinate axes, we first need to find the points where the line intersects the axes.

The given line equation is:

$$ x + y = p $$

First, let's find the intersection points with the coordinate axes.

Intersection with the x-axis:

For the x-axis, $ y = 0 $. So we substitute $ y = 0 $ into the line equation:

$$ x + 0 = p $$

Which gives:

$$ x = p $$

Hence, the intersection point with the x-axis is $ (p, 0) $.

Intersection with the y-axis:

For the y-axis, $ x = 0 $. So we substitute $ x = 0 $ into the line equation:

$$ 0 + y = p $$

Which gives:

$$ y = p $$

Hence, the intersection point with the y-axis is $ (0, p) $.

Now, the mid-point of the line segment connecting these two points $(p, 0)$ and $(0, p)$ can be found using the mid-point formula:

$$ \left( \frac{x_1 + x_2}{2}, \frac{y_1 + y_2}{2} \right) $$

Substituting $ x_1 = p $, $ y_1 = 0 $, $ x_2 = 0 $, and $ y_2 = p $ into the formula, we get:

$$ \left( \frac{p + 0}{2}, \frac{0 + p}{2} \right) = \left( \frac{p}{2}, \frac{p}{2} \right) $$

Let's denote the mid-point as $(x, y)$. Therefore, we have:

$$ x = \frac{p}{2} \quad \text{and} \quad y = \frac{p}{2} $$

Since $ x $ and $ y $ are both equal to $ \frac{p}{2} $, we can simply say:

$$ x = y $$

This is the equation of the locus of the mid-point.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: x - y = 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If the point (x, y) is equidistant from the points (2a, 0) and (0, 3a) where a > 0, then which one of the following is correct ?
questions.solution:
The distance between two points in a two-dimensional plane is given by:

$ d = \sqrt{(x_2 - x_1)^2 + (y_2 - y_1)^2} $

Calculation:

Given:

The point (x, y) is equidistant from the points (2a, 0) and (0, 3a), where $ a > 0 $.

Since the distances are equal, we have:

$ \sqrt{(2a - x)^2 + (0 - y)^2} = \sqrt{(0 - x)^2 + (3a - y)^2} $

Squaring both sides, we get:

$ (2a - x)^2 + y^2 = x^2 + (3a - y)^2 $

Expanding both sides, we obtain:

$ 4a^2 + x^2 - 4ax + y^2 = x^2 + 9a^2 + y^2 - 6ay $

Simplifying, we get:

$ 4a^2 - 4ax = 9a^2 - 6ay $

Collecting like terms, we get:

$ 5a^2 - 6ay + 4ax = 0 $

Finally, simplifying further, we get:

$ 4x - 6y + 5a = 0 $

∴ $ 4x - 6y + 5a = 0 $ is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of the line passing through origin and inclining at an angle of 75° with the positive direction of x-axis :

1. The line passes through the point $\left(1, \frac{1}{2−\sqrt{3}}\right)$.

2. The line entirely lies in first and third quadrants.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.solution:
The equation of a line passing through a point $(h, k)$ and inclined at an angle $\theta$ with the positive direction of the x-axis is given by: $(y - k) = \tan \theta (x - h)$, where $\tan \theta$ is the slope of the line.

Values Used:

  • $\tan 75° = \frac{\sqrt{3} + 1}{\sqrt{3} - 1}$

Calculations:

Given that the line passes through the origin and is inclined at an angle of 75° with the positive direction of the x-axis:

1. Checking if the line passes through the point $\left(1, \frac{1}{2−\sqrt{3}}\right)$:

The equation of the line passing through the origin (0,0) with an inclination of 75° is:

$y = \frac{\sqrt{3} + 1}{\sqrt{3} - 1} x$

Rewriting, we get: $(\sqrt{3} + 1)x - (\sqrt{3} - 1)y = 0$

Substituting the point $\left(1, \frac{1}{2−\sqrt{3}}\right)$:

$ (\sqrt{3} + 1)(1) - (\sqrt{3} - 1)\left(\frac{1}{2 - \sqrt{3}}\right) = \frac{(\sqrt{3} + 1)(2 - \sqrt{3}) - (\sqrt{3} - 1)}{2 - \sqrt{3}} = 0$

This confirms that the point $\left(1, \frac{1}{2−\sqrt{3}}\right)$ lies on the line, making statement 1 true.

2. Checking if the line lies entirely in the first and third quadrants:

The line passes through the origin and is inclined at an angle of 75° with the positive x-axis. Plotting this line clearly shows that it only occupies the first and third quadrants.

This confirms that statement 2 is also true.

∴ The correct option is (C).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: If P(3, 4) is the mid-point of a line segment between the axes, then what is the equation of the line?
questions.solution:
To find the equation of the line such that the point $ P(3, 4) $ is the midpoint of the line segment between the x-axis and y-axis, we need to determine the coordinates of the points where the line intersects the axes.

Let $ A(x_1, 0) $ be the point where the line intersects the x-axis, and $ B(0, y_1) $ be the point where the line intersects the y-axis.

If $ P(3, 4) $ is the midpoint of $ A $ and $ B $, then by the midpoint formula:

$$ P = \left( \frac{x_1 + 0}{2}, \frac{0 + y_1}{2} \right) $$

Given that $ P(3, 4) $, we equate and solve for $ x_1 $ and $ y_1 $:

$$ 3 = \frac{x_1}{2} \implies x_1 = 6 $$

$$ 4 = \frac{y_1}{2} \implies y_1 = 8 $$

Therefore, the line intersects the x-axis at $ (6, 0) $ and the y-axis at $ (0, 8) $.

To derive the equation of the line passing through $ (6, 0) $ and $ (0, 8) $, we use the two-point form of the equation of a line:

$$ y - y_1 = \frac{y_2 - y_1}{x_2 - x_1} (x - x_1) $$

Substituting $ (x_1, y_1) = (6, 0) $ and $ (x_2, y_2) = (0, 8) $:

$$ y - 0 = \frac{8 - 0}{0 - 6} (x - 6) $$

$$ y = -\frac{4}{3} ( x - 6) $$

$$ y = -\frac{4}{3} x + 8 $$

Rearranging to standard form $ Ax + By + C = 0 $:

$$ 4x + 3y - 24 = 0 $$

The correct option is:

Option B:

4x + 3y - 24 = 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: The base AB of an equilateral triangle ABC with side 8 cm lies along the y-axis such that the mid-point of AB is at the origin and B lies above the origin. What is the equation of line passing through (8, 0) and parallel to the side AC ?
questions.solution:
In an equilateral triangle, all sides are equal, and each angle measures 60°.

The slopes of parallel lines are equal.

The slope of a line inclined to the x-axis at an angle θ in the positive direction is given by tan θ.

The equation of a line with slope m that passes through the point (a,b) is given by: y - b = m(x - a).

  • tan(180° + x) = tan x

Calculation:

Given: The base AB of an equilateral triangle ABC with side length 8 cm lies along the y-axis such that the mid-point of AB is at the origin and B lies above the origin.

From the figure, it can be observed that AC makes an angle of (180° + 30°) = 210° with the positive x-axis.

⇒ Slope of AC = tan 210° = tan (180° + 30°) = tan 30° = $1 \over \sqrt{3}$

⇒ Therefore, the slope of any line parallel to AC is also $1 \over \sqrt{3}$

The equation of the line passing through the point (8, 0) and parallel to side AC is:

y - 0 = $1 \over \sqrt{3}$(x - 8)

⇒ x - $\sqrt{3}$ y - 8 = 0

∴ The correct option is (A).

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
LIST-I contains metal species and LIST-II contains their properties.

List-I List-II
(I) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}(\mathrm{CN})_{6}\right]^{4-}$
 (P) $t_{2 \mathrm{g}}$ orbitals contain 4 electrons
(II) $\left[\mathrm{RuCl}_{6}\right]^{2-}$ (Q) $\mu$ (spin-only $)=4.9 \mathrm{BM}$
(III) $\left[\mathrm{Cr}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$
 (R) low spin complex ion
(IV) $\left[\mathrm{Fe}\left(\mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O}\right)_{6}\right]^{2+}$
 (S) metal ion in $4+$ oxidation state
(T) $d^{4}$ species

[Given: Atomic number of $\mathrm{Cr}=24, \mathrm{Ru}=44, \mathrm{Fe}=26$ ]

Match each metal species in LIST-I with their properties in LIST-II, and choose the correct option
options: I $\rightarrow$ R, T; II $\rightarrow$ P, S; III $\rightarrow$ Q, T; IV $\rightarrow$ P, Q
I $\rightarrow$ R, S; II $\rightarrow$ P, T; III $\rightarrow$ P, Q; IV $\rightarrow$ Q, T
I $\rightarrow$ P, R; II $\rightarrow$ R, S; III $\rightarrow$ R, T; IV $\rightarrow$ P, T
I $\rightarrow$ Q, T; II $\rightarrow$ S, T; III $\rightarrow$ P, T; IV $\rightarrow$ Q, R
questions.solution:
(I) $${[Cr{(CN)_6}]^{4 - }}$$

$$C{r^{ + 2}} = [Ar]\,3{d^4}\,4{s^0}$$

It is d2sp3 hybridised as CN$$-$$ is a strong field ligand.

(II) $${[RuC{l_6}]^{2 - }}$$

$$R{u^{ + 4}} = [Kr]\,4{d^4}\,5{s^0}$$

t2g set contains 4 electron.

(III) $${[Cr{({H_2}O)_6}]^{2 + }}$$

$$C{r^{ + 2}} = [Ar]\,3{d^4}\,4{s^0}$$

It has 4 unpaired e$$-$$ as H2O is weak field ligand.

So, its $$\mu$$ = 4.9 B.M.

(IV) $${[Fe{({H_2}O)_6}]^{2 + }}$$

$$F{e^{ + 2}} = [Ar]\,3{d^6}\,4{s^0}$$

$$ = t_{2g}^4\,e_g^2$$

It has 4 unpaired e$$-$$, its $$\mu$$ = 4.9 B.M.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: In the arrangement shown in figure, when the switch S2 is open, the galvanometer, shows no deflection for $$l$$ = 50 cm when the switch S2 is closed, the galvanometer shows no deflection for $$l$$ = 0.416 m. The internal resistance (r) of 6 V cell is
questions.solution:
When S2 open.

Assume resistance of XY = R.

Resistance of wire per unit length, x = $$\frac{R}{L}$$ = R $$\Omega$$ m$$-$$1

$$\because$$ I = E0 / R

Now, the potential drop across 50 cm length is 6 V, so

$$\frac{E_0}{R}$$ $$\times$$ R $$\times$$ $$\frac{50}{100}=6$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ E0 = 12 V

When S2 closed, potential drop across 0.416 cm length,

V1 = $$\frac{E_0}{R}\times$$ R $$\times\,0.416=12\times0.416\approx 5$$ V

Hence, E $$-$$ Ir = 5V

$$\Rightarrow$$ 6 $$-$$ Ir = 5

$$\because$$ I = $$\frac{5}{10}$$

$$\therefore$$ 6 $$-$$ = $$\frac{5}{10}$$r

$$\Rightarrow$$ r = 2$$\Omega$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
The figure shows a circuit having eight resistances of $1 \Omega$ each, labelled $R_{1}$ to $R_{8}$, and two ideal batteries with voltages $\varepsilon_{1}=12 \mathrm{~V}$ and $\varepsilon_{2}=6 \mathrm{~V}$.

Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The magnitude of current flowing through $R_{1}$ is $7.2 \mathrm{~A}$.
The magnitude of current flowing through $R_{2}$ is $1.2 \mathrm{~A}$.
The magnitude of current flowing through $R_{3}$ is $4.8 \mathrm{~A}$.
The magnitude of current flowing through $R_{5}$ is $2.4 \mathrm{~A}$.
questions.solution:
Point A and B are at same potential so they can be merged/folded.

For loop 1


$$ \begin{aligned} & -\frac{1}{2} x-\frac{1}{2}(x+y)-x+6=0 \\\\ &\Rightarrow -2 x-\frac{y}{2}=-6 \\\\ &\Rightarrow -4 x-y=-12 \\\\ &\Rightarrow 4 x+y=12 .......(1) \end{aligned} $$

For loop 2

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{1}{2} y+1 y+12+\frac{1}{2}(x+y)=0 \\\\ &\Rightarrow \frac{3}{2} y+\frac{y}{2}+\frac{x}{2}=-12 \\\\ &\Rightarrow 2 y+\frac{x}{2}=-12 \\\\ & \begin{array}{l} \Rightarrow4 y+x=-24 \\\\ \Rightarrow4 y+x-16 x-4 y =-24-48 \end{array} \\\\ &\Rightarrow -15 x=-72 \end{aligned} $$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $x=\frac{72}{15}$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $4\left(\frac{72}{15}\right)+y=12$

$$ \begin{aligned} \Rightarrow y & =12-\frac{288}{15} \\\\ & =\frac{180-288}{15} \\\\ & =\frac{-108}{15}=-7.2 \mathrm{~A} \end{aligned} $$

Current in $R_1=7.2 \mathrm{~A}$

Current in $R_2=\frac{x+y}{2}=\left(\frac{72}{15}-\frac{108}{15}\right) \frac{1}{2}$

$$ \begin{aligned} & =\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{36}{15}\right)=\frac{2.4}{2} \mathrm{~A} \\\\ & =1.2 \mathrm{~A} \end{aligned} $$

Current in $R_3=x=4.8 \mathrm{~A}$

Current in $R_5=\frac{1}{2} x=2.4 \mathrm{~A}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
In Circuit-1 and Circuit- 2 shown in the figures, $R_{1}=1 \,\Omega, R_{2}=2 \,\Omega$ and $R_{3}=3 \,\Omega$.

$P_{1}$ and $P_{2}$ are the power dissipations in Circuit-1 and Circuit-2 when the switches $\mathrm{S}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{S}_{2}$ are in open conditions, respectively.

$Q_{1}$ and $Q_{2}$ are the power dissipations in Circuit-1 and Circuit-2 when the switches $\mathrm{S}_{1}$ and $\mathrm{S}_{2}$ are in closed conditions, respectively.


Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: When a voltage source of $6 V$ is connected across $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ in both circuits, $P_{1} < P_{2}$.
When a constant current source of $2 \mathrm{Amp}$ is connected across A and B in both circuits, $P_{1}>P_{2}$.
When a voltage source of $6 V$ is connected across $\mathrm{A}$ and $\mathrm{B}$ in Circuit-1, $Q_{1}>P_{1}$.
When a constant current source of $2 \mathrm{Amp}$ is connected across A and $\mathrm{B}$ in both circuits, $Q_{2} < Q_{1}$.
questions.solution:
Case 1 : When $\mathrm{S}_1$ and $\mathrm{S}_2$ are open

$ \left(R_{e q}\right)_1=1+\frac{5 \times \frac{1}{2}}{5+\frac{1}{2}}=1+\frac{5}{11}=\frac{16}{11} $

$ P_1=\frac{V^2}{(R_{e q})_1}=\frac{(6)^2}{16} \times 11=\frac{36 \times 11}{16}=24.75 \mathrm{~W} $

$ \left(R_{e q}\right)_2=\frac{6}{11} \Omega $

$ P_2=\frac{V^2}{(R_{e q})_2}=\frac{(6)^2}{6} \times 11=\frac{36 \times 11}{6}=66 \mathrm{~W} $

$ P_2>P_1 $

Option (A) is correct.

Case 2 : If I = $2 \mathrm{~A}$ source is used in both circuits, then

$ P_1=i^2\left(R_{e q}\right)_1=(2)^2 \times \frac{16}{11}=\frac{64}{11}=5.818 \mathrm{~W} $

$ P_2=i^2\left(R_{e q}\right)_2=(2)^2 \times \frac{6}{11}=\frac{24}{11}=2.1818 \mathrm{~W} $

$ P_1>P_2 $

Option (B) is correct.

Case 3 : For $Q_1$

$$ \begin{aligned} & R_{e q}=\frac{5}{11} \Omega \\\\ & Q_1=\frac{V^2}{R_{e q}}=\frac{(6)^2}{\frac{5}{11}}=\frac{36 \times 11}{5}=79.2 \mathrm{~W} \\\\ & P_1=24.75 \mathrm{~W} \\\\ & Q_1>P_1 \end{aligned} $$

Option (C) is correct.

Case 4 : For option (D)

$$ \begin{aligned} & Q_1=i^2 R_{e q}=(2)^2 \times \frac{5}{11}=\frac{20}{11}=1.81 \mathrm{~W} \\\\ & Q_2=i^2 R_{e q}=(2)^2 \times \frac{1}{2}=\frac{4}{2}=2 \mathrm{~W} \\\\ & Q_2>Q_1 \end{aligned} $$

Option (D) is incorrect.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Two resistances $R_{1}=X \Omega$ and $R_{2}=1 \Omega$ are connected to a wire $A B$ of uniform resistivity, as shown in the figure. The radius of the wire varies linearly along its axis from $0.2 \mathrm{~mm}$ at $A$ to $1 \mathrm{~mm}$ at $B$. A galvanometer $(\mathrm{G})$ connected to the center of the wire, $50 \mathrm{~cm}$ from each end along its axis, shows zero deflection when $A$ and $B$ are connected to a battery. The value of $X$ is ____________.
questions.solution:
According to the given condition wire is having different cross-section at two junction. (Frustum shaped conducter)



$$ R=\rho \frac{I}{\pi a b} $$

For the shown conductor in the diagram.



$$ r=\frac{a+b}{2}=\frac{0.2+1}{2}=0.6 $$ .........(i)

As $$ \mathrm{R}=\frac{\rho l}{\mathrm{~A}} $$ ...........(ii)

Hence, Resistence of left $50 \mathrm{~cm}$ wire

$$ =\frac{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6}{\pi \times 0.2 \times 0.6} $$

Resistence of Right $50 \mathrm{~cm}$ wire

$$ =\frac{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6}{\pi \times 0.6 \times 1} $$

For wheatstone balanced condition

$ \frac{R_1}{P} =\frac{R_2}{Q} \quad\left(R_1=X\right) $

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $ \frac{(X) \times \pi \times 0.2 \times 0.6}{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6} =\frac{(1 \pi) \times 0.6 \times 1}{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6} $

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $ \frac{(X) \times \pi \times 0.12}{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6} =\frac{\pi \times 0.6 \times 1}{\rho \times 0.5 \times 10^6}$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $X =5$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: The reaction of $\mathrm{Xe}$ and $\mathrm{O}_{2}{F}_{2}$ gives a $\mathrm{Xe}$ compound $\mathbf{P}$. The number of moles of $\mathrm{HF}$ produced by the complete hydrolysis of $1 \mathrm{~mol}$ of $\mathbf{P}$ is __________.
questions.solution:
$$\mathrm{Xe}+2 \mathrm{O}_{2}{F}_{2} \rightarrow \underset{(\mathrm{P})}{\mathrm{XeF}_{4}}+2 \mathrm{O}_{2}$$

$$\underset{(\mathrm{P})}{\mathrm{6XeF}_{4}}+12 \mathrm{H}_{2} \mathrm{O} \longrightarrow 4 \mathrm{Xe}+2 \mathrm{XeO}_{3}+24 \mathrm{HF}+3 \mathrm{O}_{2}$$

So, from the above reaction, it is clear that 6 moles of $\mathrm{XeF}_4$ produces 24 moles of $\mathrm{HF}$.

So, 1 mole of $\mathrm{XeF}_4$ will produce $\frac{24}{6}$ moles of HF, i.e., 4 moles of $\mathrm{HF}$.

questions.question: Thermal decomposition of $\mathrm{AgNO}_{3}$ produces two paramagnetic gases. The total number of electrons present in the antibonding molecular orbitals of the gas that has the higher number of unpaired electrons is ____________.
questions.solution:
$$ 2 \mathrm{AgNO}_3(\mathrm{~s}) \stackrel{\Delta}{\longrightarrow} 2 \mathrm{Ag}(\mathrm{s})+2 \mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})+\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g}) $$

Both the $\mathrm{NO}_2$ and $\mathrm{O}_2$ gases are paramagnetic. $\mathrm{NO}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ has 1 unpaired electron and $\mathrm{O}_2(\mathrm{~g})$ has 2 unpaired electrons.

According to MOT,

$$\therefore\,\,\,\,$$ Molecular orbital configuration of O2 (16 electrons) is

$${\sigma _{1{s^2}}}\,\,\sigma _{1{s^2}}^ * \,$$ $${\sigma _{2{s^2}}}\,\,\sigma _{2{s^2}}^ * \,$$ $${\sigma _{2p_z^2}}\,\,{\pi _{2p_x^2}} = {\pi _{2p_y^2}}\,\,\pi _{2p_x^1}^ * \,\, = \pi _{2p_y^1}^ * $$

$$\therefore\,\,\,\,$$Na = Anti bonding electrons = 6

Nb = 10

Note :

Nb = Number of electrons in bonding molecular orbital

Na $$=$$ Number of electrons in anti bonding molecular orbital

(1) $$\,\,\,\,$$ upto 14 electrons, molecular orbital configuration is



Here Na = Anti bonding electrons $$=$$ 4 and Nb = 10

(2) $$\,\,\,\,$$ After 14 electrons to 20 electrons molecular orbital configuration is - - -



Here Na = 10

and Nb = 10

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Consider the equation

$$ \int_{1}^{e} \frac{\left(\log _{\mathrm{e}} x\right)^{1 / 2}}{x\left(a-\left(\log _{\mathrm{e}} x\right)^{3 / 2}\right)^{2}} d x=1, \quad a \in(-\infty, 0) \cup(1, \infty) $$

Which of the following statements is/are TRUE?
options: An integer $$a$$ satisfies the above equation
An irrational number $$a$$ satisfies the above equation
More than one $$a$$ satisfy the above equation
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\int_1^e {{{{{\left( {\log _e^x} \right)}^{1/2}}} \over {x{{\left( {a - {{\left( {\log _e^x} \right)}^{3/2}}} \right)}^2}}} = 1} $$

Let $$\log _e^x = t$$

$$ \Rightarrow x = {e^t}$$

$$ \Rightarrow dx = {e^t}dt$$

When $$x = 1$$ then $$t = \log _e^1 = 0$$

and when $$x = e$$ then $$t = \log _e^e = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow \int_0^1 {{{\sqrt t \,.\,{e^t}dt} \over {{e^t}{{(a - t\sqrt t )}^2}}} = 1} $$

$$ \Rightarrow \int_0^1 {{{\sqrt t \,dt} \over {{{(a - t\sqrt t )}^2}}} = 1} $$

Let $${t^{1/2}} = z$$

$$ \Rightarrow {1 \over {2\sqrt t }}dt = dz$$

$$ \Rightarrow dt = 2\sqrt t \,dz \Rightarrow dt = 2z\,dz$$

When $$t = 0$$, then $$z = 0$$

When $$t = 1$$, then $$z = {1^{1/2}} = 1$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\int_0^1 {{{z \times 2z\,dz} \over {{{(a - {z^3})}^2}}} = 1} $$

$$ \Rightarrow \int_0^1 {{{2{z^2}\,dz} \over {{{(a - {z^3})}^2}}} = 1} $$

Now let $$a - {z^3} = p$$

$$ \Rightarrow - 3{z^2}\,dz = dp$$

When $$z = 0$$ then $$p = a - 0 = a$$

When $$z = 1$$ then $$p = a - {1^3} = a - 1$$

$$\therefore$$ $$\int_a^{a - 1} {{{2\,.\,\left( { - {{dp} \over 3}} \right)} \over {{p^2}}} = 1} $$

$$ \Rightarrow - {2 \over 3}\int_a^{a - 1} {{{dp} \over {{p^2}}} = 1} $$

$$ \Rightarrow - {2 \over 3}\left[ { - {1 \over p}} \right]_a^{a - 1} = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow - {2 \over 3}\left[ { - {1 \over {a - 1}} + {1 \over a}} \right] = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow - {2 \over 3}\left[ {{{ - a + a - 1} \over {a(a - 1)}}} \right] = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow {2 \over 3}\left( {{1 \over {a(a - 1)}}} \right) = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow 3{a^2} - 3a = 2$$

$$ \Rightarrow 3{a^2} - 3a - 2 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow a = {{3\, \pm \,\sqrt {9 - 4(3)( - 2)} } \over {2\,.\,3}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $$a = {{3\, \pm \,\sqrt {33} } \over 6},\,{{3 - \sqrt {33} } \over 6}$$
