questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Let $$|M|$$ denote the determinant of a square matrix $$M$$. Let $$g:\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \rightarrow \mathbb{R}$$ be the function defined by

$$ g(\theta)=\sqrt{f(\theta)-1}+\sqrt{f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\theta\right)-1} $$

where

$$ f(\theta)=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & \sin \theta & 1 \\ -\sin \theta & 1 & \sin \theta \\ -1 & -\sin \theta & 1 \end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \sin \pi & \cos \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & -\cos \frac{\pi}{2} & \log _{e}\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right) \\ \cot \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \log _{e}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \pi \end{array}\right| . $$

Let $$p(x)$$ be a quadratic polynomial whose roots are the maximum and minimum values of the function $$g(\theta)$$, and $$p(2)=2-\sqrt{2}$$. Then, which of the following is/are TRUE ?
questions.solution:
Given,

$$ f(\theta)=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & \sin \theta & 1 \\ -\sin \theta & 1 & \sin \theta \\ -1 & -\sin \theta & 1 \end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc} \sin \pi & \cos \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & -\cos \frac{\pi}{2} & \log _{e}\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right) \\ \cot \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \log _{e}\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \pi \end{array}\right| $$

Here $\cos \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=-\sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$

and $\tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=-\cot \left(\theta+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$

and $\log _e\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right)=-\log _e\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)$

Also $\sin \pi=-\cos \frac{\pi}{2}=\tan \pi=0$

$$ \Rightarrow $$ $$ f(\theta)=\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & \sin \theta & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & \sin \theta \\ 0 & -\sin \theta & 1 \end{array}\right|+\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 0 & -\sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & \tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \\ \sin \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & 0 & \log _{\mathrm{e}}\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right) \\ -\tan \left(\theta-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) & -\log _{\mathrm{e}}\left(\frac{4}{\pi}\right) & 0 \end{array}\right| $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & f(\theta)=\left(1+\sin ^2 \theta\right)+0 \text { (skew symmetric) } \\\\ & g(\theta)=\sqrt{f(\theta)-1}+\sqrt{f\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\theta\right)-1} \\\\ & =|\sin \theta|+|\cos \theta| \quad \\\\ & g(\theta) \in[1, \sqrt{2}] \text { for } \theta \in\left[0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right] \end{aligned} $$

Again let $\mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x})=\mathrm{k}(\mathrm{x}-\sqrt{2})(\mathrm{x}-1)$

$$ \begin{aligned} & 2-\sqrt{2}=\mathrm{k}(2-\sqrt{2})(2-1) \\\\ & \Rightarrow \mathrm{k}=1 \quad(\mathrm{P}(2)=2-\sqrt{2} \text { given }) \\\\ & \therefore \mathrm{P}(\mathrm{x})=(\mathrm{x}-\sqrt{2})(\mathrm{x}-1) \end{aligned} $$

For option (A) $P\left(\frac{3+\sqrt{2}}{4}\right)<0$ Correct.

For option (B) $P\left(\frac{1+3 \sqrt{2}}{4}\right)<0$ Incorrect.

For option (C) $P\left(\frac{5 \sqrt{2}-1}{4}\right)>0$ Correct.

For option (D) $P\left(\frac{5-\sqrt{2}}{4}\right)>0$ Incorrect.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of the function y = [x], x ∈ (-1, 1) where [.] is the greatest integer function:

1. Its derivative is 0 at x = 0.5

2. It is continuous at x = 0

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Greatest Integer Function: (Floor function)

The function f(x) = [x] is known as the greatest integer function, which gives the greatest integer less than or equal to x, i.e., [x] ≤ x.

The domain of [x] is the set of all real numbers (R), and the range is the set of all integers (I).

Note:

A function is differentiable only if it is continuous.

The floor function, f(x) = ⌊x⌋, is differentiable in every open interval between integers, (n, n + 1), where n is any integer.

Calculation:

Given, y = [x]

Statement 1: Its derivative is 0 at x = 0.5

We know that the floor function is differentiable at all points except integer points.

Hence, y = [x] is differentiable at x = 0.5

⇒ y = [0.5] = 0

⇒ dy/dx = 0

Statement 2: It is continuous at x = 0

We know that y = [x] is continuous only in the open intervals between integers and discontinuous at all integer values.

Therefore, only statement 1 is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the domain of the function $f(x) =\sqrt{1 - (x-1)^2} \ ?$
questions.solution:
To determine the domain of the function $ f(x) = \sqrt{1 - (x-1)^2} $, let's analyze the expression inside the square root. For the square root function to be defined, the expression inside the square root must be non-negative:

$$ 1 - (x-1)^2 \geq 0 $$

Let's solve this inequality step by step:

  1. Rewrite the inequality:

    2. $$ 1 - (x-1)^2 \geq 0 $$

  2. Rearrange to isolate the squared term:

    3. $$ (x-1)^2 \leq 1 $$

  3. Take the square root of both sides. Remember, taking the square root of a squared expression gives absolute value:

    4. $$ |x-1| \leq 1 $$

  4. Rewrite the absolute value inequality as two separate inequalities:

    5. $$ -1 \leq x-1 \leq 1 $$

  5. Add 1 to all parts of the inequality:

    6. $$ 0 \leq x \leq 2 $$

Therefore, the domain of the function $ f(x) = \sqrt{1 - (x-1)^2} $ is the interval $[0, 2]$. So the correct option is:

Option D: [0, 2]

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If $4f(x) - f \left(\frac{1}{x}\right)=\left(2x+\frac{1}{x}\right)\left(2x-\frac{1}{x}\right),$ then what is f(2) equal to?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of $ f(2) $ from the given functional equation:

$$ 4f(x) - f \left( \frac{1}{x} \right) = \left( 2x + \frac{1}{x} \right) \left( 2x - \frac{1}{x} \right) $$

First, simplify the right side of the equation. The expression

$$ \left( 2x + \frac{1}{x} \right) \left( 2x - \frac{1}{x} \right) $$

is a difference of squares which can be simplified as follows:

$$ \left( 2x + \frac{1}{x} \right) \left( 2x - \frac{1}{x} \right) = (2x)^2 - \left( \frac{1}{x} \right)^2 = 4x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2} $$

Therefore, the functional equation can be rewritten as:

$$ 4f(x) - f \left( \frac{1}{x} \right) = 4x^2 - \frac{1}{x^2} $$

To find $ f(2) $, we consider the expressions for $ x = 2 $ and $ x = \frac{1}{2} $ and use the functional equation for both cases.

For $ x = 2 $:

$$ 4f(2) - f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) = 4(2)^2 - \frac{1}{(2)^2} = 16 - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{64}{4} - \frac{1}{4} = \frac{63}{4} $$

This simplifies to:

$$ 4f(2) - f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) = \frac{63}{4} $$

For $ x = \frac{1}{2} $:

$$ 4f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) - f(2) = 4 \left( \frac{1}{2} \right)^2 - \left( \frac{1}{ \frac{1}{2} } \right)^2 = 4 \left( \frac{1}{4} \right) - (2)^2 = 1 - 4 = -3 $$

This simplifies to:

$$ 4f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) - f(2) = -3 $$

Now, let's solve these two linear equations involving $ f(2) $ and $ f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) $:

1. $ 4f(2) - f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) = \frac{63}{4} $

2. $ 4f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) - f(2) = -3 $

From the second equation, solve for $ f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) $:

$$ 4f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) = f(2) - 3 $$

$$ f \left( \frac{1}{2} \right) = \frac{f(2) - 3}{4} $$

Substitute this expression into the first equation:

$$ 4f(2) - \frac{f(2) - 3}{4} = \frac{63}{4} $$

Clear the fraction by multiplying the entire equation by 4:

$$ 16f(2) - (f(2) - 3) = 63 $$

$$ 16f(2) - f(2) + 3 = 63 $$

$$ 15f(2) = 60 $$

$$ f(2) = \frac{60}{15} = 4 $$

Thus, the value of $ f(2) $ is 4. Hence, the correct option is:

Option D: 4

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If f(x) = 4x + 3, then what is f o f o f(-1) equal to?
questions.solution:
To solve for $ f \circ f \circ f(-1) $, we first need to evaluate each step sequentially. Given the function $ f(x) = 4x + 3 $, let's begin by finding $ f(-1) $.

1. Calculate $ f(-1) $:

$$ f(-1) = 4(-1) + 3 = -4 + 3 = -1 $$

So, $ f(-1) = -1 $.

2. Next, we need to find $ f(f(-1)) $. From the previous step, we know $ f(-1) = -1 $, so:

$$ f(f(-1)) = f(-1) = -1 $$

3. Finally, we need to find $ f(f(f(-1))) $. Again, since $ f(f(-1)) = -1 $, we get:

$$ f(f(f(-1))) = f(-1) = -1 $$

Therefore, $ f \circ f \circ f(-1) $ is equal to -1.

The correct option is:

Option A: -1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following in respect of the function f(x) = 10x :

1. Its domain is (-∞, ∞)

2. It is a continuous function

3. It is differentiable at x = 0

Which of the above statements are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the given function $$f(x) = 10^x$$ based on the statements provided.

1. The domain of the function $$f(x) = 10^x$$ is $$(-\infty, \infty)$$

This is correct because the exponential function $$10^x$$ is defined for all real numbers $$x$$.

2. It is a continuous function

This is also true because the exponential function $$10^x$$ is continuous for all real numbers. There are no breaks, jumps, or holes in its graph.

3. It is differentiable at $$x = 0$$

This statement is also correct. The function $$10^x$$ is differentiable at all points in its domain, including at $$x = 0$$. To determine differentiability at $$x=0$$, we need to check if the derivative exists at that point.

The derivative of $$f(x) = 10^x$$ is given by:

$$\frac{d}{dx}(10^x) = 10^x \ln(10)$$

Therefore, at $$x = 0$$:

$$f'(0) = 10^0 \ln(10) = 1 \cdot \ln(10) = \ln(10)$$

The derivative exists and has a finite value at $$x = 0$$, indicating that the function is differentiable at $$x = 0$$.

Therefore, all three statements are correct:

  • The domain is $$(-\infty, \infty)$$
  • The function is continuous
  • The function is differentiable at $$x = 0$$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option D: 1, 2 and 3

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: In which one of the following intervals is the function $f(x) = \frac{x^3}{3}-\frac{7x^2}{2} + 6x + 5$ decreasing?
questions.solution:
To determine where the function $ f(x) = \frac{x^3}{3} - \frac{7x^2}{2} + 6x + 5 $ is decreasing, we need to analyze its derivative. A function is decreasing where its derivative is negative.

First, let's find the derivative of $ f(x) $:

$$ f'(x) = \frac{d}{dx} \left( \frac{x^3}{3} - \frac{7x^2}{2} + 6x + 5 \right) $$

Calculating the derivative term by term:

$$ f'(x) = x^2 - 7x + 6 $$

Next, we find the critical points by setting the derivative equal to zero and solving for $ x $:

$$ x^2 - 7x + 6 = 0 $$

We can factor this quadratic equation:

$$ (x - 1)(x - 6) = 0 $$

So, the critical points are:

$$ x = 1 \quad \text{and} \quad x = 6 $$

To determine where $ f'(x) $ is negative, we analyze the sign of $ f'(x) $ around the critical points. We consider the intervals $ (-\infty, 1) $, $ (1, 6) $, and $ (6, \infty) $ and test points from each interval.

1. For $ x \in (-\infty, 1) $, choose $ x = 0 $:

$$ f'(0) = (0)^2 - 7(0) + 6 = 6 \quad (\text{Positive}) $$

2. For $ x \in (1, 6) $, choose $ x = 2 $:

$$ f'(2) = (2)^2 - 7(2) + 6 = 4 - 14 + 6 = -4 \quad (\text{Negative}) $$

3. For $ x \in (6, \infty) $, choose $ x = 7 $:

$$ f'(7) = (7)^2 - 7(7) + 6 = 49 - 49 + 6 = 6 \quad (\text{Positive}) $$

The function $ f(x) $ is decreasing where the derivative is negative, which is in the interval $ (1, 6) $.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B (1, 6)

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is $\displaystyle\sum_{x=1}^5$f(2x − 1) equal to ?
questions.solution:
To solve for the sum of $ \sum_{x=1}^5 f(2x - 1) $, we first need more information about the function $ f(x) $ given that it obeys the following properties:

1. $ f(x + y) = f(x) f(y) $ for all $ x, y \in \mathbb{N} $

2. $ f(1) = 2 $

Let's start by finding $ f(x) $ for some specific values of $ x $. We know that:

For $ x = y = 1 $:

$$f(2) = f(1+1) = f(1) \cdot f(1) = 2 \cdot 2 = 4$$

For $ x = 2, y = 1 $:

$$f(3) = f(2+1) = f(2) \cdot f(1) = 4 \cdot 2 = 8$$

For $ x = 2, y = 2 $:

$$f(4) = f(2+2) = f(2) \cdot f(2) = 4 \cdot 4 = 16$$

For $ x = 3, y = 1 $:

$$f(4) = f(3+1) = f(3) \cdot f(1) = 8 \cdot 2 = 16$$

For $ x = 4, y = 1 $:

$$f(5) = f(4+1) = f(4) \cdot f(1) = 16 \cdot 2 = 32$$

It seems the function $ f(x) $ is exponential, specifically:

$$f(x) = 2^x$$

To verify:

  • $ f(1) = 2^1 = 2 $

  • $ f(2) = 2^2 = 4 $

  • $ f(3) = 2^3 = 8 $

  • $ f(4) = 2^4 = 16 $

  • $ f(5) = 2^5 = 32 $

Now, we need to find the sum of $ \sum_{x=1}^5 f(2x - 1) $.

We calculate each term:

  • For $ x = 1 $, $ 2 \cdot 1 - 1 = 1 $ $ \Rightarrow f(1) = 2 $

  • For $ x = 2 $, $ 2 \cdot 2 - 1 = 3 $ $ \Rightarrow f(3) = 8 $

  • For $ x = 3 $, $ 2 \cdot 3 - 1 = 5 $ $ \Rightarrow f(5) = 32 $

  • For $ x = 4 $, $ 2 \cdot 4 - 1 = 7 $ $ \Rightarrow f(7) = 2^7 = 128 $

  • For $ x = 5 $, $ 2 \cdot 5 - 1 = 9 $ $ \Rightarrow f(9) = 2^9 = 512 $

Summing these:

$$ \sum_{x=1}^5 f(2x - 1) = 2 + 8 + 32 + 128 + 512 = 682 $$

Therefore, the sum $ \sum_{x=1}^5 f(2x - 1) $ equals 682, which corresponds to option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is $\displaystyle\sum_{x=1}^6$2x f(x) equal to ?
questions.solution:
Given the function $ f(x) $ that satisfies the functional equation:

$$f(x + y) = f(x) f(y) \quad \text{for all} \; x, y \in \mathbb{N}$$

With the initial condition:

$$f(1) = 2$$

We can use this functional equation to find values for $ f(x) $. By induction or direct calculation:

For $ x = 2 $, let $ y = 1 $:

$$f(2) = f(1+1) = f(1)f(1) = 2 \cdot 2 = 4$$

For $ x = 3 $, let $ y = 2 $ and $ x = 1 $:

$$f(3) = f(2+1) = f(2)f(1) = 4 \cdot 2 = 8$$

Continuing this pattern, we get:

$$f(4) = f(3+1) = f(3)f(1) = 8 \cdot 2 = 16$$

$$f(5) = f(4+1) = f(4)f(1) = 16 \cdot 2 = 32$$

$$f(6) = f(5+1) = f(5)f(1) = 32 \cdot 2 = 64$$

Now, we need to find the sum:

$$\displaystyle\sum_{x=1}^6 2^x f(x)$$

Substitute the value of $ f(x) $ we calculated:

$$ 2^1 \cdot f(1) + 2^2 \cdot f(2) + 2^3 \cdot f(3) + 2^4 \cdot f(4) + 2^5 \cdot f(5) + 2^6 \cdot f(6)$$

$$= 2^1 \cdot 2 + 2^2 \cdot 4 + 2^3 \cdot 8 + 2^4 \cdot 16 + 2^5 \cdot 32 + 2^6 \cdot 64$$

$$= 2 \cdot 2 + 4 \cdot 4 + 8 \cdot 8 + 16 \cdot 16 + 32 \cdot 32 + 64 \cdot 64$$

$$= 4 + 16 + 64 + 256 + 1024 + 4096$$

$$= 4 + 16 + 64 + 256 + 1024 + 4096$$

$$= 1365 + 4096$$

$$= 5460$$

So, the sum is:

Option D: 5460

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: ​If f(x) = 4x + 1 and g(x) = kx + 2 such that fog(x) = gof(x), then what is the value of k ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $ k $ such that $ f(g(x)) = g(f(x)) $, we need to equate the compositions $ f \circ g $ and $ g \circ f $ of the functions $ f(x) $ and $ g(x) $.

Given:

$ f(x) = 4x + 1 $

$ g(x) = kx + 2 $

Let's find $ f(g(x)) $:

$ f(g(x)) = f(kx + 2) $

Substitute $ g(x) = kx + 2 $ into $ f(x) $:

$ f(g(x)) = 4(kx + 2) + 1 $

$ f(g(x)) = 4kx + 8 + 1 $

$ f(g(x)) = 4kx + 9 $

Next, let's find $ g(f(x)) $:

$ g(f(x)) = g(4x + 1) $

Substitute $ f(x) = 4x + 1 $ into $ g(x) $:

$ g(f(x)) = k(4x + 1) + 2 $

$ g(f(x)) = 4kx + k + 2 $

Now, equate $ f(g(x)) $ and $ g(f(x)) $:

$ 4kx + 9 = 4kx + k + 2 $

Simplify the equation:

$ 9 = k + 2 $

$ k = 7 $

Therefore, the value of $ k $ is 7. The correct option is:

Option A

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements in respect of the function f(x) = $\left\{\begin{array}{rc}|x|+1, & 0<|x| \leqslant 3 \\ 1, & x = 0\end{array}\right.$

1. The function attains maximum value only at x = 3

2. The function attains local minimum only at x = 0

Which of the statements given above is/are correct ?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze the function f(x) graphically:

For 0 < |x| ≤ 3, we get:

x ∈ [-3, 0) ∪ (0, 3]

From the graph, we observe that the maximum value occurs at both x = 3 and x = -3, not exclusively at x = 3.

Therefore, statement 1 is incorrect.

By examining the graph further, it is evident that the minimum value is at x = 0.

Specifically, when x = 0, f(x) = 1, which indicates that x = 0 is where the minimum value of the function occurs.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which of the following Indian Naval Ships were decommissioned in June 2022?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is INS Nishank and INS Akshay.

  • The Indian Navy bid farewell to its two ships, INS Nishank and INS Akshay, at Naval Dockyard in Mumbai on 3 June 2022.
  • Both ships were decommissioned at Naval Dockyard after 32 years of service.
  • Nishank, the fourth of the Veer-class missile corvettes, was an integral part of the "Killer Squadron", renowned for its heroics in the 1971 war.

Additional Information

  • INS Gomati was decommissioned at the Naval Dockyard in Mumbai on 28 May 2022.
  • INS Gomati derives her name from the river Gomti and was commissioned on 16 April 1988.
  • It was the oldest warrior of the Western Fleet at the time of its decommissioning.
  • The ship will be dismantled and transported to Lucknow, where it will be installed as the "Gomati Shaurya Smarak," a museum celebrating Gomati’s service career.
  • For its contribution to national maritime security, INS Gomati was twice awarded the coveted Unit Citation, once in 2007-08 and again in 2019-20.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: India signed a deal with which one of the following countries to supply MH-60R helicopters to the Indian Navy?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D, USA.

India signed a deal with the United States to supply MH-60R helicopters to the Indian Navy. This deal was finalized in 2016 and involved the purchase of 24 MH-60R Seahawk multi-role helicopters.

The MH-60R is a highly capable helicopter designed for anti-submarine warfare (ASW), anti-surface warfare (ASuW), search and rescue (SAR), and other maritime missions. It is equipped with advanced sensors, weapons systems, and communication capabilities, making it a valuable asset for the Indian Navy.

The purchase of these helicopters is part of India's ongoing efforts to modernize its naval capabilities and enhance its maritime security posture.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: With reference to India’s defence, the terms ‘Surat’ and ‘Udaygiri’ refer to
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: warships.

Here's why:

  • INS Surat and INS Udaygiri are the names of Indian Navy warships.

  • INS Surat is a Visakhapatnam-class destroyer, known for its advanced weaponry and stealth capabilities.

  • INS Udaygiri is a Nilgiri-class frigate, designed for anti-submarine warfare and surface warfare.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Coast guard patrol boats: While crucial for coastal security, they are smaller and less heavily armed than destroyers or frigates.

  • Cargo helicopters: These are primarily used for transport and logistics, not combat.

  • Maritime patrol aircrafts: These aircraft play a vital role in surveillance and reconnaissance but are not warships.

options: Puducherry and Dadra and Nagar Haveli
Puducherry and Andaman and Nicobar Islands
Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.

Here's why:

On 26th January 2020, the Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu (Merger of Union Territories) Act, 2019 came into effect. This act merged the two separate union territories of Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu into a single union territory known as Dadra and Nagar Haveli and Daman and Diu.

The other options are incorrect because no other Union Territories were merged in January 2020.

questions.question: While watching news, you hear about a place, of which you had never heard earlier. You want to know more about the place and you want to locate it on the map. Which of the following is/are required for you to be able to locate the place on the map?
options: Both longitude and latitude of the place
Latitude, longitude and altitude of the place
questions.solution:
To locate a place on a map, you need information that uniquely identifies its position on the Earth's surface. Here’s an explanation of what each of the given options entails:

Latitude: This is a measure of how far north or south a place is from the Equator. While latitude gives you the north-south positioning, it does not provide any information about the east-west location.

Longitude: This is a measure of how far east or west a place is from the Prime Meridian. Like latitude, longitude alone does not give a complete picture as it lacks the north-south information.

Both Longitude and Latitude: Longitude and latitude together form a coordinate system that can uniquely identify any location on the Earth's surface. This is the most commonly used method for mapping and navigation.

Altitude: This refers to the height above sea level. While important for certain applications, such as aviation or hiking, it is not necessary to locate a place on a standard map, which is focused on horizontal positioning rather than vertical.

Given the need to find a specific location on the Earth's surface, you require both longitude and latitude coordinates. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C: Both longitude and latitude of the place

Using both of these coordinates, you can pinpoint the location accurately on any standard map:

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which one of the following statements about metamorphic rocks is not correct?
options: Due to segregation of minerals into wavy bands or platy surfaces, some metamorphic rocks develop foliations.
Where the foliations develop into broad mineral bands, the metamorphic rock is extremely hard.
Where the foliations are moderately thin, the metamorphic rock tends to flake apart.
Rocks that originally were composed of one dominant mineral are often foliated by metamorphism.
questions.solution:
• Metamorphic rocks are rocks that have undergone changes in mineralogy, texture, and/or chemical composition due to changes in temperature and pressure.
    • The original rock might have been an igneous, sedimentary, or metamorphic rock.
  • The pressure and heat driving metamorphism result from three forces:
    • The Earth's internal heat.
    • The weight of the overlying rock.
    • Horizontal or tectonic forces causing rock deformation.
  • Deformation of some rocks, like shale and clay-rich sandstone (greywacke), leads to textural changes resulting in the formation of flat or wavy parallel planes within the metamorphosed rock. These are known as foliation.
  • Foliation forms when pressure squeezes flat or elongate minerals within a rock, aligning them.
  • These rocks develop a platy or sheet-like structure reflecting the direction of applied pressure. Therefore, Statement 1 is correct.
  • Where foliation develops into broad mineral bands, the metamorphic rock is extremely hard. Thus, Statement 2 is correct.
  • Rocks originally composed of one dominant mineral are often foliated by metamorphism.
    • Surviving minerals in the original rock may rotate during deformation, aligning with platy minerals. Thus, Statement 4 is correct.
  • The mineral crystals do not need to be large to produce foliation. For instance, slate is characterized by aligned mica flakes too small to see. Therefore, Statement 3 is not correct.

Important Points

  • Foliation mainly occurs in rocks containing platy minerals like mica and chlorite.
  • These minerals typically form when shale and clay-bearing sandstone (greywacke) are metamorphosed.
  • As platy minerals grow, their planes develop a preferred orientation, typically perpendicular to the main compressive forces.
  • Surviving minerals in the original rock may rotate to align with these platy minerals during deformation.
  • The three types of foliation are slate, schist, and gneiss.
    • Slate: low-grade, thin-layered, very small mineral grains.
    • Schist: medium-grade, thin-layered, large mineral grains.
    • Gneiss: high-grade, thick-layered, very large mineral grains.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which one of the following ‘discontinuities’ separates the Earth’s crust from the mantle?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Mohorovicic.

Here's why:

  • Mohorovicic Discontinuity (Moho): This is the boundary between the Earth's crust and the mantle. It's characterized by a significant increase in seismic wave velocities, indicating a change in rock composition and density.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Gutenberg Discontinuity: This discontinuity separates the Earth's mantle from the outer core. It's marked by a sharp decrease in the velocity of P-waves and the disappearance of S-waves, suggesting a change from solid (mantle) to liquid (outer core).

  • Conrad Discontinuity: This is a relatively minor discontinuity observed within the continental crust, separating the upper crust from the lower crust. It's not always present and is less pronounced than the Moho.

  • Repetti Discontinuity: This discontinuity is believed to mark the boundary between the upper mantle and the transition zone within the mantle.

Therefore, the Mohorovicic Discontinuity is the primary boundary that separates the Earth's crust from the mantle.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which one of the following cities of the world would represent the greatest linear velocity of rotation of the Earth?
questions.solution:
The linear velocity of any point on the Earth's surface due to its rotation depends on its distance from the Earth's axis of rotation, which is essentially related to the latitude of the location. Points on the equator experience the greatest linear velocity because they are farthest from the axis of rotation, whereas points closer to the poles have a much smaller linear velocity.

To determine which of the given cities has the greatest linear velocity, we need to identify their latitudes. Here's a brief overview:

Option A: Kampala, Uganda - Latitude: 0.3° N
Option B: St. Petersburg, Russia - Latitude: 59.9° N
Option C: Madrid, Spain - Latitude: 40.4° N
Option D: Stockholm, Sweden - Latitude: 59.3° N

Since Kampala, Uganda, is closest to the equator among these options, it would have the greatest linear velocity of rotation of the Earth. Here is a summary in table format with their respective latitudes:

City Latitude
Kampala, Uganda 0.3° N
St. Petersburg, Russia 59.9° N
Madrid, Spain 40.4° N
Stockholm, Sweden 59.3° N

Therefore, the city that represents the greatest linear velocity of rotation of the Earth is Kampala, Uganda (Option A).

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: What will happen if you are flying east across the International Date Line?
questions.solution:
The International Date Line (IDL) serves as an imaginary boundary that runs from the North Pole to the South Pole and is primarily located along the 180° longitude line. When you cross this line, the date changes, but not the time of day.

If you are flying east across the International Date Line:

  • Instead of jumping forward or backward by 12 hours, you will experience a change of one complete day (24 hours).

Therefore, you will "lose" one day. For example, if it is Monday just before you cross the IDL heading east, it will be Sunday just after you cross it.

This corresponds to the following option:

  • Option D: You will lose 24 hours

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: The Prime Meridian does not pass through which one of the following African countries?
questions.solution:
The Prime Meridian is the meridian (line of longitude) at zero degrees, from which east and west are determined. It is internationally recognized and passes through several countries, including some in Africa. To determine which of the given countries the Prime Meridian does not pass through, let's examine each option:

Option A: Morocco

Morocco is located in Northwestern Africa, away from the longitudinal line of the Prime Meridian.

Option B: Algeria

Algeria is situated further to the east of Morocco but still does not lie on the Prime Meridian.

Option C: Mali

Mali is one of the countries in Africa through which the Prime Meridian actually passes.

Option D: Ghana

Ghana is located on the Prime Meridian, which passes through its town of Tema.

Based on this information, the Prime Meridian does not pass through Option A: Morocco.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Piecing together the puzzle of geologic time to create and analyze historical maps of the Earth is known as
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: paleogeography. Here's why:

Paleogeography is the study of the Earth's geography in the past. It involves reconstructing the positions of continents, oceans, and other geographic features over millions of years. This field relies heavily on understanding geological processes, such as plate tectonics, erosion, and sedimentation, to piece together the Earth's history.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Paleoclimatology: This branch of geology focuses on studying past climates. It examines evidence like ice cores, tree rings, and sediment layers to understand past temperature, precipitation, and other climatic conditions.
  • Paleogeomorphology: This field studies the evolution of landforms over time. It analyzes how mountains, valleys, and other features have been shaped by geological processes such as erosion, uplift, and deposition.
  • Paleolithology: This term is not a standard geological term. It might be a misunderstanding of "paleontology," which is the study of prehistoric life through fossils.

In summary, paleogeography is the specific field that focuses on reconstructing the Earth's geographical features throughout history, making it the correct answer to the question.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements on computation of density :

1. Physiological density can be computed by dividing the total population by the net cultivable area.

2. Agricultural density can be computed by dividing the total agricultural population by the net cultivable area.

3. Population density can be computed by dividing the total population by the total area.

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To determine how many of the given statements about the computation of different types of density are correct, let's analyze each statement:

Statement 1: Physiological density can be computed by dividing the total population by the net cultivable area.

This statement is correct. Physiological density is a measure that calculates the number of people per unit area of arable land.

Statement 2: Agricultural density can be computed by dividing the total agricultural population by the net cultivable area.

This statement is also correct. Agricultural density measures the number of farmers specifically per unit area of arable land.

Statement 3: Population density can be computed by dividing the total population by the total area.

This statement is correct. Population density usually refers to the number of people living per unit area of land, typically using the total land area.

All three statements are correct. Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C: 3

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: The formation of ‘tors’ on small rocky hills is associated with which among the following?
questions.solution:
Tors are isolated rock outcrops that arise from the surrounding smooth and gentle slopes of a hillside or hill. These formations are particularly associated with granite due to its distinct physical and chemical properties.

Granite is an igneous rock that tends to form large, solid masses. Over time, weathering processes, including freeze-thaw weathering, salt crystal growth, and chemical weathering, can cause the joints and fractures within the granite to expand and lead to the formation of distinct rock outcrops known as tors.

Therefore, the formation of ‘tors’ on small rocky hills is most closely associated with:

Option A: Granite

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements about anticyclones :

1. Anticyclones are high pressure systems.

2. Air in the centre of the system must be subsiding.

3. Anticyclones are characterized by converging winds.

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To determine how many of the given statements about anticyclones are correct, let's analyze each one individually:

Statement 1: Anticyclones are high pressure systems.

This statement is correct. Anticyclones are indeed high-pressure systems where the atmospheric pressure at the surface of the planet is higher than its surrounding environment.

Statement 2: Air in the centre of the system must be subsiding.

This statement is also correct. In an anticyclone, air typically descends or subsides in the center. This descending air suppresses cloud formation, often leading to clear skies.

Statement 3: Anticyclones are characterized by converging winds.

This statement is incorrect. Anticyclones are characterized by diverging winds. In the Northern Hemisphere, winds rotate clockwise around an anticyclone, and in the Southern Hemisphere, they rotate counterclockwise. This rotation causes the winds to diverge outward from the center of the high-pressure system.

Based on the analysis, two out of the three given statements are correct.

Correct Option:

Option B

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Which of the following statements is/are correct?

1. The Earth’s crust is brittle in nature.

2. The mean thickness of the oceanic crust is 15 km, whereas that of the continental crust is around 30 km.

Select the correct answer using the code given below.
questions.solution:
To determine which of the statements are correct, let's evaluate each of them individually:

Statement 1: The Earth’s crust is brittle in nature.

The Earth's crust is indeed brittle in nature. This brittleness is why it is prone to fracturing and faulting, leading to phenomena such as earthquakes. Therefore, Statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: The mean thickness of the oceanic crust is 15 km, whereas that of the continental crust is around 30 km.

The mean thickness of the oceanic crust is generally around 5-10 km, not 15 km, while the mean thickness of the continental crust is usually around 30-50 km. Therefore, Statement 2 is incorrect.

Based on this analysis, the correct answer is:

Option A
1 only

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements :

1. Maharashtra State has the maximum share of national highways (length in kilometres).

2. The nature of terrain and the level of economic development is one of the reasons for the maximum share of national highways in Maharashtra.

Which of the statements given above is/are correct?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Both 1 and 2.

National Highways:

  • National Highways constitute 2.09% of the total road network in the country.
  • Road transport is the dominant segment in India’s transport sector and has contributed 3.06% to GVA.
  • National Highways carry over 40% of the total traffic across the length and breadth of the country.
  • Maharashtra has the largest network of National Highways with 17,757 km (13.4%) followed by Uttar Pradesh and Rajasthan with 11,737 km (8.9%) and 10,342 km (7.8%) respectively. Hence, statement 1 is correct.

Reasons for Maharashtra having the longest share of National Highways:

  • Nature of terrain: A large part of Maharashtra constitutes a plateau.
    • The construction and development of road networks are easier in this region.
  • Economic Development: Maharashtra is India's second most industrialized state contributing 20% of national industrial output.
    • Mumbai, Pune, Chhatrapati Sambhaji Nagar (Aurangabad), Nagpur, etc., are major industrial areas. The state has a well-developed social, physical, and industrial infrastructure.
    • It is also home to one of India’s most developed automobiles and auto components sectors, as Maharashtra accounts for almost 35.1% of India’s automobile output by value. Hence, statement 2 is correct.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements :

1. The Sharavati river creates Jog Falls.

2. The Brahmani river is formed by the confluence of the Kosi and Sankh rivers.

3. The Tamraparni is a river of Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu.

How many of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
Let's analyze each of the statements to determine their correctness:

Statement 1: The Sharavati river creates Jog Falls.

This statement is correct. The Sharavati river is known for creating the Jog Falls, which is one of the highest waterfalls in India.

Statement 2: The Brahmani river is formed by the confluence of the Kosi and Sankh rivers.

This statement is incorrect. The Brahmani river is actually formed by the confluence of the Sankh and South Koel rivers, not the Kosi river.

Statement 3: The Tamraparni is a river of Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu.

This statement is correct. The Tamraparni river flows through the Tirunelveli district of Tamil Nadu.

Based on the analysis above, two out of the three statements are correct.

The correct answer is Option B: 2.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online

Three plane mirrors form an equilateral triangle with each side of length $L$. There is a small hole at a distance $l>0$ from one of the corners as shown in the figure. A ray of light is passed through the hole at an angle $\theta$ and can only come out through the same hole. The cross section of the mirror configuration and the ray of light lie on the same plane.

Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The ray of light will come out for $\theta=30^{\circ}$, for $0< l < L$.
There is an angle for $l=\frac{L}{2}$ at which the ray of light will come out after two reflections.
The ray of light will NEVER come out for $\theta=60^{\circ}$, and $l=\frac{L}{3}$.
The ray of light will come out for $\theta=60^{\circ}$, and $0 < l < \frac{L}{2}$ after six reflections.
questions.solution:

$$\Rightarrow$$ (A) is correct

Considering the symmetry of the situation, we can have :

$$\Rightarrow$$ (B) is correct

As is clear from the above diagram, ray comes out.

$$\Rightarrow$$ (C) is not correct

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: A rod of length $2 \mathrm{~cm}$ makes an angle $\frac{2 \pi}{3} \mathrm{rad}$ with the principal axis of a thin convex lens. The lens has a focal length of $10 \mathrm{~cm}$ and is placed at a distance of $\frac{40}{3} \mathrm{~cm}$ from the object as shown in the figure. The height of the image is $\frac{30 \sqrt{3}}{13} \mathrm{~cm}$ and the angle made by it with respect to the principal axis is $\alpha$ rad. The value of $\alpha$ is $\frac{\pi}{n} r a d$, where $n$ is __________ .
questions.solution:
$\frac{\mathrm{h}_{\mathrm{i}}}{\mathrm{h}_0}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{\mathrm{u}} \Rightarrow \frac{-\frac{30 \sqrt{3}}{13}}{\sqrt{3}}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{-\frac{43}{3}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}_1=\frac{430}{13} \mathrm{~cm}$

Now using,

$ \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{u}}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{f}} \Rightarrow \frac{1}{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{1}{10}-\frac{3}{40} \Rightarrow \mathrm{v}=40 \mathrm{~cm}$

$$ \therefore $$ $ \mathrm{x}=40-\frac{430}{13}=\frac{90}{13} \mathrm{~cm}$

$\tan \alpha=\frac{\frac{30 \sqrt{3}}{\frac{90}{13}}}{\mathrm{~N}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \Rightarrow \alpha=30^{\circ}=\frac{\pi}{6}$

$$ \therefore $$ N = 6

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: Consider a configuration of $n$ identical units, each consisting of three layers. The first layer is a column of air of height $h=\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~cm}$, and the second and third layers are of equal thickness $d=$ $\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2} \mathrm{~cm}$, and refractive indices $\mu_{1}=\sqrt{\frac{3}{2}}$ and $\mu_{2}=\sqrt{3}$, respectively. A light source 0 is placed on the top of the first unit, as shown in the figure. A ray of light from 0 is incident on the second layer of the first unit at an angle of $\theta=60^{\circ}$ to the normal. For a specific value of $n$, the ray of light emerges from the bottom of the configuration at a distance $l=\frac{8}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~cm}$, as shown in the figure. The value of $n$ is ________.
questions.solution:
$$ \begin{aligned} &x_1 =\frac{1}{3} \times \tan 60 \\\\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~cm} \end{aligned} $$

By Snell's law

$$ \begin{aligned} &1 \times \sin 60 =\frac{\sqrt{3}}{\sqrt{2}} \sin \theta_2 \\\\ & \Rightarrow \theta_2 =45^ \mathrm{o} \\\\ &\therefore x_2 =d \end{aligned} $$

Again, by applying Snell's law

$$ \begin{aligned} &\sqrt{\frac{3}{2} \times \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}} =\sqrt{3} \times \sin \theta_3 \\\\ &\Rightarrow \theta_2 =30^{\circ} \\\\ & \therefore x_3 =\frac{d}{\sqrt{3}} \end{aligned} $$

Also
$$ \begin{aligned} & x_1+x_2+x_3 =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}+d+\frac{d}{\sqrt{3}} \\\\ & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}+\left(\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2}\right)+\frac{\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{3}} \\\\ & =\frac{2+(3-\sqrt{3})+\sqrt{3}-1}{2 \sqrt{3}} \\\\ & =\frac{4}{2 \sqrt{3}} \\\\ & x_1+x_2+x_3 =\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~cm} \\\\ & \text {And,}~~ \eta =\frac{l}{x_1+x_2+x_3} \\\\ &\eta =\frac{8 / \sqrt{3}}{2 / \sqrt{3}}=4 \\\\ &\eta =4 \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: An object and a concave mirror of focal length $f=10 \mathrm{~cm}$ both move along the principal axis of the mirror with constant speeds. The object moves with speed $V_{0}=15 \mathrm{~cm} \mathrm{~s}^{-1}$ towards the mirror with respect to a laboratory frame. The distance between the object and the mirror at a given moment is denoted by $u$. When $u=30 \mathrm{~cm}$, the speed of the mirror $V_{m}$ is such that the image is instantaneously at rest with respect to the laboratory frame, and the object forms a real image. The magnitude of $V_{m}$ is _________ $\mathrm{cm} \,\mathrm{s}^{-1}$.
questions.solution:
Magnification,

$$ \begin{aligned} & m =\frac{f}{u-f} \\\\ & =\frac{10}{30-10}=\frac{1}{2} \end{aligned} $$

Also $\quad m^2=\frac{v_m}{v_0-v_m}$ [where, $v_m=$ speed of mirror]

$$ \begin{aligned} & m^2\left(v_{\mathrm{o}}-v_{\mathrm{m}}\right) =v_m \\\\ & \Rightarrow \left(v_0-v_m\right) \times \frac{1}{4} =v_{\mathrm{m}} \\\\ & \Rightarrow v_0 =5 v_m \\\\ & \Rightarrow v_m =\frac{v_0}{5}=\frac{15}{5} \\\\ & \Rightarrow v_m =3 \mathrm{~cm} / \mathrm{s} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: Then her face was bowed.
options: Then she was being bowed her face.
Her face was bowed by them.
Then her face has been bowed.
questions.solution:
Option B : Her face was bowed by them.

The given sentence, "Then her face was bowed," is in the passive voice. The subject of the sentence (her face) is receiving the action (being bowed), and the doer of the action is not explicitly mentioned.

Option B, "Her face was bowed by them," provides the same meaning in the passive voice. It indicates that her face was bowed, and it specifies that the action was performed by "them" (the doer of the action).

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: What will be the acceleration due to gravity at a depth d, where g is acceleration due to gravity on the surface of earth?
questions.solution:
Acceleration due to gravity at the surface of earth,

$$g = {{GM} \over {{R^2}}} = {4 \over 3}\pi \rho GR$$ ...... (i)

[$$\because$$ $$M = {4 \over 3}\pi {R^3}\,.\rho $$]

Where, $$\rho$$ = density of earth.

Acceleration due to gravity at depth d from the surface of earth,

$$g' = {4 \over 3}\pi \rho G(R - d)$$ ...... (ii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get,

$$g' = g\left[ {1 - {d \over R}} \right]$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question: Two spherical stars $A$ and $B$ have densities $\rho_{A}$ and $\rho_{B}$, respectively. $A$ and $B$ have the same radius, and their masses $M_{A}$ and $M_{B}$ are related by $M_{B}=2 M_{A}$. Due to an interaction process, star $A$ loses some of its mass, so that its radius is halved, while its spherical shape is retained, and its density remains $\rho_{A}$. The entire mass lost by $A$ is deposited as a thick spherical shell on $B$ with the density of the shell being $\rho_{A}$. If $v_{A}$ and $v_{B}$ are the escape velocities from $A$ and $B$ after the interaction process, the ratio $\frac{v_{B}}{v_{A}}=\sqrt{\frac{10 n}{15^{1 / 3}}}$. The value of $n$ is __________ .
questions.solution:
Due to an interaction process, star A losses some of it's mass and radius becomes $${R \over 2}$$. Let new mass of star A is M'A. Here in both cases density of star A remains same $${\rho _A}$$.

$$\therefore$$ Initially $$({\rho _A}) = {{{M_A}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi {R^3}}}$$

Finally $$({\rho _A}) = {{M{'_A}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi {{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^3}}}$$

Density remains same,

$$\therefore$$ $${{{M_A}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi {R^3}}} = {{M{'_A}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi {{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^3}}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 8M{'_A} = {M_A}$$

$$ \Rightarrow M{'_A} = {{{M_A}} \over 8}$$

$$\therefore$$ Lost mass by $$A = {M_A} - M{'_A} = {M_A} - {{{M_A}} \over 8} = {{7{M_A}} \over 8}$$

This lost mass $${{7{M_A}} \over 8}$$ is attached on the star B and density of the attached mass stay $${\rho _A}$$. So new radius of star B is $${R_2}$$.

Density of the removed part from star A is,

$${\rho _A} = {{{{7{M_A}} \over 8}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi \left( {{R^3} - {{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^3}} \right)}}$$

Density of the added part in star B stay's same as $${\rho _A}$$,

$$\therefore$$ $${\rho _A} = {{{{7{M_A}} \over 8}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi \left( {R_2^3 - {R^3}} \right)}}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{{{7{M_A}} \over 8}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi \left( {{R^3} - {{\left( {{R \over 2}} \right)}^3}} \right)}} = {{{{7{M_A}} \over 8}} \over {{4 \over 3}\pi \left( {R_2^3 - {R^3}} \right)}}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {R^3} - {{{R^3}} \over 8} = R_2^3 - {R^3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R_2^3 = 2{R^3} - {{{R^3}} \over 8}$$

$$ \Rightarrow R_2^3 = {{15{R^3}} \over 8}$$

$$ \Rightarrow {R_2} = {(15)^{{1 \over 3}}} \times {R \over 2}$$

Escape velocity from star A after interaction process,

$${V_A} = \sqrt {{{2G\left( {{{{M_A}} \over 8}} \right)} \over {{R \over 2}}}} $$

And escape velocity from star B after interaction process,

$${V_B} = \sqrt {{{2G\left( {{{23{M_A}} \over 8}} \right)} \over {{{(15)}^{{1 \over 3}}} \times {R \over 2}}}} $$

$$\therefore$$ $${{{V_B}} \over {{V_A}}} = \sqrt {{{{{23{M_A}} \over 8}} \over {{{(15)}^{{1 \over 3}}} \times {R \over 2}}} \times {{{R \over 2}} \over {{{{M_A}} \over 8}}}} $$

$$ = \sqrt {{{23} \over {{{(15)}^{{1 \over 3}}}}}} $$ ..... (1)

Given,

$${{{V_B}} \over {{V_A}}} = \sqrt {{{10n} \over {{{(15)}^{{1 \over 3}}}}}} $$ ..... (2)

Comparing equation (1) and (2), we get,

$$10n = 23$$

$$ \Rightarrow n = 2.3$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: A monoatomic gas of molar mass m is kept in a insulated container. Container is moving with velocity v. If the container is suddenly stopped, then the change in the temperature of the gas is
questions.solution:
Let the insulated container has n moles of the monoatomic gas. The loss of kinetic energy of the gas,

$$\Delta {E_K} = {1 \over 2}(mn){v^2}$$

If the change in temperature of the gas is $$\Delta$$T, then heat gained by the gas is,

$$\Delta Q = {3 \over 2}nR\,\Delta T$$

Now, according to question,

$$\Delta Q = \Delta {E_K}$$

$${3 \over 2}nR\,\Delta T = {1 \over 2}mn{v^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \Delta T = {{m{v^2}} \over {3R}}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: One mole of an ideal diatomic gas undergoes a process as shown in the figure. The molar specific heat of the gas in the process is
questions.solution:
In the curve, V $$\propto$$ $$\frac{1}{T}$$

$$\Rightarrow$$ V = m$$\frac{1}{T}$$ where, m = slope of curve.

$$\Rightarrow$$ VT = constant

$$\therefore$$ TdV + VdT = 0 ..... (i)

$$\Rightarrow$$ dV = $$-\frac{V}{T}$$dT

As, Q = $$\Delta$$U + W

CdT = CVdT + pdV ..... (ii)

CdT = CVdT $$-$$ $$\frac{p}{T}$$ VdT

$$\Rightarrow$$ C = CV $$-$$ $$\frac{pV}{T}$$

= CV $$-$$ R ($$\because$$ $$\frac{pV}{T}$$ = R)

For diatomic gas, CV = $$\frac{5}{2}$$R

$$\therefore$$ C = CV $$-$$ R = $$\frac{5}{2}$$R $$-$$ R = $$\frac{3R}{2}$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
List I describes thermodynamic processes in four different systems. List II gives the magnitudes (either exactly or as a close approximation) of possible changes in the internal energy of the system due to the process.

List-I List-II
(I) $10^{-3} \mathrm{~kg}$ of water at $100^{\circ} \mathrm{C}$ is converted to steam at the same temperature, at a pressure of $10^{5} \mathrm{~Pa}$. The volume of the system changes from $10^{-6} \mathrm{~m}^{3}$ to $10^{-3} \mathrm{~m}^{3}$ in the process. Latent heat of water $=2250\, \mathrm{~kJ} / \mathrm{kg}$.
 (P) $2 \mathrm{~kJ}$
(II) $0.2$ moles of a rigid diatomic ideal gas with volume $V$ at temperature $500 \mathrm{~K}$ undergoes an isobaric expansion to volume $3 \mathrm{~V}$. Assume $R=8.0 \mathrm{Jmol}^{-1} \mathrm{~K}^{-1}$.
 (Q) $7 k J$
(III) One mole of a monatomic ideal gas is compressed adiabatically from volume $V=\frac{1}{3} \mathrm{~m}^{3}$ and pressure $2 \mathrm{kPa}$ to volume $\frac{V}{8}$.
 (R) $4 \mathrm{~kJ}$
(IV) Three moles of a diatomic ideal gas whose molecules can vibrate, is given $9 \mathrm{~kJ}$ of heat and undergoes isobaric expansion.
 (S) $5 \mathrm{~kJ}$
(T) $3 \mathrm{~kJ}$

Which one of the following options is correct?
options: I $\rightarrow$ T, II $\rightarrow$ R, III $\rightarrow$ S, IV $\rightarrow$ Q
I $\rightarrow$ S, II $\rightarrow$ P, III $\rightarrow$ T, IV $\rightarrow$ P
I $\rightarrow$ P, II $\rightarrow$ R, III $\rightarrow$ T, IV $\rightarrow$ Q
I $\rightarrow$ Q, II $\rightarrow$ R, III $\rightarrow$ S, IV $\rightarrow$ T
questions.solution:
$$U = ML - P\Delta V$$

$$ = {10^{ - 3}} \times 2250 - {10^2}kP \times ({10^{ - 3}} - {10^{ - 6}})$$ m3

= 2.25 kJ $$-$$ 0.1 kJ

= 2.15 kJ

I $$\to$$ P

(II) $${C_V} = {{5R} \over 2}$$ (rigid diatomic)

For isobaric expansion

$$V \propto T$$

$${{{V_1}} \over {{V_2}}} = {{{T_1}} \over {{T_2}}} \Rightarrow {V \over {3V}} = {{500} \over {{T_2}}} \Rightarrow {T_2} = 1500\,K$$

$$\Delta U = n{C_V}\Delta T = 0.2 \times {{5 \times 8} \over 2} \times (1500 - 500)$$ J = 4 kJ

II $$\to$$ R

(III) Adiabatic expansion $$\left( {\gamma = {5 \over 3}} \right)$$

$${P_1}V_1^\gamma = {P_2}V_2^\gamma \Rightarrow $$ (2 kPa) $$ \times V_0^{5/3} = {P_2} \times {\left( {{{{V_0}} \over 8}} \right)^{5/3}}$$

$${P_2} = 64$$ kPa

$$\Delta U = n{C_V}\Delta T$$

$$ = {{3nR\Delta T} \over 2} = {3 \over 2}({P_2}{V_2} - {P_1}{V_1}) = {3 \over 2} \times \left( {64 \times {1 \over {3 \times 8}} - 2 \times {1 \over 3}} \right)$$

$$ = {3 \over 2} \times \left( {{8 \over 3} - {2 \over 3}} \right) = 3$$ kJ

III $$\to$$ T

(IV) For isobaric expaision,

$$\Delta U = n{C_V}\Delta T = {7 \over 2}\,nR\Delta T$$

$$\Delta Q = n{C_P}\Delta T = {9 \over 2}\,nR\Delta T$$

$${{\Delta U} \over {\Delta Q}} = {7 \over 9}$$

$$\Delta U = {7 \over 9}\Delta Q = 7$$ kJ

IV $$\to$$ Q

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: A vertical tower stands on a horizontal plane and is surmounted by a vertical flagstaff of height h. At a point on the plane the angles of elevation of the bottom and top of the flagstaff are θ and 2θ respectively. What is the height of the tower ?
questions.solution:
Given:

A flagstaff of height $ h $ is mounted on a vertical tower.

The angles of elevation to the bottom and top of the flagstaff are $ \theta $ and $ 2\theta $ respectively.

Formulas Used:

1. $\tan\ \theta = \frac{\text{Perpendicular}}{\text{Base}}$

2. $\tan 2\theta = \frac{2\tan\ \theta}{1 - \tan^2 \theta}$

3. $\cos 2\theta = \frac{1 - \tan^2 \theta}{1 + \tan^2 \theta}$

Calculation:

Let the height of the tower be $ x $.

In right triangle $ \triangle ABC $:

$\tan\ \theta = \frac{AC}{AB} = \frac{x}{AB}$

⇒ $ AB = \frac{x}{\tan\ \theta} $      ------(1)

In right triangle $ \triangle ABD $:

$\tan 2\theta = \frac{AD}{AB} = \frac{(x + h)}{AB}$

⇒ $\frac{2\tan\ \theta}{1 - \tan^2\ \theta} = \frac{(x + h)}{AB}$   [Using formula (2)]

⇒ $\frac{2\tan\ \theta}{1 - \tan^2\ \theta} = \frac{(x + h) \tan\ \theta}{x}$   [From equation (1)]

⇒ $\frac{2}{1 - \tan^2\ \theta} = 1 + \frac{h}{x}$

⇒ $\frac{h}{x} = \frac{2}{1 - \tan^2 \theta} - 1$

⇒ $\frac{h}{x} = \frac{2 - (1 - \tan^2 \theta)}{1 - \tan^2 \theta}$

⇒ $\frac{h}{x} = \frac{1 + \tan^2 \theta}{1 - \tan^2 \theta}$

⇒ $\frac{x}{h} = \frac{1 - \tan^2 \theta}{1 + \tan^2 \theta} = \cos 2\theta$     [Using formula (3)]

⇒ $ x = h \cos 2\theta $

∴ The height of the tower is $ h \cos 2\theta $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: The shadow of a tower becomes x metre longer, when the angle of elevation of sun changes from 60° to θ. If the height of the tower is $\sqrt{3}x$ metre, then which one of the following is correct ?
questions.solution:
To solve the problem, let's first establish some basic trigonometric relationships and use them to derive the required conclusions. The key quantities given are:

  • The height of the tower, $ h = \sqrt{3}x $ meters.
  • The initial angle of elevation of the sun, which is 60 degrees.
  • The final angle of elevation of the sun, which is $ \theta $.
  • The shadow length increases by $ x $ meters when the angle changes from 60 degrees to $ \theta $.

First, let’s use the initial angle of elevation.

From trigonometry, tan of the angle of elevation is given by:

$$ \tan(60^\circ) = \frac{\text{height of the tower}}{\text{initial shadow length}} $$

Since $ \tan(60^\circ) = \sqrt{3} $, we have:

$$ \sqrt{3} = \frac{\sqrt{3}x}{L_1} $$

Solving for $ L_1 $ (initial shadow length):

$$ L_1 = x $$

For the angle $ \theta $, we have:

$$ \tan(\theta) = \frac{\text{height of the tower}}{\text{final shadow length}} $$

The final shadow length is $ L_1 + x = x + x = 2x $.

$$ \tan(\theta) = \frac{\sqrt{3}x}{2x} = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$

We need to determine $ \theta $ such that:

$$ \tan(\theta) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$

By examining the values for the tangent function within standard trigonometric angles, it is known that:

  • $\tan(60^\circ) = \sqrt{3}$
  • $\tan(45^\circ) = 1$
  • $\tan(30^\circ) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}$
  • $\tan(90^\circ) \to \infty$

Therefore, for the angle $ \theta $ with $\tan(\theta) = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$:

The angle $ \theta $ must lie between 30 degrees and 45 degrees.

Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: $30^\circ < \theta < 45^\circ$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the height of the lamp post ?
questions.solution:
Given that triangle ABC has sides AB = 16 m, BC = 10 m, and CA = 10 m. A lamp post is situated at the midpoint of side AB. Let's name the midpoint of AB as M. Therefore, AM = MB = 8 m, since AB = 16 m.

The lamp post at M subtends an angle of 45° at vertex B. Let's denote the height of the lamp post as h.

In isosceles right triangle BLM (where L is the top of the lamp post), we know that:

$$ \tan(45^\circ) = \frac{\text{opposite}}{\text{adjacent}} $$

Here, the opposite side of the angle 45° is the height of the lamp post (h), and the adjacent side is the length of MB (8 m). Since the angle is 45°:

$$ \tan(45^\circ) = 1 $$

Therefore, we have:

$$ 1 = \frac{h}{8} $$

Solving for h, we get:

$$ h = 8 \text{ m} $$

So, the height of the lamp post is 8 meters.

Hence, the correct answer is Option C: 8 m.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: At what height is the top of the tower above the ground level ?
questions.solution:
1. Let the foot of the tower be F, and let the top of the tower be T. Since the tower leans toward north, the horizontal displacement of T from F is some distance $w$ to the north, and the vertical height of T above ground is $h$.

  2. Point P is $x$ south of F, so the horizontal distance from P to the projection of T on the ground is

    $$ \overline{PT_{\rm ground}} \;=\; x + w. $$

    Similarly, from Q (which is $y$ south of F) to T’s projection is

    $$ \overline{QT_{\rm ground}} \;=\; y + w. $$

  3. From P the angle of elevation of T is $15^\circ$, so

    $$ \tan15^\circ \;=\;\frac{h}{\,x+w\,} \quad\Longrightarrow\quad x + w \;=\; h\cot15^\circ \;=\; h\tan75^\circ \;=\;h\,(2+\sqrt3). $$

  4. From Q the angle of elevation is $75^\circ$, so

    $$ \tan75^\circ \;=\;\frac{h}{\,y+w\,} \quad\Longrightarrow\quad y + w \;=\; h\cot75^\circ \;=\; h\tan15^\circ \;=\;h\,(2-\sqrt3). $$

  5. Subtract the two results:

    $$ (x + w)\;-\;(y + w) \;=\;h\bigl[(2+\sqrt3)-(2-\sqrt3)\bigr] \;\Longrightarrow\; x - y \;=\;2\sqrt3\;h \;\Longrightarrow\; h \;=\;\frac{x - y}{2\sqrt3}. $$

Answer: $\displaystyle \frac{x - y}{2\sqrt3}$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: If θ is the inclination of the tower to the horizontal, then what is cot θ equal to ?
questions.solution:
1. Let F be the foot of the tower and T its top. In the horizontal plane, take north as the positive direction. Let the tower lean north by a horizontal distance $δ$ and have vertical height $h$. Then the inclination of the tower to the horizontal is

$$\tan θ=\frac{h}{δ},\qquad\cot θ=\frac{δ}{h}.$$

  1. From point P (due south at distance $x$), the horizontal distance to T is $δ+x$, so

$$\tan 15°=\frac{h}{δ+x}=2-\sqrt3. \tag{1}$$

From Q (distance $y$),

$$\tan 75°=\frac{h}{δ+y}=2+\sqrt3. \tag{2}$$

  1. Divide (1) by (2):

$$\frac{δ+y}{δ+x}=\frac{2-\sqrt3}{2+\sqrt3}=(2-\sqrt3)^2=7-4\sqrt3.$$

So

$$δ+y=(7-4\sqrt3)(δ+x)\,\Longrightarrow\,(4\sqrt3-6)\,δ=(7-4\sqrt3)\,x-y$$

$$\Longrightarrow\;δ=\frac{(7-4\sqrt3)x-y}{4\sqrt3-6} =\frac{2\sqrt3(x-y)-3(x+y)}{6}.$$

  1. From (1),

$$h=(δ+x)(2-\sqrt3).$$

But

$$δ+x=\frac{2\sqrt3(x-y)-3(x+y)+6x}{6}=\frac{(x-y)(3+2\sqrt3)}{6},$$

and

$$(3+2\sqrt3)(2-\sqrt3)=\sqrt3.$$

Hence

$$h=\frac{(x-y)\,\sqrt3}{6}.$$

  1. Therefore

$$\cot θ=\frac{δ}{h} =\frac{\displaystyle\frac{2\sqrt3(x-y)-3(x+y)}{6}}{\displaystyle\frac{\sqrt3(x-y)}{6}} =\frac{2\sqrt3(x-y)-3(x+y)}{\sqrt3(x-y)} =2-\frac{3(x+y)}{\sqrt3\,(x-y)} =2-\frac{\sqrt3\,(x+y)}{x-y}.$$

Answer: cot θ = $2-\displaystyle\frac{\sqrt3\,(x+y)}{x-y}$ (Option D).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the length of the tower ?
questions.solution:
There are two points P and Q due south of a leaning tower, which leans towards the north. P is at a distance x and Q is at a distance y from the base of the tower (x > y). The angles of elevation of the top of the tower from P and Q are 15° and 75°, respectively.

What is the height of the tower?

Option A:

$\frac{\text{x−y}}{2 \sqrt{3}} \sqrt{1+\left\{2+\frac{\sqrt{3}(\text{x+y})}{\text{x−y}}\right\}^2}$

Option B:

$\frac{\text{x−y}}{2 \sqrt{3}} \sqrt{1+\left\{2−\frac{\sqrt{3}(\text{x+y})}{\text{x−y}}\right\}^2}$

Option C:

$\frac{\text{x−y}}{4 \sqrt{3}} \sqrt{1+\left\{2+\frac{\sqrt{3}(\text{x+y})}{\text{x−y}}\right\}^2}$

Option D:

$\frac{\text{x−y}}{4 \sqrt{3}} \sqrt{1+\left\{2−\frac{\sqrt{3}(\text{x+y})}{\text{x−y}}\right\}^2}$

Explanation

Concept:

tan(x + y) = $\frac {tan x + tan y}{1-(tan x)(tan y)}$

tan(x - y) = $\frac {tan x - tan y}{1+(tan x)(tan y)}$

tan0° tan30° tan45° tan60° tan90°
0 $\frac{1}{√3}$ 1 √3 Not defined

Calculation:

According to the problem, two points P and Q make angles of 15° and 75° with the top of the tower, respectively, as shown in the figure below:

Let the distance from the ground to the tower's base be a.

We need to find the length of the tower, which is represented as AM.

First, let's find the height of the tower from the ground level, represented as OM (denoted as m).

In triangle MOP:

tan(15°) = $\frac{OM}{OP}$

⇒tan(45° - 30°) = $\frac{tan45°-tan30°}{1+tan45° tan30° }$ = $\frac{OM}{OP}$

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OP}$ = $\frac{1-√3}{1+√3}$

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OP}=\frac{1-√3}{1+√3}[\frac{1-√3}{1-√3}]$ (Rationalizing)

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OP}=\frac{(1-√3)^2}{1^2-√3^2}$

⇒ $\frac{m}{a+x} = \frac{1-2√3+3}{1-3}$

⇒ $\frac{m}{a+x} = √3-2$

⇒ m = (a + x)(√3 - 2)...(equation 1)

Now, considering triangle MOQ:

tan(75°) = $\frac{OM}{OQ}$

⇒tan(45° + 30°) = $\frac{tan45°+tan30°}{1-tan45° tan30° }$ = $\frac{OM}{OQ}$

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OQ} = \frac{1+√3}{1-√3}$

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OQ} = \frac{1+√3}{1-√3}[\frac{1+√3}{1+√3}]$ (Rationalizing)

⇒ $\frac{OM}{OQ} = \frac{(1+√3)^2}{1^2-√3^2}$

⇒ $\frac{m}{a+y} = \frac{1+2√3+3}{1-3}$

⇒ $\frac{m}{a+y} = -(√3+2)$

⇒ m = - (a + y)(√3 + 2)

Now, substituting the value of m from equation 1, we get:

 (a + x)(√3 - 2) = - (a + y)(√3 + 2)

⇒ (√3 a - 2a + √3 x - 2x) = - (√3 a + 2a + √3 y + 2y)

⇒ (√3 a - 2a + √3 x - 2x) = - (√3 a + 2a + √3 y + 2y)

⇒ 2(√3 a) = 2(x - y) - (√3)(x + y)

⇒ a = $\frac{2(x-y) - √3(x+y)}{2√3}$

Substituting the value of a in equation 1:

m = ( $\frac{2(x-y) - √3(x+y)}{2√3}$ + x)(√3 - 2)

⇒ m = $\frac{2(x-y) - √3(x+y) + 2√3 x}{2√3}$ (√3 - 2)

⇒ m = $\frac{x(2+√3) - y(2+√3)}{2√3}$ (√3 - 2)

On simplifying, we get:

m = $\frac{x - y}{2√3}$

Now, consider triangle MOA and apply the Pythagorean theorem:

(OM)2 + (OA)2 = (MA)2

m2 + a2 = (MA)2

(MA)2 = ($\frac{x - y}{2√3}$)2 + $\frac{2(x - y) - √3(x + y)}{2√3}$2

On simplifying, we get:

(MA)2 = ($\frac{x - y}{2√3}$)2 (1 + $2 - \frac{√3(x + y)}{x - y}$)

The length of the tower, AM, hence, is:

(MA) = $\frac{x - y}{2√3} \sqrt{1 + \left\{2 - \frac{√3(x + y)}{x - y}\right\}^2}$

Thus, the length of the tower is:

AM = $\frac{x - y}{2√3} \sqrt{1 + \left\{2 - \frac{√3(x + y)}{x - y}\right\}^2}$

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Who among the following laid the foundation of the Vijayanagara Empire?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option A: Harihara and Bukka.

Here's why:

Harihara and Bukka, two brothers, are credited with establishing the Vijayanagara Empire in 1336 AD. They were originally under the rule of the Kakatiya dynasty, but after the decline of the Kakatiyas, they rose to power. They conquered the region around Vijayanagara, which is now in Karnataka, and declared their independence. They built the city of Vijayanagara, which became the capital of their empire, and established a strong administrative system and a flourishing culture.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Krishnadeva Raya (1509-1529) was a renowned Vijayanagara emperor, known for his military prowess and patronage of arts and literature. He is considered to be the most famous ruler of the Vijayanagara Empire but he did not found the empire.
  • Rama Raya was a general in the Vijayanagara army, who briefly ruled the empire in the mid-16th century. He was not the founder.
  • Virupaksha Raya was another king of the Vijayanagara Empire, but his reign was not as prominent as that of Harihara and Bukka.

Therefore, the foundation of the Vijayanagara Empire is attributed to Harihara and Bukka.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: The book, Kalila wa Dimna is an Arabic translation of the
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Panchatantra.

Here's why:

Kalila wa Dimna is a collection of animal fables that originated in India. It was originally written in Sanskrit and is believed to be based on the Panchatantra, a classic Indian text. The Panchatantra is a collection of animal stories that teach moral and ethical lessons.

The Panchatantra was translated into Arabic by an Indian scholar named Ibn al-Muqaffa in the 8th century CE. He named his translation Kalila wa Dimna, which is the Arabic equivalent of the Sanskrit names "Karataka" and "Damanaka," the two main characters in the story.

Let's look at why the other options are incorrect:

  • Option A: Hitopadesha: The Hitopadesha is another collection of Indian fables, but it is a later work than the Panchatantra and was influenced by it.
  • Option C: Suryasiddhanta: The Suryasiddhanta is an ancient Indian astronomical treatise, not a collection of fables.
  • Option D: Kathasaritsagar: The Kathasaritsagar is a collection of tales, but it is primarily focused on romantic and heroic narratives, unlike the moral fables of Kalila wa Dimna.

questions.question: Who among the following initiated the Bhoodan Movement?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option B: Vinoba Bhave.

Here's why:

  • Vinoba Bhave was a prominent Indian social activist and a close associate of Mahatma Gandhi. He initiated the Bhoodan Movement (Land Gift Movement) in 1951. The movement aimed at persuading wealthy landowners to voluntarily donate a part of their land to landless farmers.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Ram Prasad Bismil was a revolutionary freedom fighter involved in the Kakori Conspiracy and other movements against British rule. He was not associated with the Bhoodan Movement.

  • Mahatma Gandhi, while a strong advocate for social justice and upliftment of the poor, did not directly initiate the Bhoodan Movement. However, his philosophy of non-violence and rural development greatly influenced Vinoba Bhave.

  • Kanhu Murmu was a Santhal tribal leader who led the Santhal rebellion (Hul) against British oppression in the mid-19th century. He was not connected to the Bhoodan Movement.
In essence, the Bhoodan Movement was Vinoba Bhave's initiative to address land inequality and promote social harmony through voluntary land redistribution.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Which one of the following rivers in India was not crossed by Alexander and his army?
questions.solution:
Alexander the Great and his army, during their campaign in India, crossed several rivers. Here is a brief explanation of the rivers to determine which one was not crossed by Alexander and his army:

1. Hyphasis: The Hyphasis River is known today as the Beas River. This was the easternmost point of Alexander's campaign in India. It is well documented that his troops refused to march further east of this river, leading him to halt his advance and turn back westward.

2. Acesines: The Acesines River is known today as the Chenab River. Alexander did cross this river on his march through India.

3. Hydraotes: The Hydraotes River is the modern Ravi River. Alexander also crossed this river during his campaign.

4. Hydaspes: The Hydaspes River is known as the Jhelum River today. Alexander fought a famous battle against King Porus on the banks of this river and successfully crossed it.

Based on this information, while Alexander crossed the Acesines (Chenab), Hydraotes (Ravi), and Hydaspes (Jhelum), he did not cross the Hyphasis (Beas) as it was the furthest point of his conquest in India, where his troops refused to proceed further.

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option A: Hyphasis

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Who among the following has described the medieval Indian postal system as of two kinds—the horse-post called ‘Uluq’ and the foot-post called ‘Dawa’?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option C: Ibn Battuta.

Here's why:

  • Ibn Battuta (1304-1368) was a Moroccan scholar and traveler who journeyed extensively across the Islamic world, including India during the Delhi Sultanate under Muhammad bin Tughlaq's reign. He provides a firsthand account of the postal system, mentioning the two types:

  • Uluq: The fast horse-post used for communication by the state.

  • Dawa: The foot-post, which was slower but more widely used for regular communication.

Let's look at the other options:

  • Al-Biruni (973-1048) was a scholar who traveled to India but wrote about an earlier period. His works focus more on mathematics, astronomy, and Indian culture, rather than the specifics of the postal system.

  • Duarte Barbosa (c. 1480-1521) was a Portuguese traveler who focused on the coastal regions of India, primarily describing trade and customs. He doesn't provide a detailed account of the postal system.

  • Seydi Ali Reis (1498-1562) was an Ottoman admiral and cartographer. While his work offers insights into the Indian Ocean world, he doesn't specifically detail the internal communication systems of medieval India.

Therefore, Ibn Battuta's firsthand account makes him the most reliable source for information about the medieval Indian postal system's two branches – Uluq and Dawa.

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: At which among the following Harappan sites are fire altars found?
questions.solution:
Fire altars were a significant feature in the Harappan civilization and are indicative of ritualistic practices. Among the options provided, fire altars have been found at:

Option A: Kalibangan

Kalibangan, located in present-day Rajasthan, India, is known for its archaeological significance and the presence of fire altars. These altars suggest that the inhabitants practiced some form of ritual or religious activity involving fire.

To summarize, the correct answer is:

Option A: Kalibangan

questions.title: NDA General Ability 4 September 2022
questions.question: Who among the following is the author of the Hastyayurveda ?
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option A: Palakapya.

Here's why:

  • Palakapya was an ancient Indian sage who is credited as the author of Hastyāyurveda, a Sanskrit text on the treatment and management of elephants. This work covers a wide range of topics, including elephant anatomy, physiology, diseases, and their treatments.

  • Vagbhata, Sushruta, and Charaka are prominent figures in Indian medicine, but they are known for other works:

  • Vagbhata is the author of the Ashtanga Hridayam, an important Ayurvedic text.

  • Sushruta is known as the "father of surgery" and authored the Sushruta Samhita, a foundational text in surgery.

  • Charaka is considered the "father of medicine" and his work, the Charaka Samhita, is a cornerstone of Ayurvedic medicine.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question:
Which one of the following is correct order of given isotopes?

I. T2 > D2 > P2 (order of boiling point)

II. T2 > D2 > P2 (order of bond energy)

III. T2 = D2 = P2 (order of bond length)

IV. T2 < D2 < P2 (order of reactivity with Cl2)
questions.solution:
The correct answer is Option D: All of these. Let's break down why each statement is true:

I. T2 > D2 > P2 (order of boiling point)

This is correct. Boiling point is directly related to the strength of intermolecular forces. The heavier the isotope, the stronger the London dispersion forces (temporary dipoles) between molecules. Thus, T2 (tritium) has the highest boiling point followed by D2 (deuterium) and then P2 (protium).

II. T2 > D2 > P2 (order of bond energy)

This is also correct. Bond energy is related to the strength of the covalent bond. Heavier isotopes have slightly stronger bonds due to the increased mass of the nucleus, leading to a stronger electrostatic attraction between the nuclei and the shared electrons. Therefore, T2 has the highest bond energy, followed by D2 and then P2.

III. T2 = D2 = P2 (order of bond length)

This is correct. Bond length is primarily determined by the size of the atoms involved. Since all three isotopes of hydrogen have the same number of protons and electrons, their atomic radii are essentially identical. Consequently, the bond lengths in T2, D2, and P2 are very similar, and for practical purposes, we can consider them equal.

IV. T2 < D2 < P2 (order of reactivity with Cl2)

This is also correct. The reactivity of hydrogen isotopes with chlorine is inversely proportional to the strength of the H-H bond. As we discussed earlier, T2 has the strongest bond, followed by D2 and P2. Therefore, P2 is the most reactive with Cl2, followed by D2 and then T2.

In summary, all four statements accurately reflect the trends in properties for the isotopes of hydrogen. Heavier isotopes generally exhibit stronger intermolecular forces, stronger bonds, and slightly lower reactivity.

List-I List-II

(II) $\mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}$

(III) $\mathrm{BaCl}_{2}$
 (R) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2}+\mathrm{MgCl}_{2} \rightarrow$
(IV) $\mathrm{CaCO}_{3}$
 (S) $\mathrm{BaO}_{2}+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow$
(T) $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_{3}\right)_{2}+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_{2} \rightarrow$

Match each compound in LIST-I with its formation reaction(s) in LIST-II, and choose the correct option
options: I $\rightarrow$ Q; II $\rightarrow$ P; III $\rightarrow$ S; IV $\rightarrow$ R
I $\rightarrow$ T; II $\rightarrow$ P; III $\rightarrow$ Q; IV $\rightarrow$ R
I $\rightarrow$ T; II $\rightarrow$ R; III $\rightarrow$ Q; IV $\rightarrow$ P
I $\rightarrow$ Q; II $\rightarrow$ R; III $\rightarrow$ S; IV $\rightarrow$ P
questions.solution:
(P) $\mathrm{Mg}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3\right)_2+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2+\mathrm{CaCO}_3+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

(Q) $\mathrm{BaO}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4 \rightarrow \mathrm{BaSO}_4+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$

(R) $\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2+\mathrm{MgCl}_2 \rightarrow \mathrm{Mg}(\mathrm{OH})_2+\mathrm{CaCl}_2$

(S) $\mathrm{BaO}_2+\mathrm{HCl} \rightarrow \mathrm{BaCl}_2+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$

(T) $\mathrm{Ca}\left(\mathrm{HCO}_3\right)_2+\mathrm{Ca}(\mathrm{OH})_2 \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{CaCO}_3+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$

Ans. $\quad \mathrm{I} \rightarrow \mathrm{Q}, \mathrm{S} ; \mathrm{II} \rightarrow \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{R} ; \mathrm{III} \rightarrow \mathrm{S} ; \mathrm{IV} \rightarrow \mathrm{P}, \mathrm{T}$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Consider the hyperbola

$$ \frac{x^{2}}{100}-\frac{y^{2}}{64}=1 $$

with foci at $S$ and $S_{1}$, where $S$ lies on the positive $x$-axis. Let $P$ be a point on the hyperbola, in the first quadrant. Let $\angle S P S_{1}=\alpha$, with $\alpha<\frac{\pi}{2}$. The straight line passing through the point $S$ and having the same slope as that of the tangent at $P$ to the hyperbola, intersects the straight line $S_{1} P$ at $P_{1}$. Let $\delta$ be the distance of $P$ from the straight line $S P_{1}$, and $\beta=S_{1} P$. Then the greatest integer less than or equal to $\frac{\beta \delta}{9} \sin \frac{\alpha}{2}$ is ________.
questions.solution:
From property we know, tangent and normal is bisector of the angle between focal radii.

$$\therefore$$ Tangent AB divides the angle $$\angle SP{S_1} = \alpha $$ equal parts.

From another property, we know, if we draw perpendicular to the tangent on the hyperbola from two foci, then product of length of the perpendicular from foci = b2

$$\therefore$$ $$l \times \delta = {b^2}$$

Given hyperbola, $${{{x^2}} \over {100}} - {{{y^2}} \over {64}} = 1$$

$$\therefore$$ $${a^2} = 100$$

and $${b^2} = 64$$

$$\therefore$$ $$l \times \delta = 64$$ ....... (1)

From right angle triangle S1 MP we get, $$\sin {\alpha \over 2} = {l \over \beta }$$

$$\therefore$$ $$l = \beta \sin {\alpha \over 2}$$ ....... (2)

Putting value of l in equation (1), we get

$$\left( {\beta \sin {\alpha \over 2}} \right) \times \delta = 64$$

$$ \Rightarrow \delta \beta \sin {\alpha \over 2} = 64$$

$$\therefore$$ $${{\beta \delta } \over 9}\sin {\alpha \over 2}$$

$$ = {{64} \over 9} = 7.1$$

$$\therefore$$ Greatest integer $$ = [7.1] = 7$$
