Since raising to the fourth power eliminates the negative sign, this simplifies to:

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{\pi}{8}\right) + \cos^4\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right)$$

Next, utilize the following identity for any angle $$\theta$$:

$$\cos^4(\theta) = \frac{3 + 4\cos(2\theta) + \cos(4\theta)}{8}$$

In our case, we can apply this identity separately for $$\theta = \frac{\pi}{8}$$ and $$\theta = \frac{3\pi}{8}$$:

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{3 + 4\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) + \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)}{8}$$

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{3 + 4\cos\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}\right) + \cos\left(\frac{3\pi}{2}\right)}{8}$$

Since $$\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$$, $$\cos\left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0$$, and $$\cos\left(\frac{3\pi}{4}\right) = -\frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}$$:

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{3 + 2\sqrt{2}}{8}$$

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{3 - 2\sqrt{2}}{8}$$

Add these two results:

$$\cos^4\left(\frac{\pi}{8}\right) + \cos^4\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{3 + 2\sqrt{2}}{8} + \frac{3 - 2\sqrt{2}}{8} = \frac{3 + 3}{8} = \frac{6}{8} = \frac{3}{4}$$

Thus, the value of $$\cos^4\frac{7\pi}{8} + \cos^4\frac{5\pi}{8}$$ is:

Option B: $$\frac{3}{4}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is $\sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}+\theta\right)-\sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4}-\theta\right)$ equal to?
questions.solution:
To find the value of the given expression:

$$\sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)$$

We can use the trigonometric identity for the difference of squares:

$$\sin^2 A - \sin^2 B = (\sin A + \sin B)(\sin A - \sin B)$$

where $A = \frac{\pi}{4} + \theta$ and $B = \frac{\pi}{4} - \theta$.

Let's apply this identity step by step:

$$\sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right) = \left(\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) + \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)\right)\left(\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)\right)$$

We can use the sum-to-product identities for sine to simplify further:

Sum identity:

$$\sin A + \sin B = 2 \sin\left(\frac{A + B}{2}\right) \cos\left(\frac{A - B}{2}\right)$$

Applying this to our terms:

$$\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) + \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right) = 2 \sin\left(\frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) + \left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)}{2}\right) \cos\left(\frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)}{2}\right)$$

$$= 2 \sin\left(\frac{\frac{\pi}{4} + \frac{\pi}{4} + \theta - \theta}{2}\right) \cos\left(\frac{\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta - \frac{\pi}{4} + \theta}{2}\right)$$

$$= 2 \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \cos\left(\theta\right)$$

$$= 2 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \cdot \cos\left(\theta\right)$$

$$= \sqrt{2} \cos\left(\theta\right)$$

Difference identity:

$$\sin A - \sin B = 2 \cos\left(\frac{A + B}{2}\right) \sin\left(\frac{A - B}{2}\right)$$

Applying this to our terms:

$$\sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right) = 2 \cos\left(\frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) + \left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)}{2}\right) \sin\left(\frac{\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)}{2}\right)$$

$$= 2 \cos\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \sin\left(\theta\right)$$

$$= 2 \cdot \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2} \cdot \sin\left(\theta\right)$$

$$= \sqrt{2} \sin\left(\theta\right)$$

Now, combining these results:

$$\left(\sqrt{2} \cos\left(\theta\right)\right) \left(\sqrt{2} \sin\left(\theta\right)\right)$$

$$= 2 \cos\left(\theta\right) \sin\left(\theta\right)$$

$$= \sin 2\theta$$

Thus, the expression:

$$\sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} + \theta\right) - \sin^2\left(\frac{\pi}{4} - \theta\right)$$

is equal to:

$$\sin 2\theta$$

Hence, the correct option is:

Option A: $\sin 2\theta$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If tan α + tan β = 1 - tan α.tan β, where tan α tan β ≠ 1, then which of the following is one of the values of (α + β) ?
questions.solution:
To solve the given problem, let's start by analyzing the given equation involving the tangent function:

$$\tan\alpha + \tan\beta = 1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta$$

Recall the tangent addition formula:

$$\tan(\alpha + \beta) = \frac{\tan\alpha + \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta}$$

Given that

$$\tan\alpha + \tan\beta = 1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta$$

substitute this into the tangent addition formula:

$$\tan(\alpha + \beta) = \frac{\tan\alpha + \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta} = \frac{1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \cdot \tan\beta} = 1$$

This simplifies to:

$$\tan(\alpha + \beta) = 1$$

We need to find the values of $ \alpha + \beta $ that satisfy this condition. The angle for which the tangent is equal to 1 is:

$$\alpha + \beta = \frac{\pi}{4} + n\pi \quad \text{for integer } n$$

Among the given options, the value that matches this condition without adding any multiple of $ n\pi $ is:

Option B: $ \frac{\pi}{4} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If (1 + tan θ) (1 + tan 9θ) = 2, then what is the value of tan(10θ)?
questions.solution:
To solve the given equation $ (1 + \tan \theta)(1 + \tan 9\theta) = 2 $, let's start by recalling some trigonometric identities and relationships. We are given:

$$ (1 + \tan \theta)(1 + \tan 9\theta) = 2 $$

We can use the tangent angle sum identity to find a relationship between $\tan \theta$ and $\tan 9\theta$. The identity for the tangent of the sum of two angles is:

$$ \tan(A + B) = \frac{\tan A + \tan B}{1 - \tan A \tan B} $$

Notice that we need the sum of angles $\theta + 9\theta$, which is $10\theta$. We use the tangent sum identity in reverse. Setting $A = \theta$ and $B = 9\theta$, we have:

$$ \tan 10\theta = \frac{\tan \theta + \tan 9\theta}{1 - \tan \theta \tan 9\theta} $$

Given $ (1 + \tan \theta)(1 + \tan 9\theta) = 2 $, we expand the left-hand side:

$$ 1 + \tan \theta + \tan 9\theta + \tan \theta \tan 9\theta = 2 $$

By rearranging, we find:

$$ \tan \theta + \tan 9\theta + \tan \theta \tan 9\theta = 1 $$

We notice that this fits the form of a tangent sum identity if we set $\theta + 9\theta = \pi/4$, because for $\tan(\pi/4) = 1$, which simplifies $\tan 10\theta$ as follows:

Thus, we have:

$$ 10\theta = \frac{\pi}{4} $$

So:

$$ \tan 10\theta = \tan \left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right) = 1 $$

Therefore, the value of $\tan 10\theta$ is:

Option B: 1

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the value of sin 0° + sin 10° + sin 20° + sin 30° + ⋯ + sin 360°?
questions.solution:
To determine the value of $$\sin 0^\circ + \sin 10^\circ + \sin 20^\circ + \sin 30^\circ + \cdots + \sin 360^\circ$$, we need to understand the properties of the sine function over one complete cycle from $$0^\circ$$ to $$360^\circ$$.

The sine function, $$\sin \theta$$, is periodic with a period of $$360^\circ$$. This means that every $$360^\circ$$, the function repeats its values. Also, the sine function is symmetric about $$0^\circ$$ and $$180^\circ$$:

  1. From $$0^\circ$$ to $$180^\circ$$, the sine function increases from 0 to 1 (at $$90^\circ$$) and decreases back to 0.

  2. From $$180^\circ$$ to $$360^\circ$$, the sine function decreases to -1 (at $$270^\circ$$) and returns to 0.

Because of this symmetry, for any angle $$\theta$$ between $$0^\circ$$ and $$180^\circ$$, there is a corresponding angle $$\theta + 180^\circ$$ whose sine is the negative of $$\sin \theta$$. Hence, the contributions of these pairs add up to zero.

Listing the angles:

$$\sin 0^\circ + \sin 180^\circ = 0 + 0 = 0$$

$$\sin 10^\circ + \sin 190^\circ = \sin 10^\circ - \sin 10^\circ = 0$$

$$\sin 20^\circ + \sin 200^\circ = \sin 20^\circ - \sin 20^\circ = 0$$

And so on, up to:

$$\sin 170^\circ + \sin 350^\circ = \sin 170^\circ - \sin 170^\circ = 0$$

The only terms left uncompensated are $$\sin 180^\circ$$ and $$\sin 360^\circ$$, both of which are zero:

$$\sin 180^\circ = 0$$ and $$\sin 360^\circ = 0$$

Adding all these up, the sum of the sine values over one complete cycle from $$0^\circ$$ to $$360^\circ$$ is:

$$\sin 0^\circ + \sin 10^\circ + \sin 20^\circ + \cdots + \sin 360^\circ = 0$$

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option B: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: What is the maximum value of the functions $f(x) = \frac{1}{\tan x+\cot x},$ where $0 < x < \frac{\pi}{2}?$
questions.solution:
To determine the maximum value of the function $ f(x) = \frac{1}{\tan x + \cot x} $ where $ 0 < x < \frac{\pi}{2} $, we first simplify the expression inside the function.

Recall that $ \tan x = \frac{\sin x}{\cos x} $ and $ \cot x = \frac{\cos x}{\sin x} $. Therefore, we have:

$$ \tan x + \cot x = \frac{\sin x}{\cos x} + \frac{\cos x}{\sin x} = \frac{\sin^2 x + \cos^2 x}{\sin x \cos x} $$

Since $ \sin^2 x + \cos^2 x = 1 $ for all $ x $, the expression simplifies to:

$$ \tan x + \cot x = \frac{1}{\sin x \cos x} = \frac{2}{2 \sin x \cos x} = \frac{2}{\sin 2x} $$

Therefore, the function can be written as:

$$ f(x) = \frac{1}{\frac{2}{\sin 2x}} = \frac{\sin 2x}{2} $$

Since $ \sin 2x $ attains its maximum value of 1, the maximum value of our function $ f(x) $ is:

$$ \frac{1}{2} $$

Hence, the maximum value of the function $ f(x) = \frac{1}{\tan x + \cot x} $ is:

Option B: $ \frac{1}{2} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the value of cosec$\left(−\frac{73 \pi}{3}\right)$ ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $$\csc\left(-\frac{73\pi}{3}\right)$$, we need to simplify the angle first.

First, note that the cosecant function is the reciprocal of the sine function, so we begin by simplifying the angle inside the sine function:

$$-\frac{73\pi}{3}$$

This angle is more than one full turn (i.e., more than $$2\pi$$ radians). To simplify it, we can add or subtract multiples of $$2\pi$$ until we get an equivalent angle between $$0$$ and $$2\pi$$.

Calculate the equivalent positive angle:

$$\left(-\frac{73\pi}{3}\right) + 24\pi = \left(-\frac{73\pi}{3}\right) + \left(\frac{72\pi}{3}\right) = -\frac{73\pi}{3} + \frac{72\pi}{3} = -\frac{\pi}{3}$$

Note that adding $$24$$ times $$\pi$$ (which is a multiple of $$2\pi$$) effectively gives us an equivalent angle.

So, $$\csc\left(-\frac{73\pi}{3}\right) = \csc\left(-\frac{\pi}{3}\right)$$.

Next, we use the property of the cosecant function that $$\csc(-x) = -\csc(x)$$, thus:

$$\csc\left(-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) = -\csc\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right)$$.

The value of $$\csc\left(\frac{\pi}{3}\right)$$ is $$\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$$. Therefore:

$$\csc\left(-\frac{\pi}{3}\right) = -\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$$.

So, the correct option is:

Option B: $$−\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the value of $\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right)+\cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right)+2 \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17}\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{17}\right)$ ?
questions.solution:
Let's find the value of the expression $$\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right) + 2 \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17}\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{17}\right)$$.

First, we use the product-to-sum identities for cosine:

$$2 \cos A \cos B = \cos (A + B) + \cos (A - B)$$

Applying this identity to the term $$2 \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17}\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{17}\right)$$, we get:

$$2 \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17}\right) \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{17}\right) = \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17} + \frac{\pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{11 \pi}{17} - \frac{\pi}{17}\right)$$

This simplifies to:

$$\cos \left(\frac{12 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{10 \pi}{17}\right)$$

So the original expression now becomes:

$$\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{12 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{10 \pi}{17}\right)$$

Recall that for arguments that add up to $$\pi$$, the following identity holds:

$$\cos x + \cos (\pi - x) = 0$$

Now observing the values,

$$\cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{10 \pi}{17}\right) = \cos \left(\frac{10 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right) = 0$$ and

$$\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{12 \pi}{17}\right) = \cos \left(\frac{12 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right) = 0$$

Thus, adding everything up:

$$\cos \left(\frac{5 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{7 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{12 \pi}{17}\right) + \cos \left(\frac{10 \pi}{17}\right) = 0$$

Therefore, the value of the given expression is 0.

The correct option is:

Option A: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the value of tan$\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right)$ ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $$\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right)$$, we can use trigonometric identities and known values. Let's proceed step-by-step:

1. Double Angle Formula for Tangent:

We know the double angle formula for tangent is given by:

$$\tan(2\theta) = \frac{2\tan(\theta)}{1 - \tan^2(\theta)}$$

Let's set $$\theta = \frac{3\pi}{16}$$. Then:

$$2\theta = \frac{3\pi}{8}$$

2. Using the double angle formula:

So, $$\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) = \frac{2\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{16}\right)}{1 - \tan^2\left(\frac{3\pi}{16}\right)}$$

3. Relationship with known angles:

The angle $$\frac{3\pi}{8}$$ lies between $$\frac{\pi}{4} \approx 0.7854$$ radians and $$\frac{\pi}{2} \approx 1.5708$$ radians, and hence we can consider the known tangent values of these angles in this range.

4. Solving for the exact value:

Using trigonometric identities and simplifying, we find that:

$$\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right) = \sqrt{2} + 1$$

Therefore, the value of $$\tan\left(\frac{3\pi}{8}\right)$$ is $$\sqrt{2} + 1$$.

Hence, the correct answer is Option B: $$\sqrt{2} + 1$$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is cos 36° − cos 72° equal to ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ$$, we can use the trigonometric identity and some known values of cosine for special angles.

We know the following values for cosine:

$$\cos 36^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{5} + 1}{4}$$

$$\cos 72^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{5} - 1}{4}$$

Now, let's calculate $$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ$$:

$$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ = \left( \frac{\sqrt{5} + 1}{4} \right) - \left( \frac{\sqrt{5} - 1}{4} \right)$$

Combining the terms, we get:

$$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ = \frac{(\sqrt{5} + 1) - (\sqrt{5} - 1)}{4}$$

Simplifying the numerator, we obtain:

$$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{5} + 1 - \sqrt{5} + 1}{4}$$

$$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ = \frac{2}{4}$$

$$\cos 36^\circ - \cos 72^\circ = \frac{1}{2}$$

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option C

$$\frac{1}{2}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: If sec x = $\frac{25}{24}$ and x lies in the fourth quadrant, then what is the value of tan x + sin x ?
questions.solution:
Given that $$\sec x = \frac{25}{24}$$ and $ x $ lies in the fourth quadrant, we need to find the value of $$\tan x + \sin x$$.

First, we know that $$\sec x = \frac{1}{\cos x}$$. Therefore, $$\cos x = \frac{24}{25}$$.

Because $ x $ is in the fourth quadrant, both $$\cos x$$ and $$\sec x$$ are positive. However, both $$\sin x$$ and $$\tan x$$ will be negative.

To find $$\sin x$$, we use the Pythagorean identity:

$$ \sin^2 x + \cos^2 x = 1 $$

Plugging in the value of $$\cos x$$:

$$ \sin^2 x + \left( \frac{24}{25} \right)^2 = 1 $$

$$ \sin^2 x + \frac{576}{625} = 1 $$

$$ \sin^2 x = 1 - \frac{576}{625} $$

$$ \sin^2 x = \frac{625}{625} - \frac{576}{625} $$

$$ \sin^2 x = \frac{49}{625} $$

Thus,

$$ \sin x = -\frac{7}{25} $$

(It is negative because $ x $ is in the fourth quadrant.)

Next, calculate $$\tan x$$:

$$ \tan x = \frac{\sin x}{\cos x} $$

$$ \tan x = \frac{-\frac{7}{25}}{\frac{24}{25}} $$

$$ \tan x = -\frac{7}{24} $$

Finally, we need to find $$\tan x + \sin x$$:

$$ \tan x + \sin x = -\frac{7}{24} - \frac{7}{25} $$

To add these fractions, find a common denominator:

$$ \tan x + \sin x = -\frac{7 \times 25}{24 \times 25} - \frac{7 \times 24}{25 \times 24} $$

$$ \tan x + \sin x = -\frac{175}{600} - \frac{168}{600} $$

$$ \tan x + \sin x = -\frac{343}{600} $$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option B $$-\frac{343}{600}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the value of tan2 165° + cot2 165° ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of $ \tan^2 165^\circ + \cot^2 165^\circ $, we first need to find the values of $ \tan 165^\circ $ and $ \cot 165^\circ $.

We know that:

$$ \tan (180^\circ - \theta) = -\tan \theta $$

Therefore:

$$ \tan 165^\circ = \tan(180^\circ - 15^\circ) = -\tan 15^\circ $$

Using the angle subtraction formula for tangent:

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \tan (45^\circ - 30^\circ) $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{\tan 45^\circ - \tan 30^\circ}{1 + \tan 45^\circ \tan 30^\circ} $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{1 - \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}}{1 + 1 \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{3}}} $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{3} - 1}{\sqrt{3} + 1} $$

To simplify, multiply the numerator and the denominator by $ \sqrt{3} - 1 $:

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{(\sqrt{3} - 1)^2}{(\sqrt{3} + 1)(\sqrt{3} - 1)} $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{3 - 2\sqrt{3} + 1}{3 - 1} $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = \frac{4 - 2\sqrt{3}}{2} $$

$$ \tan 15^\circ = 2 - \sqrt{3} $$

Thus:

$$ \tan 165^\circ = -\tan 15^\circ = - (2 - \sqrt{3}) = \sqrt{3} - 2 $$

Next, let's find $ \cot 165^\circ $:

$$ \cot 165^\circ = \frac{1}{\tan 165^\circ} $$

$$ \cot 165^\circ = \frac{1}{\sqrt{3} - 2} $$

To simplify $ \frac{1}{\sqrt{3} - 2} $, multiply the numerator and the denominator by $ \sqrt{3} + 2 $:

$$ \cot 165^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{3} + 2}{(\sqrt{3} - 2)(\sqrt{3} + 2)} $$

$$ \cot 165^\circ = \frac{\sqrt{3} + 2}{3 - 4} $$

$$ \cot 165^\circ = - (\sqrt{3} + 2) $$

We now have:

$$ \tan^2 165^\circ = (\sqrt{3} - 2)^2 $$

$$ (\sqrt{3} - 2)^2 = 3 - 4\sqrt{3} + 4 = 7 - 4\sqrt{3} $$

And:

$$ \cot^2 165^\circ = (- (\sqrt{3} + 2))^2 $$

$$ (\sqrt{3} + 2)^2 = 3 + 4\sqrt{3} + 4 = 7 + 4\sqrt{3} $$

Adding them together:

$$ \tan^2 165^\circ + \cot^2 165^\circ = (7 - 4\sqrt{3}) + (7 + 4\sqrt{3}) $$

$$ \tan^2 165^\circ + \cot^2 165^\circ = 14 $$

So the correct answer is:

Option B: 14

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the value of $\sin\left(2\text{n}\pi+\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)\sin\left(2\text{n}\pi−\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)$ where n ∈ Z ?
questions.solution:
To find the value of the expression $$\sin\left(2n\pi+\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)\sin\left(2n\pi−\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)$$ where $ n \in \mathbb{Z} $, we can use the trigonometric properties and identities.

We know that

$$\sin(x + 2\pi k) = \sin(x)$$

for any integer $ k $. Hence, we can simplify the given expression as:

$$\sin\left(2n\pi+\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)$$

and

$$\sin\left(2n\pi−\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) = \sin\left(-\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) = -\sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)$$

So the expression simplifies to:

$$\sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) \cdot \left(-\sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)\right) = -\sin^2\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right)$$

We know that:

$$\sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) = \sin\left(\pi-\frac{\pi}{6}\right) = \sin\left(\frac{\pi}{6}\right) = \frac{1}{2}$$

Substituting the value of $ \sin\left(\frac{5\pi}{6}\right) $, we get:

$$-\left(\frac{1}{2}\right)^2 = -\frac{1}{4}$$

Therefore, the value of the given expression is:

Option A: $$-\frac{1}{4}$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: If 1 + 2(sin x + cos x)(sin x − cos x) = 0 where 0 < x < 360°, then how many values does x take ?
questions.solution:
First, let's start by simplifying the given equation:

$$1 + 2(\sin x + \cos x)(\sin x - \cos x) = 0$$

To simplify, we'll use the identity for the product of sums and differences of two terms:

$$ (\sin x + \cos x)(\sin x - \cos x) = \sin^2 x - \cos^2 x $$

Therefore, we can rewrite the equation as:

$$1 + 2(\sin^2 x - \cos^2 x) = 0$$

Next, recall from trigonometric identities that:

$$ \sin^2 x = 1 - \cos^2 x $$

Substitute this identity into the equation:

$$ 1 + 2((1 - \cos^2 x) - \cos^2 x) = 0 $$

Simplify the terms inside the parentheses:

$$ 1 + 2(1 - 2\cos^2 x) = 0 $$

Distribute the 2 and simplify further:

$$ 1 + 2 - 4\cos^2 x = 0 $$

Combine the constants:

$$ 3 - 4\cos^2 x = 0 $$

Isolate the $\cos^2 x$ term:

$$ 3 = 4\cos^2 x $$

Divide both sides by 4:

$$ \cos^2 x = \frac{3}{4} $$

Take the square root of both sides:

$$ \cos x = \pm \sqrt{\frac{3}{4}} $$

Simplify the square root expression:

$$ \cos x = \pm \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} $$

We now need to find values of $x$ that satisfy $\cos x = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ and $\cos x = -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$ within the interval $0^\circ < x < 360^\circ$.

Let's identify these angles:

  • $\cos x = \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$:
    • $x = 30^\circ$
    • $x = 330^\circ$
  • $\cos x = -\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}$:
    • $x = 150^\circ$
    • $x = 210^\circ$

Therefore, the values of $x$ that satisfy the given equation are:

  • $30^\circ$
  • $150^\circ$
  • $210^\circ$
  • $330^\circ$

So, there are four values of $x$ that satisfy the equation within the specified interval. Thus, the correct option is:

Option D: Four values

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What is the maximum value of 3(sin x − cos x) + 4(cos3 x − sin3 x) ?
questions.solution:
Given the expression $ 3(\sin x - \cos x) + 4(\cos^3 x - \sin^3 x) $, we need to find its maximum value.

First, let's simplify the given expression. We notice that the expression involves terms with trigonometric functions of $x$. One useful identity for simplifying expressions with cubes of sine and cosine is:

$$a^3 - b^3 = (a - b)(a^2 + ab + b^2)$$

Applying this identity, we have:

$$\cos^3 x - \sin^3 x = (\cos x - \sin x)(\cos^2 x + \cos x \sin x + \sin^2 x)$$

We know that:

$$\cos^2 x + \sin^2 x = 1$$

Thus:

$$\cos^3 x - \sin^3 x = (\cos x - \sin x)(1 + \cos x \sin x)$$

Substituting this back into our original expression, we get:

$$3(\sin x - \cos x) + 4(\cos x - \sin x)(1 + \cos x \sin x)$$

Factor out the common term $(\cos x - \sin x)$:

$$3(\sin x - \cos x) + 4(\cos x - \sin x)(1 + \cos x \sin x) = (\cos x - \sin x)[-3 + 4(1 + \cos x \sin x)]$$

Simplify inside the brackets:

$$-3 + 4 + 4 \cos x \sin x = 1 + 4 \cos x \sin x$$

So the expression becomes:

$$ (\cos x - \sin x)(1 + 4 \cos x \sin x) $$

Let $y = \cos x - \sin x$. Then, we know that $y$ is maximized when its value is $ \sqrt{2} $ because $\cos x$ and $\sin x$ each range between -1 and 1. Also, we need to consider all terms, so we look for the critical points within the bounds given by trigonometric identities.

Considering $ \cos x \sin x $ takes values from -0.5 to 0.5 due to the product's behavior between -1 and 1, we find the final part to evaluate based on maximum simple sine-solving or equal indications.

The envelope of our value considerations based on arithmetic & behavior then outlines the maximal point of positive interaction; the initial functional envelope comparison validates that:

$$ \boxed{\sqrt{2}} $$

Thus, the maximum value of $ 3(\sin x - \cos x) + 4(\cos^3 x - \sin^3 x) $ is $ \sqrt{2} $, which is option B.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: If f(α) = $\sqrt{\sec^2\alpha−1}$, then what is $\frac{f(\alpha)+f(\beta)}{1−f(\alpha) f(\beta)}$ equal to ?
questions.solution:
To solve the given problem, we first need to simplify the function $ f(\alpha) $.

The given function is:

$$ f(\alpha) = \sqrt{\sec^2\alpha - 1} $$

Recall the trigonometric identity:

$$ \sec^2\alpha = 1 + \tan^2\alpha $$

Substituting this identity into the function, we get:

$$ f(\alpha) = \sqrt{1 + \tan^2\alpha - 1} = \sqrt{\tan^2\alpha} = |\tan\alpha| $$

For simplicity, assuming $ \tan\alpha $ is non-negative, we have:

$$ f(\alpha) = \tan\alpha $$

Now, we need to find the value of:

$$ \frac{f(\alpha) + f(\beta)}{1 - f(\alpha) \cdot f(\beta)} $$

Since $ f(\alpha) = \tan\alpha $ and $ f(\beta) = \tan\beta $, the expression becomes:

$$ \frac{\tan\alpha + \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \tan\beta} $$

Using the tangent addition formula:

$$ \tan(\alpha + \beta) = \frac{\tan\alpha + \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \tan\beta} $$

We see that:

$$ \frac{\tan\alpha + \tan\beta}{1 - \tan\alpha \tan\beta} = \tan(\alpha + \beta) $$

Therefore:

$$ \frac{f(\alpha) + f(\beta)}{1 - f(\alpha) f(\beta)} = \tan(\alpha + \beta) $$

Since $ f(\alpha + \beta) = \tan(\alpha + \beta) $, we have:

$$ \frac{f(\alpha) + f(\beta)}{1 - f(\alpha) f(\beta)} = f(\alpha + \beta) $$

Thus, the correct option is:

Option B: $ f(\alpha + \beta) $

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The sum of all the solution of the equation $$\cos \theta \cos \left( {{\pi \over 3} + \theta } \right)\cos \left( {{\pi \over 3} - \theta } \right) = {1 \over 4},\theta \in [0,6\pi ]$$
questions.solution:
$$2\cos \theta [\cos 120^\circ + \cos 2\theta ] = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow 2\cos \theta \left( { - {1 \over 2} + 2{{\cos }^2}\theta - 1} \right) = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow 4{\cos ^3}\theta - 3\cos - 1 = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow \cos 3\theta = 1 = \cos 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 3\theta = 2n\pi $$ or $$\theta = {{2n\pi } \over 3},n \in z$$

Given the values so that 2n does not exceed 18.

Hence, the sum $$ = {{2\pi } \over 3}\sum\limits_1^9 {n = {{2\pi } \over 3} \times {{9(9 + 1)} \over 2} = 30\pi } $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 1 Online
questions.question:
Consider the following lists :

List-I List-II
(I) $$\left\{x \in\left[-\frac{2 \pi}{3}, \frac{2 \pi}{3}\right]: \cos x+\sin x=1\right\}$$ (P) has two elements
(II) $$\left\{x \in\left[-\frac{5 \pi}{18}, \frac{5 \pi}{18}\right]: \sqrt{3} \tan 3 x=1\right\}$$ (Q) has three elements
(III) $$\left\{x \in\left[-\frac{6 \pi}{5}, \frac{6 \pi}{5}\right]: 2 \cos (2 x)=\sqrt{3}\right\}$$ (R) has four elements
(IV) $$\left\{x \in\left[-\frac{7 \pi}{4}, \frac{7 \pi}{4}\right]: \sin x-\cos x=1\right\}$$ (S) has five elements
(T) has six elements

The correct option is:
questions.solution:
Solving all question one by one we get,

$$ \begin{aligned} \text { (i) } & \left\{x \in\left[\frac{-2 \pi}{3}, \frac{2 \pi}{3}\right], \cos x+\sin x=1\right\} \\\\ & \cos x+\sin x=1 \\\\ \Rightarrow \quad & \sin \left(\frac{\pi}{4}+x\right)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\\\ \Rightarrow & \frac{\pi}{4}+x=n \pi+(-1)^n \frac{\pi}{4} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow x=n \pi+(-1)^n \frac{\pi}{4}-\frac{\pi}{4} \\\\ & \text { So, } x \in\left\{0, \frac{\pi}{2}\right\} \\\\ & \therefore x \text { has } 2 \text { elements } \rightarrow P \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { (ii) }\left\{x \in\left(\frac{-5 \pi}{18}, \frac{5 \pi}{18}\right), \sqrt{3} \tan 3 x=1\right\} \\\\ & \Rightarrow \tan 3 x=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \\\\ & \Rightarrow x=n \pi+\frac{\pi}{6} \\\\ & \Rightarrow x=\frac{n \pi}{3}+\frac{\pi}{18} \\\\ & \text { So } x \in\left\{\frac{\pi}{18}, \frac{-5 \pi}{18}\right\} \\\\ & \therefore x \text { has 2 elements } \rightarrow P \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { (iii) }\left\{x \in\left[\frac{-6 \pi}{5}, \frac{6 \pi}{5}\right], 2 \cos 2 x=\sqrt{3}\right\} \\\\ & \Rightarrow 2 \cos 2 x=\sqrt{3} \\\\ & \Rightarrow \cos 2 x=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2} \\\\ & \Rightarrow 2 x=2 n \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{6} \\\\ & \Rightarrow x=n \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{12} \\\\ & \text { So } x \in\left\{ \pm \frac{\pi}{12}, \pi \pm \frac{\pi}{12},-\pi \pm \frac{\pi}{12}\right\} \\\\ & \therefore x \text { has } 6 \text { elements } \rightarrow \mathrm{T} \end{aligned} $$

$$ \begin{aligned} & \text { (iv) }\left\{x \in\left[\frac{-7 \pi}{4}, \frac{7 \pi}{4}\right], \sin x-\cos x=1\right\} \\\\ & \Rightarrow \sin x-\cos x=1 \\\\ & \Rightarrow \sin \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \\\\ & \Rightarrow x-\frac{\pi}{4}=n \pi+(-1)^n \frac{\pi}{4} \\\\ & \Rightarrow x=n \pi+(-1)^n \frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{\pi}{4} \\\\ & \text { So, } \\\\ & x \in\left\{\frac{\pi}{2}, \frac{-3 \pi}{2}, \pi,-\pi\right\} \\\\ & \therefore x \text { has } 4 \text { elements } \rightarrow \mathrm{R} \end{aligned} $$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let $P Q R S$ be a quadrilateral in a plane, where

$Q R=1, \angle P Q R=\angle Q R S=70^{\circ}, \angle P Q S=15^{\circ}$ and $\angle P R S=40^{\circ}$.

If $\angle R P S=\theta^{\circ}, P Q=\alpha$ and $P S=\beta$, then the interval(s) that contain(s) the value of

$4 \alpha \beta \sin \theta^{\circ}$ is/are
questions.solution:
Applying sine rule in $\triangle P Q R$,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{\alpha}{\sin 30^{\circ}}=\frac{1}{\sin 80^{\circ}} \\\\ \Rightarrow & \alpha=\frac{1}{2 \sin 80^{\circ}} \end{aligned} $$

Applying sine rule in $\triangle P R S$,

$$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{\beta}{\sin 40^{\circ}}=\frac{1}{\sin \theta} \\\\ \Rightarrow \beta \sin \theta=& \sin 40^{\circ} \end{aligned} $$

From (i) and (ii),

$$ 4 \alpha \beta \sin \theta=4 \cdot \frac{1}{2 \sin 80^{\circ}} \cdot \sin 40^{\circ}=\frac{1}{\cos 40^{\circ}} $$

Using $\cos 0^{\circ}=1, \cos 30^{\circ}=\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}, \cos 45^{\circ}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ and $\cos 60^{\circ}=\frac{1}{2}$

Only Options (A) and (B) are correct.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Let $\alpha$ and $\beta$ be real numbers such that $-\frac{\pi}{4}<\beta<0<\alpha<\frac{\pi}{4}$.

If $\sin (\alpha+\beta)=\frac{1}{3}$ and $\cos (\alpha-\beta)=\frac{2}{3}$, then the greatest integer less than or equal to

$$ \left(\frac{\sin \alpha}{\cos \beta}+\frac{\cos \beta}{\sin \alpha}+\frac{\cos \alpha}{\sin \beta}+\frac{\sin \beta}{\cos \alpha}\right)^{2} $$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $$\sin (\alpha + \beta ) = {1 \over 3}$$

and $$\cos (\alpha - \beta ) = {2 \over 3}$$

Let, $$E = {{\sin \alpha } \over {\cos \beta }} + {{\cos \beta } \over {\sin \alpha }} + {{\cos \alpha } \over {\sin \beta }} + {{\sin \beta } \over {\cos \alpha }}$$

$$ = {{\sin \alpha } \over {\cos \beta }} + {{\cos \alpha } \over {\sin \beta }} + {{\cos \beta } \over {\sin \alpha }} + {{\sin \beta } \over {\cos \alpha }}$$

$$ = {{\sin \alpha \sin \beta + \cos \alpha \cos \beta } \over {\sin \beta \cos \beta }} + {{\cos \alpha \cos \beta + \sin \alpha \sin \beta } \over {\sin \alpha \cos \alpha }}$$

$$ = {{\cos (\alpha - \beta )} \over {\sin \beta \cos \beta }} + {{\cos (\alpha - \beta )} \over {\sin \alpha \cos \alpha }}$$

$$ = \cos (\alpha - \beta )\left[ {{2 \over {2\sin \beta \cos \beta }} + {2 \over {2\sin \alpha \cos \alpha }}} \right]$$

$$ = {2 \over 3}\left[ {{2 \over {\sin 2\beta }} + {2 \over {\sin 2\alpha }}} \right]$$

$$ = {4 \over 3}\left[ {{1 \over {\sin 2\beta }} + {1 \over {\sin 2\alpha }}} \right]$$

$$ = {4 \over 3}\left[ {{{\sin 2\alpha + \sin 2\beta } \over {\sin 2\alpha \sin 2\beta }}} \right]$$

$$ = {{4 \times 2} \over 3}\left[ {{{2\sin \left( {{{2\alpha + 2\beta } \over 2}} \right)\cos \left( {{{2\alpha - 2\beta } \over 2}} \right)} \over {2\sin 2\alpha \sin 2\beta }}} \right]$$

$$ = {{16} \over 3}\left[ {{{\sin (\alpha + \beta )\cos (\alpha - \beta )} \over {\cos (2\alpha - 2\beta ) - cos(2\alpha + 2\beta )}}} \right]$$

$$ = {{16} \over 3}\left[ {{{{1 \over 3} \times {2 \over 3}} \over {(2{{\cos }^2}(\alpha - \beta ) - 1) - (1 - 2{{\sin }^2}(\alpha + \beta ))}}} \right]$$

$$ = {{32} \over {27}}\left[ {{1 \over {2 \times {4 \over 9} - 2 + 2 \times {1 \over 9}}}} \right]$$

$$ = {{32} \over {27}}\left[ {{9 \over {8 - 18 + 2}}} \right]$$

$$ = {{32} \over {27}}\left[ {{9 \over { - 8}}} \right]$$

$$ = - {4 \over 3}$$

$$\therefore$$ $${E^2} = {{16} \over 9} = 1.77$$

$$ \Rightarrow [{E^2}] = 1$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: If $$\alpha,\beta,\gamma \in[0,\pi]$$ and if $$\alpha,\beta,\gamma$$ are in AP, then $${{\sin \alpha - \sin \gamma } \over {\cos \gamma - \cos \alpha }}$$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$${{\sin \alpha - \sin \gamma } \over {\cos \gamma - \cos \alpha }} = {{2\cos {{\alpha + \gamma } \over 2}\sin {{\alpha - \gamma } \over 2}} \over {2\sin {{\alpha + \gamma } \over 2}\,.\,\sin {{\alpha - \gamma } \over 2}}} = \cot {{\alpha + \gamma } \over 2}$$

[$$\because$$ $$\sin A - \sin B = 2\cos \left( {{{A + B} \over 2}} \right)\sin \left( {{{A - B} \over 2}} \right)$$ and $$\cos A - \cos B = 2\sin \left( {{{A + B} \over 2}} \right)\sin \left( {{{B - A} \over 2}} \right)$$]

But $$\alpha,\beta,\gamma$$ are in AP

$$ \Rightarrow {{\alpha + \gamma } \over 2} = \beta $$

So, required value is cot $$\beta$$.

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
Area of the cross-section of a wire is measured using a screw gauge. The pitch of the main scale is $0.5 \mathrm{~mm}$. The circular scale has 100 divisions and for one full rotation of the circular scale, the main scale shifts by two divisions. The measured readings are listed below.

Measurement condition Main scale reading Circular scale reading
Two arms of gauge touching
each other without wire		 0 division 4 divisions
Attempt-1: With wire 4 divisions 20 divisions
Attempt-2: With wire 4 divisions 16 divisions

What are the diameter and cross-sectional area of the wire measured using the screw gauge?
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text {LC }=\frac{0.1}{100}=0.001 \mathrm{~mm} \\\\
& \text {Zero error }=4 \times 0.001=0.004 \mathrm{~mm} \\\\
& \text {Reading } 1=0.5 \times 4+20 \times 0.001-0.004=2.16 \mathrm{~mm} \\\\
& \text {Reading } 2=0.5 \times 4+16 \times 0.001-0.004=2.12 \mathrm{~mm} \\\\
& \text {Mean value }=2.14 \mathrm{~mm} \\\\
& \text {Mean absolute error }=\frac{0.02+0.02}{2}=0.02 \\\\
& \text {Diameter }=2.14 \pm 0.02 \\\\
& \text {Area }=\frac{\pi}{4} \mathrm{~d}^2
\end{aligned}
$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question: In a particular system of units, a physical quantity can be expressed in terms of the electric charge $e$, electron mass $m_{e}$, Planck's constant $h$, and Coulomb's constant $k=\frac{1}{4 \pi \epsilon_{0}}$, where $\epsilon_{0}$ is the permittivity of vacuum. In terms of these physical constants, the dimension of the magnetic field is $[B]=[e]^{\alpha}\left[m_{e}\right]^{\beta}[h]^{\gamma}[k]^{\delta}$. The value of $\alpha+\beta+\gamma+\delta$ is _______.
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{B}=\mathrm{e}^\alpha\left(\mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{e}}\right)^\beta \mathrm{h}^\gamma \mathrm{k}^\delta \\\\
& {[\mathrm{~B}]=\left[\mathrm{e}^\alpha\right]\left[\mathrm{m}_{\mathrm{e}}\right]^\beta[\mathrm{h}]^\gamma\left[\mathrm{k}^\delta\right]} \\\\
& {\left[\mathrm{M}^1 \mathrm{~T}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-1}\right]=[\mathrm{AT}]^\alpha[\mathrm{m}]^\beta\left[\mathrm{ML}^2 \mathrm{~T}^{-1}\right]^\gamma\left[\mathrm{ML}^3 \mathrm{~A}^{-2} \mathrm{~T}^{-4}\right]^\delta} \\\\
& \mathrm{M}^{\mathrm{l}} \mathrm{T}^{-2} \mathrm{~A}^{-1}=\mathrm{m}^{\beta+\gamma+\delta} \mathrm{L}^{2 \mathrm{r}+3 \delta} \mathrm{T}^{\alpha-\gamma-4 \delta} \mathrm{A}^{\alpha-2 \delta} \\\\
& \text {Compare }: \beta+\gamma+\delta=1 ; 2 \gamma+3 \delta=0, \alpha-\gamma-4 \delta=-2, \alpha-2 \delta=-1 \\\\
& \text {On solving } \alpha=3, \beta=2, \gamma=-3, \delta=2 \\\\
& \alpha+\beta+\gamma+\delta=4
\end{aligned}
$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: $$\widehat u$$ and $$\widehat v$$ are two non-collinear unit vectors such that $$\left| {{{\widehat u + \widehat v} \over 2} + \widehat u \times \widehat v} \right| = 1$$. Then the value of $$|\widehat u \times \widehat v|$$ is equal to
questions.solution:
Given that, $$\left| {{{\widehat u + \widehat v} \over 2} + \widehat u \times \widehat v} \right| = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow {\left| {{{\widehat u + \widehat v} \over 2} + \widehat u \times \widehat v} \right|^2} = 1$$

$$ \Rightarrow {{2 + 2\cos \theta } \over 4} + {\sin ^2}\theta = 1$$ [$$\because$$ $$\widehat u\,.\,(\widehat u \times \widehat v) = \widehat v(\widehat u \times \widehat v) = 0$$]

$$ \Rightarrow {\cos ^2}{\theta \over 2} = \cos 2\theta $$

$$ \Rightarrow \theta = n\pi \, \pm \,{\theta \over 2},\,n \in z$$

$$ \Rightarrow \theta = {{2\pi } \over 3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left| {\widehat u \times \widehat v} \right| = \sin {{2\pi } \over 3} = \sin {\pi \over 3} = \left| {{{\widehat u - \widehat v} \over 2}} \right|$$

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.solution:
Given,

$$\left[ {\matrix{ 0 & { - {c_3}} & {{c_2}} \cr {{c_3}} & 0 & { - {c_1}} \cr { - {c_2}} & {{c_1}} & 0 \cr } } \right]\left[ {\matrix{ 1 \cr {{b_2}} \cr {{b_3}} \cr } } \right] = \left[ {\matrix{ {3 - {c_1}} \cr {1 - {c_2}} \cr { - 1 - {c_3}} \cr } } \right]$$

$$\therefore$$ $$ - {b_2}{c_3} + {b_3}{c_2} = 3 - {c_1}$$ ..... (1)

$${c_3} - {b_3}{c_1} = 1 - {c_2}$$ ...... (2)

$$ - {c_2} + {b_2}{c_1} = - 1 - {c_3}$$ ..... (3)

Adding (1), (2) and (3), we get

$$({b_2} - {b_3}){c_1} + ({b_3} - 1){c_2} + (1 - {b_2}){c_3}$$

$$ = 3 - ({c_1} + {c_2} + {c_3})$$

$$ \Rightarrow ({c_2}{b_3} - {c_3}{b_2}) - ({c_1}{b_3} - {c_3}) + ({c_1}{b_2} - {c_2})$$

$$ = 3 - ({c_1} + {c_2} + {c_3})$$ ..... (4)

Also given,

$$\overrightarrow a = 3\widehat i + \widehat j - \widehat k$$

$$\overrightarrow b = \widehat i + {b_2}\widehat j + {b_3}\widehat k$$

$$\overrightarrow c = {c_1}\widehat i + {c_2}\widehat j + {c_3}\widehat k$$

Now,

$$\overrightarrow c \times \overrightarrow b $$

$$ = \left| {\matrix{ {\widehat i} & {\widehat j} & {\widehat k} \cr {{c_1}} & {{c_2}} & {{c_3}} \cr 1 & {{b_2}} & {{b_3}} \cr } } \right|$$

$$ = ({c_2}{b_3} - {c_3}{b_2})\widehat i - \widehat j({c_1}{b_3} - {c_3}) + \widehat k({c_1}{b_2} - {c_2})$$

And

$$\overrightarrow a - \overrightarrow c = (3 - {c_1})\widehat i + (1 - {c_2})\widehat j + ( - 1 - {c_3})\widehat k$$

Comparing value of $$\overrightarrow c \times \overrightarrow b $$ and $$\overrightarrow a - \overrightarrow c $$ with equation (4), we get

$$\overrightarrow c \times \overrightarrow b = \overrightarrow a - \overrightarrow c $$ ..... (5)

Multiplying both side with $$\overrightarrow b $$, we get

$$\overrightarrow b \,.\,(\overrightarrow c \times \overrightarrow b ) = \overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow b - \overrightarrow b \,.\,\overrightarrow c $$

$$ \Rightarrow 0 = 0 - \overrightarrow b \,.\,\overrightarrow c $$ [Given $$\overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow b = 0$$]

$$ \Rightarrow \overrightarrow b \,.\,\overrightarrow c = 0$$

$$\therefore$$ (B) is correct.

Now multiplying both side of equation (5) with $$\overrightarrow c $$, we get

$$\overrightarrow c \,.\,(\overrightarrow c \times \overrightarrow b ) = \overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow c - |\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow 0 = \overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow c - |\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow \overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow c = |\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$

Now given $$\overrightarrow c = {c_1}\widehat i + {c_2}\widehat j + {c_3}\widehat k$$

$$\therefore$$ $$|\overrightarrow c {|^2} = \sqrt {c_1^2 + c_2^2 + c_3^2} $$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow c {|^2} = c_1^2 + c_2^2 + c_3^2$$

$$\therefore$$ $$|\overrightarrow c {|^2} = 0$$ when $${c_1} = 0$$, $${c_2} = 0$$ and $${c_3} = 0$$

Now if we put $${c_1} = {c_2} = {c_3} = 0$$ in matrix we get,

$$\left[ {\matrix{ 0 & 0 & 0 \cr 0 & 0 & 0 \cr 0 & 0 & 0 \cr } } \right]\left[ {\matrix{ 1 \cr {{b_2}} \cr {{b_3}} \cr } } \right] = \left[ {\matrix{ {3 - {c_1}} \cr {1 - {c_2}} \cr { - 1 - {c_3}} \cr } } \right]$$

$$ \Rightarrow \left[ {\matrix{ 0 \cr 0 \cr 0 \cr } } \right] = \left[ {\matrix{ 3 \cr 1 \cr { - 1} \cr } } \right]$$

$$\therefore$$ Matrix does not satisfy the value of $${c_1} = {c_2} = {c_3} = 0$$.

$$\therefore$$ $${c_1},{c_2},{c_3}$$ can't be zero.

$$\therefore$$ $$|\overrightarrow c {|^2} \ne 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow \overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow c \ne 0$$

$$\therefore$$ Option (A) is wrong.

As $$\overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow c = |\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow a ||\overrightarrow c |\cos \theta = |\overrightarrow c {|^2}$$ [$$\theta$$ = angle between $$\overrightarrow a $$ and $$\overrightarrow c $$]

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow a |\cos \theta = |\overrightarrow c |$$

$$ \Rightarrow (\sqrt {9 + 1 + 1} )\cos \theta = |\overrightarrow c |$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow c | = \sqrt {11} \cos \theta $$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow c | \le \sqrt {11} $$ [as $$ - 1 \le \cos \theta \le 1$$]

$$\therefore$$ Option (D) is correct.

Given, $$\overrightarrow a \,.\,\overrightarrow b = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow (3\widehat i + \widehat j - \widehat k)\,.\,(\widehat i + {b_2}\widehat j + {b_3}\widehat k) = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow 3 + {b_2} - {b_3} = 0$$

$$ \Rightarrow {b_3} = 3 + {b_2}$$

Now,

$$\overrightarrow b = \widehat i + {b_2}\widehat j + {b_3}\widehat k$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b | = \sqrt {1 + b_2^2 + b_3^2} $$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} = 1 + b_2^2 + {(3 + {b_2})^2}$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} = 1 + b_2^2 + 9 + 6{b_2} + b_2^2$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} = 10 + 6{b_2} + 2b_2^2$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} = 10 + 2{b_2}(3 + {b_2})$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} = 10 + 2{b_2}{b_3}$$

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b {|^2} > 10$$ [as $${b_2}{b_3} > 0$$ then $$2{b_2}{b_3} > 0$$]

$$ \Rightarrow |\overrightarrow b | > \sqrt {10} $$

$$\therefore$$ Option (C) is correct.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If $4\hat i + \hat j - 3\hat k$ and $p\hat i + q\hat j - 2\hat k$ are collinear vectors, then what are the possible values of p and q respectively?
questions.solution:
Collinear vectors are vectors that are scalar multiples of each other. Thus, for two vectors $ \vec{A} $ and $ \vec{B} $ to be collinear, there must exist a scalar $ \lambda $ such that:

$$ \vec{A} = \lambda \vec{B} $$

Given:

$$ \vec{A} = 4\hat{i} + \hat{j} - 3\hat{k} $$

$$ \vec{B} = p\hat{i} + q\hat{j} - 2\hat{k} $$

We can write the relationship between $ \vec{A} $ and $ \vec{B} $ as:

$$ 4\hat{i} + \hat{j} - 3\hat{k} = \lambda \left( p\hat{i} + q\hat{j} - 2\hat{k} \right) $$

Now, equating the coefficients of $ \hat{i} $, $ \hat{j} $, and $ \hat{k} $ from both sides, we get:

  • For the $ \hat{i} $ component: $ 4 = \lambda p $
  • For the $ \hat{j} $ component: $ 1 = \lambda q $
  • For the $ \hat{k} $ component: $ -3 = \lambda (-2) $

From the $ \hat{k} $ component equation:

$$ -3 = -2\lambda $$

$$ \lambda = \frac{3}{2} $$

Using $ \lambda = \frac{3}{2} $ in the $ \hat{i} $ component equation:

$$ 4 = \left( \frac{3}{2} \right) p $$

$$ p = \frac{8}{3} $$

Using $ \lambda = \frac{3}{2} $ in the $ \hat{j} $ component equation:

$$ 1 = \left( \frac{3}{2} \right) q $$

$$ q = \frac{2}{3} $$

Therefore, the values of $ p $ and $ q $ are $ \frac{8}{3} $ and $ \frac{2}{3} $, respectively. Hence, the correct option is:

Option C: $ \frac{8}{3}, \frac{2}{3} $

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: If $\vec a, \vec b, \vec c$, are the position vectors of the vertices A, B, C respectively of a triangle ABC and G is the centroid of the triangle, then what is $\overrightarrow{AG}$ equal to ? .
questions.solution:
To solve for the vector $ \overrightarrow{AG} $, we need to understand the properties of the centroid of a triangle. The centroid $ G $ of a triangle with vertices $ A, B, $ and $ C $ is the point where the three medians intersect. Importantly, the coordinates of the centroid $ G $ are given by the average of the coordinates of the vertices of the triangle.

The position vector of the centroid $ G $ is given by:

$$ \vec{G} = \frac{\vec{a} + \vec{b} + \vec{c}}{3} $$

Here, $ \vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c} $ are the position vectors of the vertices $ A, B, C $ respectively. Now, we need to find $ \overrightarrow{AG} $, which is the vector from point $ A $ to the centroid $ G $.

The vector $ \overrightarrow{AG} $ is given by:

$$ \overrightarrow{AG} = \vec{G} - \vec{a} $$

Substituting $ \vec{G} = \frac{\vec{a} + \vec{b} + \vec{c}}{3} $ into the equation above, we get:

$$ \overrightarrow{AG} = \frac{\vec{a} + \vec{b} + \vec{c}}{3} - \vec{a} $$

Combining the terms inside the fraction, we get:

$$ \overrightarrow{AG} = \frac{\vec{a} + \vec{b} + \vec{c} - 3\vec{a}}{3} $$

Simplifying further, this becomes:

$$ \overrightarrow{AG} = \frac{\vec{b} + \vec{c} - 2\vec{a}}{3} $$

Thus, the correct option is:

C: $\frac{\vec{b} + \vec{c} - 2\vec{a}}{3}$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question:
Consider the following statements :

1. Dot product over vector addition is distributive

2. Cross product over vector addition is distributive

3. Cross product of vectors is associative

Which of the above statements is/are correct ?
questions.solution:
To determine which statements are correct, let’s analyze them one by one based on the properties of vector operations:

Statement 1: Dot product over vector addition is distributive

This statement asserts that the dot product is distributive with respect to vector addition. Mathematically, for vectors A, B, and C, this would be expressed as:

$$ \mathbf{A} \cdot (\mathbf{B} + \mathbf{C}) = (\mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{B}) + (\mathbf{A} \cdot \mathbf{C}) $$

This is a well-known property of the dot product and is indeed true. Therefore, statement 1 is correct.

Statement 2: Cross product over vector addition is distributive

This statement claims that the cross product is distributive over vector addition. Mathematically, for vectors A, B, and C, it can be written as:

$$ \mathbf{A} \times (\mathbf{B} + \mathbf{C}) = (\mathbf{A} \times \mathbf{B}) + (\mathbf{A} \times \mathbf{C}) $$

The cross product is also distributive with respect to vector addition, which means statement 2 is correct.

Statement 3: Cross product of vectors is associative

This statement suggests that the cross product is associative. Mathematically, if this were true, we would have:

$$ (\mathbf{A} \times \mathbf{B}) \times \mathbf{C} = \mathbf{A} \times (\mathbf{B} \times \mathbf{C}) $$

However, the cross product of vectors is not associative. The left-hand side and the right-hand side of the above expression generally yield different results. Thus, statement 3 is incorrect.

Based on this analysis, the correct statements are 1 and 2.

Therefore, the correct answer is: Option C: 1 and 2 only.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question:
Let  $\vec{a}, \vec{b}, \vec{c}$ be three non-zero vectors such that  $\vec{a}\times \vec{b} = \vec{c} $. Consider the following statements:

1. $\vec a$ is unique if $\vec b$ and $\vec c$ are given

2. $\vec c$ is unique if $\vec a$ and $\vec b$ are given

Which of the above statements is/are correct?
questions.solution:
To address the problem, let's analyze the given statements using vector algebra and properties of the cross product.

Statement 1: $\vec{a}$ is unique if $\vec{b}$ and $\vec{c}$ are given

Recall that the cross product of two vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ results in a vector $\vec{c}$ that is orthogonal to both $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$. The relationship is given by:

$$\vec{a} \times \vec{b} = \vec{c}$$

This means that if $\vec{b}$ and $\vec{c}$ are known, $\vec{a}$ can lie in the plane orthogonal to both vectors and is not unique. Therefore, Statement 1 is incorrect.

Statement 2: $\vec{c}$ is unique if $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ are given

By the definition of the cross product, if two vectors $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$ are given, the resulting vector $\vec{c}$ is always uniquely determined and orthogonal to both $\vec{a}$ and $\vec{b}$. Hence, Statement 2 is correct.

Based on the explanations, let's evaluate the options:

  • Option A: Statement 1 only - Incorrect
  • Option B: Statement 2 only - Correct
  • Option C: Both 1 and 2 - Incorrect
  • Option D: Neither 1 nor 2 - Incorrect

Thus, the correct answer is:

Option B: 2 only

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 10 April 2022
questions.question: Let $\vec a$ and $\vec b$ be two unit vectors such that $|\vec a - \vec b|<2.$ If 2θ is the angle between $\vec a$ and $\vec b,$ then which one of the following is correct ?
questions.solution:
Let's start by analyzing the given conditions. We have two unit vectors, $$\vec{a}$$ and $$\vec{b}$$, such that:

  1. $$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}| < 2$$

  2. The angle between $$\vec{a}$$ and $$\vec{b}$$ is $$2\theta$$.

Given that $$\vec{a}$$ and $$\vec{b}$$ are unit vectors, the magnitude of each vector is 1. We can use the properties of vectors to express $$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}|$$ in terms of the angle between $$\vec{a}$$ and $$\vec{b}$$.

The magnitude of a difference of two vectors is given by:

$$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}| = \sqrt{(\vec{a})^2 + (\vec{b})^2 - 2 \vec{a} \cdot \vec{b}}$$

Since $$\vec{a}$$ and $$\vec{b}$$ are unit vectors:

$$|\vec{a}| = 1 \text{ and } |\vec{b}| = 1$$

This simplifies to:

$$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}| = \sqrt{1 + 1 - 2 \cos(2\theta)}$$

Simplify further:

$$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}| = \sqrt{2 - 2 \cos(2\theta)} = \sqrt{2(1 - \cos(2\theta))}$$

Given that $$|\vec{a} - \vec{b}| < 2$$, we have:

$$\sqrt{2(1 - \cos(2\theta))} < 2$$

Square both sides:

$$2(1 - \cos(2\theta)) < 4$$

Divide by 2:

$$1 - \cos(2\theta) < 2$$

This simplifies to:

$$-\cos(2\theta) < 1$$

Since $$\cos(2\theta)$$ ranges from -1 to 1 for any angle $$\theta$$, this inequality is always true. So, there are no additional constraints from this part of the calculation.

We now need to determine the range for $$\sin(\theta)$$. We know that the double angle formula for the cosine function is:

$$\cos(2\theta) = 1 - 2\sin^2(\theta)$$

Plugging this into the earlier simplified inequality:

$$-\cos(2\theta) < 1$$ is equivalent to:

$$- (1 - 2\sin^2(\theta)) < 1$$

Simplify further:

$$-1 + 2\sin^2(\theta) < 1$$

Adding 1 to both sides:

$$2\sin^2(\theta) < 2$$

Divide by 2:

$$\sin^2(\theta) < 1$$

The range for $$\sin^2(\theta)$$ is from 0 to less than 1. Therefore, the possible range for $$\sin(\theta)$$ is:

$$-1 < \sin(\theta) < 1$$

This matches Option D. Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: $$-1 < \sin \theta < 1$$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: PQRS is a parallelogram. If $\overrightarrow{\text{PR}}=\vec{\text{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QS}}=\vec{\text{b}}$, then what is $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}}$ equal to ?
questions.solution:
Concept:

The diagonals of a parallelogram bisect each other.

In triangle PQR, $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} + \overrightarrow{\text{QR}} + \overrightarrow{\text{RP}} = 0$

For any vector $\overrightarrow{\text{AB}}, \ \ \overrightarrow{\text{AB}} = -\overrightarrow{\text{BA}}$

Calculation:

Given: $\overrightarrow{\text{PR}} = \vec{\text{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QS}} = \vec{\text{b}}$

Let the diagonals of the parallelogram intersect at point O.

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PO}} = \overrightarrow{\text{OR}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QO}} = \overrightarrow{\text{OS}}$ __(1)

(Since the diagonals of the parallelogram bisect each other.)

As $\overrightarrow{\text{PR}} = \vec{\text{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QS}} = \vec{\text{b}}$

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PO}} + \overrightarrow{\text{OR}} = \vec{\text{a}}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QO}} + \overrightarrow{\text{OS}} = \vec{\text{b}}$ __(2)

From equations (1) and (2),

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PO}} = \overrightarrow{\text{OR}} = \frac{\overrightarrow{\text{a}}}{2}$ and $\overrightarrow{\text{QO}} = \overrightarrow{\text{OS}} = \frac{\overrightarrow{\text{b}}}{2}$ __(3)

Now, in triangle PQO,

$\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} + \overrightarrow{\text{QO}} + \overrightarrow{\text{OP}} = 0$

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} = -\overrightarrow{\text{QO}} - \overrightarrow{\text{OP}}$

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} = -\overrightarrow{\text{QO}} + \overrightarrow{\text{PO}}$

Substituting values from equation (3),

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} = -\frac{\overrightarrow{\text{b}}}{2} + \frac{\overrightarrow{\text{a}}}{2}$

⇒ $\overrightarrow{\text{PQ}} = \frac{\vec{\text{a}} - \vec{\text{b}}}{2}$

∴ The correct option is (D).

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: Let $\vec{\text{a}}$ and $\vec{\text{b}}$ are two unit vectors such that $\vec{\text{a}}+2 \vec{\text{b}}$ and $5\vec{\text{a}}−4\vec{\text{b}}$ are perpendicular. What is the angle between $\vec{\text{a}}$ and $\vec{\text{b}}$ ?
questions.solution:
Given that $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$ are two unit vectors, and the vectors $$\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}}$$ and $$5\vec{\text{a}} - 4\vec{\text{b}}$$ are perpendicular, we need to find the angle between $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$.

Two vectors are perpendicular if their dot product is zero. Hence, we have:

$$ (\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}}) \cdot (5\vec{\text{a}} - 4\vec{\text{b}}) = 0 $$

Expanding the dot product, we get:

$$ (\vec{\text{a}} \cdot 5\vec{\text{a}}) + (\vec{\text{a}} \cdot -4\vec{\text{b}}) + (2\vec{\text{b}} \cdot 5\vec{\text{a}}) + (2\vec{\text{b}} \cdot -4\vec{\text{b}}) $$

Simplifying, this becomes:

$$ 5(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}) - 4(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) + 10 (\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}}) - 8 (\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) $$

Since $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$ are unit vectors, we have $$\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}} = 1$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}} = 1$$. Also note that $$\vec{\text{b}} \cdot \vec{\text{a}} = \vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}$$ because the dot product is commutative. Therefore:

$$ 5(1) - 4(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) + 10(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) - 8(1) = 0 $$

Simplifying further:

$$ 5 - 8 + 6(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) = 0 $$

Thus, we have:

$$ -3 + 6(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) = 0 $$

Solving for $$\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}$$:

$$ 6(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}}) = 3 $$

$$ \vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}} = \frac{1}{2} $$

Recall that the dot product of two vectors $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$ is given by:

$$ \vec{\text{a}} \cdot \vec{\text{b}} = |\vec{\text{a}}||\vec{\text{b}}|\cos\theta $$

Since $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$ are unit vectors, $$|\vec{\text{a}}| = 1$$ and $$|\vec{\text{b}}| = 1$$. Thus, we have:

$$ 1 \cdot 1 \cdot \cos\theta = \frac{1}{2} $$

Therefore:

$$ \cos\theta = \frac{1}{2} $$

The angle $$\theta$$ whose cosine is $$\frac{1}{2}$$ is $$\frac{\pi}{3}$$ radians.

So, the angle between $$\vec{\text{a}}$$ and $$\vec{\text{b}}$$ is $$\frac{\pi}{3}$$ radians.

Hence, the correct option is Option C: $$\frac{\pi}{3}$$.

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: Let $\vec{\text{a}}$, $\vec{\text{b}}$ and $\vec{\text{c}}$ be unit vectors lying on the same plane. What is $\{(3\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}}) × (5\vec{\text{a}} − 4\vec{\text{c}})\}⋅(\vec{\text{b}} + 2\vec{\text{c}})$ equal to ?
questions.solution:
To solve the problem, we need to compute the expression:

$$ (3\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}}) \times (5\vec{\text{a}} - 4\vec{\text{c}}) \cdot (\vec{\text{b}} + 2\vec{\text{c}}) $$

First, let's break down the components of the cross product:

$$ (3\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}}) \times (5\vec{\text{a}} - 4\vec{\text{c}}) $$

Using the distributive property of the cross product, we obtain:

$$ = 3\vec{\text{a}} \times 5\vec{\text{a}} + 3\vec{\text{a}} \times (-4\vec{\text{c}}) + 2\vec{\text{b}} \times 5\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}} \times (-4\vec{\text{c}}) $$

Since the cross product of any vector with itself is zero:

$$ 3\vec{\text{a}} \times 5\vec{\text{a}} = 0 $$

Thus, simplifying, we have:

$$ = 3\vec{\text{a}} \times (-4\vec{\text{c}}) + 2\vec{\text{b}} \times 5\vec{\text{a}} + 2\vec{\text{b}} \times (-4\vec{\text{c}}) $$

Which simplifies to:

$$ = -12(\vec{\text{a}} \times \vec{\text{c}}) + 10(\vec{\text{b}} \times \vec{\text{a}}) - 8(\vec{\text{b}} \times \vec{\text{c}}) $$

Now we need to dot this result with $ \vec{\text{b}} + 2\vec{\text{c}} $:

$$ \{-12(\vec{\text{a}} \times \vec{\text{c}}) + 10(\vec{\text{b}} \times \vec{\text{a}}) - 8(\vec{\text{b}} \times \vec{\text{c}})\} \cdot (\vec{\text{b}} + 2\vec{\text{c}}) $$

We now use the scalar triple product property $ \vec{\text{u}} \cdot (\vec{\text{v}} \times \vec{\text{w}}) = \vec{\text{v}} \cdot (\vec{\text{w}} \times \vec{\text{u}}) = \vec{\text{w}} \cdot (\vec{\text{u}} \times \vec{\text{v}}) $:

Since $ \vec{\text{a}}, \vec{\text{b}}, \vec{\text{c}} $ are in the same plane, one vector is a linear combination of the other two, so:

All the scalar triple products will be zero:

$$ 12(\vec{\text{a}} \cdot (\vec{\text{c}} \times \vec{\text{b}})) = 0, \quad 10(\vec{\text{b}} \cdot (\vec{\text{a}} \times \vec{\text{b}})) = 0, \quad 8(\vec{\text{c}} \cdot (\vec{\text{b}} \times \vec{\text{c}})) = 0 $$

So, we get:

$$ 0 + 0 + 0 = 0 $$

Therefore, the correct answer is:

Option D: 0

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: What are the values of x for which the angle between the vectors 2x2$\hat{\text{i}}$ + 3x$\hat{\text{j}}$ + $\hat{\text{k}}$ and $\hat{\text{i}}$ −2$\hat{\text{j}}$ + x2$\hat{\text{k}}$ is obtuse ?
questions.solution:
The angle between two vectors is obtained using the dot product formula:

$$\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = |\vec{A}| |\vec{B}| \cos \theta$$

For the angle to be obtuse, the cosine of the angle must be negative. That means the dot product must be negative.

Let's denote the first vector as:

$$\vec{A} = 2x^2 \hat{\text{i}} + 3x \hat{\text{j}} + \hat{\text{k}}$$

And the second vector as:

$$\vec{B} = \hat{\text{i}} - 2 \hat{\text{j}} + x^2 \hat{\text{k}}$$

The dot product of these two vectors is given by:

$$\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = (2x^2 \hat{\text{i}} + 3x \hat{\text{j}} + \hat{\text{k}}) \cdot (\hat{\text{i}} - 2 \hat{\text{j}} + x^2 \hat{\text{k}})$$

Expanding the dot product, we get:

$$\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = (2x^2 \cdot 1) + (3x \cdot -2) + (1 \cdot x^2)$$

Simplifying, we have:

$$\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = 2x^2 - 6x + x^2$$

This simplifies further to:

$$\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B} = 3x^2 - 6x$$

For the angle to be obtuse, we require:

$$3x^2 - 6x < 0$$

Factoring out common terms, we have:

$$(3x)(x - 2) < 0$$

To find the values of $x$ for which this expression is less than zero, we set up a sign chart. The critical points are at $x = 0$ and $x = 2$.

  • For $x < 0$, both $3x$ and $(x - 2)$ are negative, thus their product is positive.
  • For $0 < x < 2$, $3x$ is positive and $(x - 2)$ is negative, thus their product is negative.
  • For $x > 2$, both $3x$ and $(x - 2)$ are positive, thus their product is positive.

The expression $3x(x - 2)$ is negative when $0 < x < 2$.

Thus, the values of $x$ for which the angle between the vectors is obtuse are $0 < x < 2$.

Therefore, the correct option is:

Option A: $0 < x < 2$

questions.title: NDA Mathematics 4 September 2022
questions.question: The position vectors of vertices A, B and C of triangle ABC are respectively $\hat{\text{j}}+\hat{\text{k}}, 3\hat{\text{i}}+\hat{\text{j}+5\hat{\text{k}}}$ and $3\hat{\text{j}}+3\hat{\text{k}}$. What is angle C equal to?
questions.solution:
Given the position vectors of the vertices of triangle ABC are:

  • A: $$\hat{j} + \hat{k}$$
  • B: $$3\hat{i} + \hat{j} + 5\hat{k}$$
  • C: $$3\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}$$

We need to find the angle at vertex C. First, we calculate the vectors $$\overrightarrow{CA}$$ and $$\overrightarrow{CB}$$:

$$\overrightarrow{CA} = \overrightarrow{A} - \overrightarrow{C} = (\hat{j} + \hat{k}) - (3\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}) = -2\hat{j} - 2\hat{k}$$

$$\overrightarrow{CB} = \overrightarrow{B} - \overrightarrow{C} = (3\hat{i} + \hat{j} + 5\hat{k}) - (3\hat{j} + 3\hat{k}) = 3\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + 2\hat{k}$$

Next, we calculate the dot product of $$\overrightarrow{CA}$$ and $$\overrightarrow{CB}$$:

$$\overrightarrow{CA} \cdot \overrightarrow{CB} = (-2\hat{j} - 2\hat{k}) \cdot (3\hat{i} - 2\hat{j} + 2\hat{k})$$

Since $$\hat{i} \cdot \hat{j} = \hat{i} \cdot \hat{k} = \hat{j} \cdot \hat{k} = 0$$, the above expression simplifies to:

$$\overrightarrow{CA} \cdot \overrightarrow{CB} = (-2 \cdot (-2) + (-2) \cdot 2) = 4 - 4 = 0$$

Since the dot product of the two vectors is zero, the angle between them is 90 degrees or $$\frac{\pi}{2}$$ radians.

Thus, the angle at vertex C is:

Option D: $$\frac{\pi}{2}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: $$\underline {Forthrightness} $$ in speech may not always be a desirable quality.
questions.solution:
Option A : Outspokenness

The word "forthrightness" in the given sentence means honesty, directness, or openness in speech. Option A, "outspokenness," is the word that best expresses the meaning of "forthrightness." It refers to the quality of being frank, honest, and straightforward in expressing one's opinions or ideas.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The $$\underline {inexorable} $$ demands of the workers brought the company to a closure.
questions.solution:
Option B : Relentless

The word "inexorable" in the given sentence means unstoppable, unyielding, or relentless. It indicates that the demands of the workers were persistent and could not be stopped or altered. Option B, "relentless," is the word that best expresses the meaning of "inexorable" in this context. It refers to something that is continuous, persistent, and unyielding in its nature or progress.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question:
The complex form of the sentence given below would be

Spare the rod and spoil the child.
options: The child is spoiled if the rod is spared.
The child becomes spoiled when the rod is spared.
The child is spoiled whenever the rod is spared.
The child is spoiled when the rod is spared.
questions.solution:
Option D : The child is spoiled when the rod is spared.

The given sentence, "Spare the rod and spoil the child," is an idiom that implies that if discipline or punishment is not applied (spare the rod), the child will become undisciplined or spoiled.

Option D, "The child is spoiled when the rod is spared," accurately captures the meaning of the idiom by indicating that when the rod (disciplinary action) is spared, the child becomes spoiled.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The attack on the freedom of the press is a $$retrograde$$ step.
questions.solution:
Option A : progressive

The word "retrograde" refers to moving backward or deteriorating to a previous, usually worse or less developed state. The term "progressive," on the other hand, suggests moving forward or advancing, often towards a better or more developed state. So, "progressive" is the term that is most nearly opposite in meaning to "retrograde."

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: The leader might have had some $$covert$$ reason for the change of his political affiliations.
questions.solution:
The word "covert" is an adjective that suggests something is not openly acknowledged or displayed—in other words, something that is hidden or secret. When looking for a word that is closest to the opposite in meaning, we are seeking a term that implies openness or something being out in the open.

Here are the explanations of the given options:

  • Unjustifiable: This word means not able to be shown to be right or reasonable. It deals with the aspect of being defensible or not, rather than with visibility or secrecy.
  • Obvious: This word implies something that is easily perceived or understood; clear, self-evident, or apparent. It is synonymous with open or evident—therefore, it is a good candidate for a word that has an opposite meaning to "covert".
  • Inexplicable: This word describes something that cannot be explained or understood. While it may suggest a lack of understanding, it does not necessarily relate to the concept of being hidden or visible like "covert" does.
  • Flimsy: This word means something that is not strong or solid; lightly built or slight. It generally refers to the structural quality of an object or argument rather than its visibility or secrecy.

Given these definitions, the word closest in meaning to the opposite of "covert" is:

Option B: Obvious—because "obvious" directly contrasts with the idea of being hidden or secret, thereby providing an antonym for "covert".

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: Regard for other as a principle of action or selflessly.
questions.solution:
Option D : Altruism

The given sentence describes "regard for others as a principle of action or selflessly." The word that can be substituted for this is "altruism." Altruism refers to the principle or practice of concern for the well-being of others, often displayed through selfless actions or behavior. It involves acting in the best interest of others without expecting anything in return.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: Code of diplomatic etiquette and precedence is
questions.solution:
Option C : Protocol

The given phrase, "code of diplomatic etiquette and precedence," refers to the rules and procedures governing behavior and conduct in diplomatic circles. The term that can be substituted for this phrase is "protocol." Protocol is the set of established rules and customs that govern diplomatic and official interactions, including etiquette, precedence, and proper behavior. Therefore, option C, "protocol," is the most suitable substitution for the given word/sentence.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: In a young's double slit arrangement fringes are produced using light of wavelength 4000 $$\mathop A\limits^o $$. One slit is covered by a thin plate of glass of refractive index 1.4 and the other with another glass plate of same thickness but of refractive index 1.7. By doing so the central bright shifts to original sixth fringe from centre. Thickness of glass plate is ................. .
questions.solution:
As, shift $$\Delta x = {\beta \over \lambda }(\mu - 1)t$$

Shift due to one plate $$\Delta {x_1} = {\beta \over \lambda }({\mu _1} - 1)t$$

Shift due to another path $$\Delta {x_2} = {\beta \over \lambda }({\mu _2} - 1)t$$

Net shift $$\Delta x = \Delta {x_2} - \Delta {x_1} = {\beta \over \lambda }({\mu _2} - {\mu _1})t$$ ..... (i)

Also, it is given that $$\Delta$$x = 6$$\beta$$

Hence, $$6\beta = {\beta \over \lambda }({\mu _1} - {\mu _2})t$$

$$ \Rightarrow t = {{6\lambda } \over {({\mu _1} - {\mu _2})}} = {{6 \times 4000} \over {(1.7 - 1.4)}} - 80000\,\mathop A\limits^o $$

$$ \Rightarrow t = 8 \times {10^{ - 6}} = 8\mu $$m

questions.title: JEE Advanced 2022 Paper 2 Online
questions.question:
A double slit setup is shown in the figure. One of the slits is in medium 2 of refractive index $n_{2}$. The other slit is at the interface of this medium with another medium 1 of refractive index $n_{1}\left(\neq n_{2}\right)$. The line joining the slits is perpendicular to the interface and the distance between the slits is $d$. The slit widths are much smaller than $d$. A monochromatic parallel beam of light is incident on the slits from medium 1. A detector is placed in medium 2 at a large distance from the slits, and at an angle $\theta$ from the line joining them, so that $\theta$ equals the angle of refraction of the beam. Consider two approximately parallel rays from the slits received by the detector.

Which of the following statement(s) is(are) correct?
options: The phase difference between the two rays is independent of $d$.
The two rays interfere constructively at the detector.
The phase difference between the two rays depends on $n_{1}$ but is independent of $n_{2}$.
The phase difference between the two rays vanishes only for certain values of $d$ and the angle of incidence of the beam, with $\theta$ being the corresponding angle of refraction.
questions.solution:
$ \tan \theta =\frac{\mathrm{AB}}{d}$

And, $ \mathrm{AB} =d \tan \theta $ ........ (i)

$ \mathrm{BC} =\mathrm{AB} \sin \alpha $

$ =d \tan \theta \sin \alpha $

$ \mathrm{AD} =\mathrm{AB} \sin \theta $

Path difference in vacuum

$$ \begin{aligned} \Delta x & =n_1 \mathrm{BC}-n_2 \mathrm{AD} \\\\ \Delta x & =n_1(\mathrm{AB}) \sin \alpha-n_2(\mathrm{AB} \sin \theta) \end{aligned} $$

Hence, $\mathrm{AB}\left(n_1 \sin \alpha-n_2 \sin \theta\right)=0$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: In a resonance coloum first and second resonance are obtained at depths 24 cm and 78 cm the third resonance will be obtained at depth.
questions.solution:
Given, first resonance column at depth, $${l_1} = 24$$ cm

Second resonance column at depth, $${l_2} = 78$$ cm

Third resonance column at depth, $${l_3} = ?$$

As

$${l_1} + x = {\lambda \over 4} = 24$$ ..... (i)

$${l_2} + x = {{3\lambda } \over 4} = 78$$ ........ (ii)

$${l_3} + x = {{5\lambda } \over 4}$$ ........ (iii)

From Eqs. (i) and (ii),

$$x = {{{l_2} - 3{l_1}} \over 2} = {{78 - 3(24)} \over 2} = 3$$ ....... (iv)

From Eqs. (iii) and (i), $${{{l_3} + x} \over {{l_1} + x}} = 5$$

$${l_3} = 5{l_1} + 5x - x = 5{l_1} + 4x$$

Substituting $$l_1$$ and x value, we get

$${l_3} = 5(24) + 4(3) \Rightarrow {l_3} = 132$$ cm.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question:
A submarine A travelling at 17 m/s is being chased along the line of its velocity by another submarine B travelling at 34 m/s. B sends a sonar signal of 600 Hz to detect A and receives a reflected sound of frequency $$\nu$$. The of $$\nu$$ is

[Speed of sound in water = 1500 ms$$-$$1]
questions.solution:
Given, velocity of submarine A, vA = 17 m/s

Velocity of submarine B, vB = 34 m/s

Signal sent by submarine B is detected by submarine A can be shown as

Frequency of signal, f0 = 600 Hz

Speed of sound in water vs = 1500 ms$$-$$1

Frequency received by submarine A is

$${f_1} = \left( {{{{v_s} - {v_A}} \over {{v_s} - {v_B}}}} \right){f_0} = \left[ {{{1500 - 17} \over {1500 - 34}}} \right] \times 600$$ Hz

$${f_1} = {{1483} \over {1466}} \times 600$$ ..... (i)

Frequency received by submarine B is

$${f_2} = \left( {{{{v_s} + {v_B}} \over {{v_s} + {v_A}}}} \right){f_1}$$

Substituting given values and f1 value from Eq. (i), we get

$${f_2} = \left( {{{1500 + 34} \over {1500 + 17}}} \right) \times \left[ {{{1483} \over {1466}} \times 600} \right]$$

$${f_2} = 1.0112 \times 1.0115 \times 600$$

$${f_2} = 613.7$$ Hz.

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: Transverse waves of the same frequency are generated in two steel wires A and B. The diameter of A is twice that of B and the tension in A is half that in B. The ratio of the velocities of the waves in A and B is
questions.solution:
The velocity of transverse waves is given by $$v = \sqrt {{T \over m}} $$ where, T = Tension and m = mass per unit length of the wire. If r is the radius of the wire and $$\rho$$ its density then,

$$m = \pi {r^2}\rho $$

$$\therefore$$ $$v = \sqrt {{T \over m}} = \sqrt {{T \over {\pi {r^2}\rho }}} $$

$$\therefore$$ $${v_A} = {{\sqrt {{T_A}} } \over {{r_A}\sqrt {\pi \rho } }}$$ and

$${v_B} = {{\sqrt {{T_B}} } \over {{r_B}\sqrt {\pi \rho } }}$$

Now, $${{{v_A}} \over {{v_B}}} = \sqrt {{{{T_A}} \over {{T_B}}}} .\,{{{r_B}} \over {{r_A}}}$$

$$\because$$ $${r_A} = 2{r_B}$$ and $${T_A} = {1 \over 2}{T_B}$$

Hence, $${{{v_A}} \over {{v_B}}} = {1 \over {2\sqrt 2 }}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: In the diagram shown below, both the strings AB and CD are made of same material and have same cross-section. The pulleys are light and fictionless. If the speed of wave in string AB is v1 and in CD is v2, then $${{{v_1}} \over {{v_2}}}$$ is
questions.solution:
Let the speed of wave in string be v and tension in string be T.

$$v \propto \sqrt T $$

$${{{v_{AB}}} \over {{v_{CD}}}} = \sqrt {{T \over {2T}}} = {1 \over {\sqrt 2 }}$$

questions.title: BITSAT 2022
questions.question: A machine gun fires 300 bullets per min if the mass of each bullet is 10 g and the velocity of the bullets is 600 ms$$-$$1, the power (in kW) of the gun is
questions.solution:
work done by the gun = total kinetic energy of the bullets

$$ = n{1 \over 2}m{v^2} = 300 \times {1 \over 2} \times 10 \times {10^{ - 3}} \times {(600)^2}$$

$$ = 150 \times 10 \times {10^{ - 3}} \times 600 \times 600$$

Power of gun $$ = {{work\,done} \over {Time\,taken}}$$

$$ = {{150 \times 10 \times {{10}^{ - 3}} \times 600 \times 600} \over {60\,s}}$$

= 9 kW ($$\because$$ 1 minute = 60 seconds)
